column2: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ॥ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ ਜੀ ਕੀ ਫ਼ਤੇ॥ ਤਸ਼ਨੀਫ਼ਿ ਨੰਦਲਾਲ, ਦੀਵਾਨਿ ਗੋਯਾ॥ ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲਾਂ॥ ਗਜ਼ਲ ੧॥ ਹਵਾਇ ਬੰਦਗੀ, ਆਵੁਰਦ ਦਰ ਵਜੂਦ ਮਰਾ॥
column5: The attraction of meditation and religious devotion brought me into this world,

column2: ਵਗਰਨਹ ਜ਼ੋਕ਼ਿ ਚੁਨੀਂ, ਆਮਦਨ ਨਬੂਦ ਮਰਾ॥੧॥
column5: Otherwise, I had no urge to come. (1) (1)

column2: ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਅਸਤ ਉ਼ਮਰ, ਕਿਹ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਬੁਗਜ਼ਰਦ॥
column5: Only that part of my life is useful and happy that is spent in remembering the Akaalpurakh

column2: ਵਰਨਹ ਚਿ ਹ਼ਾਸਿਲ ਅਸਤ, ਅਜ਼ੀਂ ਗੁੰਬਜ਼ਿ ਕਬੂਦ ਮਰਾ॥੨॥
column5: Otherwise what is the benefit to me from this blue sky or the world. (1) (2)

column2: ਦਰ ਆਂ ਜ਼ਮਾਂ, ਕਿ ਨਿਆਈ ਯਾਦ ਮੇ-ਮੀਰਮ॥
column5: For any moment that you are out of my remembrance, I feel that I am dying,

column2: ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਤੋ, ਜ਼ੀਂ ਜ਼ੀਸਤਨ, ਚਿਹ ਸੂਦ ਮਰਾ॥੩॥
column5: What is the purpose of my life (it became worthless) without your reminiscence.(1) (3)

column2: ਫ਼ਿਦਾਸਤ, ਜਾਨੋ ਦਿਲਿ ਮਨ, ਬ-ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਮਕ਼ੱਦਮਿ ਪਾਕ॥
column5: I can sacrifice my heart and soul uninhibitedly for (the dust of the feet of) this holy person

column2: ਹਰ ਆਂ ਕਸੇ, ਕਿਹ ਬ-ਸੂਇ ਤੋ, ਰਹਿ ਨਮੂਦ ਮਰਾ॥੪॥
column5: Who has shown me the way to You, the Akaalpurakh. (1) (4)

column2: ਨਬੂਦ ਹੇਚ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਂਹਾ, ਜ਼ਿ ਆਸਮਾਨੋ ਜ਼ਮੀਂ॥
column5: There was no sign-posts on the Pilgrim's way through earth or sky at that time,

column2: ਕਿ ਸ਼ੌਕ਼ਿ ਰੂਇ ਤੋ, ਆਵੁਰਦ ਦਰ ਵਜੂਦ ਮਰਾ॥੫॥
column5: When my longing for Your glimpse made me prostrate in Your honor. (1) (5)

column2: ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਤੋ, ਗੋਯਾ; ਨ ਮੇ-ਤਵਾਨਮ ਜ਼ੀਸਤ॥
column5: O Goya! I cannot live without Your remembrance, If pining for you ceases, then an end to life is the only thing coveted;

column2: ਬਸੂਇ ਦੋਸਤ, ਰਹਾਈ ਦਿਹੰਦ, ਜ਼ੂਦ ਮਰਾ॥੬॥੧॥
column5: I will be free then to move in the direction of my Beloved. (1) (6)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨॥ ਦੀਨੋ ਦੁਨੀਆ, ਦਰ ਕਮੰਦਿ, ਆਂ ਪਰੀ ਰੁਖ਼ਸਾਰਿ ਮਾ॥
column5: Both the religion and the actions of the world are in the grip of my beloved, handsome and fairy-faced friend.

column2: ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਕ਼ੀਮਤਿ, ਯੱਕ ਤਾਰਿ ਮੂਇ ਯਾਰਿ ਮਾ॥੧॥
column5: Both the worlds, this and the next, are (not) equal to or match the price (weight) of just one hair of my friend-mentor, Guru Gobind Singh Ji. (2) (1)

column2: ਮਾ ਨ ਮੇ-ਆਰੇਮ, ਤਾਬਿ ਗ਼ਮਜ਼ਾਇ ਮਿਜ਼ਗਾਨਿ ਊ॥
column5: I don't have the strength to bear the intensity of even one of his skewed looks,

column2: ਯੱਕ ਨਿਗਾਹਿ ਜਾਂ-ਫਿਜ਼ਾਇਸ਼, ਬਸ ਬਵਦ ਦਰਕਾਰਿ ਮਾ॥੨॥
column5: Just one of his (blessed) looks, that prolongs life instantly, is enough for me. (2) (2)

column2: ਗਾਹੇ ਸੂਫ਼ੀ ਗਾਹੇ ਜ਼ਾਹਿਦ, ਗਹਿ ਕ਼ਲੰਦਰ ਮੇ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥
column5: Sometimes, he acts like a mystic, sometimes like a meditator, and other times like a carefree recluse;

column2: ਰੰਗਹਾਇ ਮੁਖ਼ਤਲਿਫ਼, ਦਾਰਦ ਬੁੱਤਿ ਅੱਯਾਰਿ ਮਾ॥੩॥
column5: He is our pilot steering our way; he operates in numerous different postures. (2) (3)

column2: ਕ਼ਦਰਿ ਲਾਅ਼ਲਿ ਊ, ਬਜੁਜ਼ ਆਸ਼ਿਕ਼, ਨਾ ਦਾਨਦ ਹੇਚਕਸ॥
column5: Who other than his true devotee-lover can evaluate his pearl-like lips?

column2: ਕ਼ੀਮਤਿ ਯਾਕ਼ੂਤ ਦਾਨਦ, ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਗੌਹਰਬਾਰਿ ਮਾ॥੪॥
column5: The value of this gem is only appreciated by a diamond-rearing-vision. (2) (4)

column2: ਹਰ ਨਫ਼ਸ, ਗੋਯਾ ਬ-ਯਾਦਿ, ਨਰਗਿਸਿ ਮਖ਼ਮੂਰਿ ਊ॥
column5: During every moment of Goyaa's life, my alert heart and soul are relishing

column2: ਬਾਦਾਹਾਇ ਸ਼ੌਕ਼ ਮੇ-ਨੋਸ਼ਦ, ਦਿਲਿ ਹੁਸ਼ਿਆਰਿ ਮਾ॥੫॥੨॥
column5: the memory of intoxication of his Narcissus-like-imbued eyes. (2) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੩॥ ਬਿਦਿਹ ਸਾਕ਼ੀ, ਮਰਾ ਯੱਕ ਜਾਮ, ਜ਼ਾਂ ਰੰਗੀਨੀਇ ਦਿਲਹਾ॥
column5: O bartender! Kindly spare a heart-warming-shot out of your enjoyable intoxication for me also,

column2: ਬਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਪਾਕ-ਬੀਂ, ਆਸਾਂ ਕੁਨਮ, ਈਂ ਜੁਮਲਹ ਮੁਸ਼ਕਿਲਹਾ॥੧॥
column5: so that with my eyes of recognizing the God-sent prophet, I can solve all my riddles. (3) (1)

column2: ਮਰਾ ਦਰ ਮੰਜ਼ਿਲਿ ਜਾਨਾਂ, ਹਮਹ ਐ਼ਸ਼ੋ ਹਮਹ ਸ਼ਾਦੀ॥
column5: When moving towards the destination, my Beloved, I am ever in blissful elation.

column2: ਜਰਸ ਬੇਹੂਦਹ ਮੇ-ਨਾਲਦ, ਕੁਜਾ ਬੰਦੇਮ ਮਹ਼ਮਿਲਹਾ॥੨॥
column5: The bell in the neck of a female camel is peeling for nothing. It cannot stop me from proceeding to my goal. (3) (2)

column2: ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹ਼ਾਜ਼ਿਰ ਬਵਦ ਦਾਇਮ, ਬਿਬੀਂ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਪਾਕਸ਼ ਰਾ॥
column5: The Akaalpurakh is Omnipresent. Our strive to seek his glimpses should be spontaneous

column2: ਨਾ ਗਿਰਦਾਬੇ ਦਰੋ ਹਾਇਲ, ਨਾ ਦਰਿਆਓ, ਨਾ ਸਾਹਿਲਹਾ॥੩॥
column5: Without any obstacle of the whirlpool, the torrent or the bank of the river. (3) (3)

column2: ਚਿਰਾ ਬੇਹੂਦਹ ਮੇ ਗਰਦੀ, ਬ-ਸਹਿਰਾਂ ਓ ਬ-ਦਸ਼ਤ, ਐ ਦਿਲ॥
column5: Why are we wandering (aimlessly) in jungles and wildernesses,

column2: ਚੂੰ ਆਂ ਸੁਲਤ਼ਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੂਬਾਂ, ਕਰਦਹ ਅੰਦਰ ਦੀਦਹ ਮੰਜ਼ਿਲਹਾ॥੪॥
column5: When He, The Master of Beauty, has made his abode in our own eyes? (3) (4)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ-ਪਾਕਿਸ਼ ਨੇਸਤ, ਦਰ ਹਰ ਜਾ ਕਿ ਮੇ-ਬੀਨਮ॥
column5: When I look around without the Akaalpurakh, I see only a big void;

column2: ਬੁਗੋ ਗੋਯਾ, ਕੁਜਾ ਬੁ ਗੁਜ਼ਾਰਮ, ਈਂ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਓ ਅਹਲਿਲ ਹਾ॥੫॥੩॥
column5: Then Goyaa, you tell me, with whom can I entrust this world and my family and household affairs? (3) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੪॥ ਬਿਆ ਐ ਸਾਕ਼ੀਇ ਰੰਗੀਨ, ਜ਼ਿ ਮੈ ਪੁਰ ਕੁਨ ਅੱਯਾਗ ਈਂਜਾ॥
column5: O bartender! Please fill up my glass with a colored alcohol, cup of love,

column2: ਨਸ਼ਾਇ ਲਾਅ਼ਲਿ ਮੈ-ਗੂਨਤ, ਜ਼ਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਦ ਸੁਰਾਗ਼ ਈਂਜਾ॥੧॥
column5: Because ruby-colored alcohol will give me clues towards my Master. (4) (1)

column2: ਅਨਲ-ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਲਬਿ ਮਨਸੂਰ, ਹਮਚੂੰ ਸ਼ੀਸ਼ਾ ਕੁਲਕੁਲ ਕਰਦ॥
column5: If the long-necked decanter, like the word, 'Analhaqq', coming out of Mansoor's lips, were to create the ripple sound,

column2: ਕਿਹ ਆਰਦ ਤਾਬਿ ਈਂ ਸਹਬਾ, ਕੁਜਾ ਜਾਮਿ ਦਿਮਾਗ਼ ਈਂਜਾ॥੨॥
column5: Then, who will endure the strength of such a wee-hour alcohol? And where would there be a brain-cup (to receive such a powerful drink)? (4) (2)

column2: ਜਹਾਂ ਤਾਰੀਕ ਸ਼ੁਦ; ਜਾਨਾਂ, ਬਰ ਅਫ਼ਰੋਜ਼ਈ ਕ਼ੱਦਿ ਰਾਅ਼ਨਾ॥
column5: There is darkness in the world. O beloved! Enhance your beauty by increased burnishing, and your moon-face be a candle to the way-farer.

column2: ਨੁਮਾ ਰੁਖ਼ਸਾਰਾਇ ਤਾਬਾਂ; ਕਿ ਮੇ-ਬਾਇਦ ਚਰਾਗ਼ ਈਂ ਜਾ॥੩॥
column5: And make your face conspicuously identifiable, because there is a need of a light (lamp) in this world. (4) (3)

column2: ਬਈਂ ਯਕ-ਦਮ ਕਿ ਯਾਦ ਆਈ, ਤਵਾਂ ਉ਼ਮਰੇ ਬਸਰ ਬੁਰਦਨ॥
column5: The whole life becomes worth living if we can grab a moment (ecstacy) in our Master's remembrance,

column2: ਅਗਰ ਯਕਦਮ ਕਸੇ ਯਾਬਦ, ਬਸ਼ੌਕ਼ਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਫ਼ਰਾਗ਼ ਈਂਜਾ॥੪॥
column5: Provided, one happens to have such a trice of eagerness, keenness and zest for Akaalpurakh's love. (4) (4)

column2: ਦੋ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਨ, ਕਿ ਦਰਿਯਾਇ ਅਜ਼ੀਮੁੱਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਵਦ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: Goyaa says, Both of my eyes are like a long and large stream,

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਹਰ ਅਸ਼ਕਮ, ਬਵਦ ਸ਼ਾਦਾਬੀਇ ਸਦ ਬਾਗ਼ ਬਾਗ਼, ਈਂ ਜਾ॥੫॥੪॥
column5: And every tear coming out of them can bring greenery to hundreds of smiling gardens (because of my faith). (4) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੫॥ ਰਹਿ-ਰਸਾਨਿ, ਰਾਹਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼, ਆਮਦ ਅਦਬ॥
column5: It is essential for the travellers on the path towards the Creator to have His remembrance in their hearts,

column2: ਹਮ ਬਦਿਲ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ, ਓ ਹਮ ਬਲਬ॥੧॥
column5: And additionally, should have meditation of His Naam on their lips. (5) (1)

column2: ਹਰ ਕੁਜਾ ਦੀਦੇਮ, ਅਨਵਾਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ॥
column5: At every place, I observe the radiance and manifestation of the Providence

column2: ਬਸਕਿ ਅਜ਼ ਸੁਹ਼ਬਤਿ ਬਜ਼ੁਰਗਾਂ, ਸ਼ੁਦ ਜਜ਼ਬ॥੨॥
column5: When I get absorbed in the company of noble souls (which confers on me the blessing of realization). (5) (2)

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ-ਮਾ, ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਜਮਾਲਸ਼, ਵਾ ਨ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥
column5: Our (inside) eyes cannot be really opened without Akaalpurakh's elegance,

column2: ਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ, ਜੁਮਲਹ ਖ਼ਲਕ਼ ਰਾ, ਦੀਦੇਮ ਰੱਬ॥੩॥
column5: Because we perceive His presence in the entire humanity. (5) (3)

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਕ਼ਦਮਸ਼, ਰੌਸ਼ਨੀਇ ਦਿਲ ਕੁਨਦ॥
column5: The dust of their feet (humility) can enlighten our hearts, provided,

column2: ਗਰ ਤੁਰਾ, ਬਾ-ਸਾਲਿਕ਼ਾਂ, ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਨਸਬ॥੪॥
column5: we can establish a sound relationship with those noble souls who are treading this path (of deep devotion for the Master). (5) (4)

column2: ਕੀਸਤ ਗੋਯਾ; ਕੁ ਮੁਰਾਦਿ ਦਿਲ ਨ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ॥
column5: Bhai Sahib (Goyaa) is posing the question: Who is that person

column2: ਹਰਕਸੇ ਬਾ-ਨਫ਼ਸਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ, ਕਰਦਹ ਗ਼ਜ਼ਬ॥੫॥੫॥
column5: whose inner desires have not been fulfilled after he can get over his ego? (5) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੬॥ ਦਿਲ ਅਗਰ ਦਾਨਾ ਬਵਦ, ਅੰਦਰ ਕਿਨਾਰਸ਼ ਯਾਰਹਸ੍ਤ॥
column5: If our mind and heart were intelligent, then the Beloved is in their embrace, and,

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮ ਗਰ ਬੀਨਾ ਬਵਦ, ਦਰ ਹਰ ਤ਼ਰਫ਼ ਦੀਦਾਰ ਹਸ੍ਤ॥੧॥
column5: if our eyes were able to appreciate all that they see, then, they will see glimpses and glimpses (of the Beloved) all over. (6) (1)

column2: ਹਰ ਤ਼ਰਫ਼ ਦੀਦਾਰ; ਅੱਮਾ ਦੀਦਾਇ ਬੀਨਾ ਕੁਜਾਸਤ॥
column5: There are glimpses and glimpses (of the Beloved) all over, but where is the eye to appreciate them?

column2: ਹਰ ਤ਼ਰਫ ਤ਼ੂਰ ਅਸਤ, ਹਰ ਸੂ ਸ਼ੋਅ਼ਲਾਏ ਅਨਵਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੨॥
column5: There is the Mount Sinai all over, and there are leaping flames of His glow and refulgence. (6) (2)

column2: ਸਰ ਅਗਰ ਦਾਰੀ; ਬਿਰੌ ਸਰ ਰਾ ਬਿਨਿਹ ਦਰਿ ਪਾਇ ਊ॥
column5: If you have a head on your body, you should go to Him and place it on His lotus feet;

column2: ਜਾਂ ਅਗਰ ਦਾਰੀ, ਨਿਸਾਰਸ਼ ਕੁਨ, ਅਗਰ ਦਰਕਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੩॥
column5: And, if you have life in you which you value very much, then sacrifice it for Him. (6) (3)

column2: ਦਸਤ ਅਗਰ ਦਾਰੀ; ਬਿਰੌ ਦਾਮਾਨਿ ਜਾਨਾਂ ਰਾ ਬਿਗੀਰ॥
column5: If you have a hand, then grab and hold tight the corner of His robe.

column2: ਸੂਏ ਊ ਮੇ ਰੌ, ਅਗਰ ਪਾ ਰਾ, ਸਰਿ ਰਫ਼ਤਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੪॥
column5: If your feet are anxious (or have the strength) to walk, then start walking fast towards Him. (6) (4)

column2: ਗ਼ੋਸ਼ ਗਰ ਸ਼ੁਨਵਾ ਬਵਦ, ਜੁਜ਼ ਨਾਮਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਕੈ ਬੁਸ਼ਨਵਦ॥
column5: If we have ears that have perfect hearing then they should not hear anything other than the Naam of Akaalpurakh.

column2: ਗਰ ਜ਼ਬਾਂ ਗੋਯਾ ਬਵਦ, ਦਰ ਹਰ ਸੁਖ਼ਨ ਅਸਰਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੫॥
column5: If our tongue has the power to speak, then it should utter mystic expressions. (6) (5)

column2: ਬ੍ਰਾਹਮਨਿ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਾਕ਼ਿ ਬੁੱਤ, ਜ਼ਾਹਿਦ ਫ਼ਿਦਾਇ ਖ਼ਾਨਕ਼ਾਹ॥
column5: A Brahmin is a devotee of his idol and a Muslim of his shrine;

column2: ਹਰ ਕੁ ਜਾ, ਜਾਮਿ ਮੁਹ਼ੱਬਤ, ਦੀਦਾਅਮ ਸਰਸ਼ਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੬॥
column5: I am enamored wherever I find an adorer-connoisseur of 'devotion'. (6) (6)

column2: ਬੇ-ਅਦਬ ਪਾ ਰਾ ਮਨਿਹ ਮਨਸੂਰਵਸ਼, ਦਰ ਰਾਹਿ ਇ਼ਸ਼ਕ਼॥
column5: Like Mansorr, do not step on the path of devotion with arrogance,

column2: ਰਹ-ਰਵਿ, ਈਂ ਰਾਹਿ ਰਾ, ਅੱਵਲ ਕ਼ਦਮ ਬਰ ਦਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੭॥
column5: Otherwise, it is a path where there is a crucifix at the very first step.(6) (7)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿ ਦਾਰੀ ਦਰ ਬਿਸਾਤਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ, ਨਿਸਾਰਿ ਯਾਰ ਕੁਨ॥
column5: Goya says, If your disposition is similar to mine of getting mollified by diamonds,

column2: ਗਰ ਤੁਰਾ ਮਾਨਿੰਦਿ ਗੋਯਾ, ਤ਼ੱਬਆ਼-ਇ ਗੌਹਰਬਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੮॥੬॥
column5: Even then, you should willingly sacrifice all your assets for your Beloved. (6) (8)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੭॥ ਗਦਾਇ ਕੂਇ ਤੁਰਾ, ਮੈਲਿ ਬਾਦਿਸ਼ਾਹੀ ਨੇਸਤ॥
column5: Goyaa, the beggar and mendicant in your street, has absolutely no desire for an imperial kingdom,

column2: ਹਵਾਇ ਸਲਤ਼ਨਤੋ, ਜ਼ੌਕ਼ਿ ਕਜਕੁਲਾਹੀ ਨੇਸਤ॥੧॥
column5: He does have a desire for a realm but not only for the sake of a royal tilted cap of power (which brings in ego). (7) (1)

column2: ਹਰ ਆਂ ਕਿ, ਮੁਮਲਿਕਤਿ ਦਿਲ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ, ਸੁਲਤ਼ਾਂ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥
column5: Whosoever has won over the realm of 'mind', he is considered as the all-powerful monarch,

column2: ਕਸੇ ਕਿ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ ਤੁਰਾ, ਹਮਚੁ ਓ ਸਿਪਾਹੀ ਨੇਸਤ॥੨॥
column5: And, whosoever has discovered You does not have a rival as a soldier. (7) (2)

column2: ਗਦਾਇ ਦਰਗਹਿ ਤੋ, ਬਾਦਿਸ਼ਾਹਿ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਸਰਾਂਸਤ॥
column5: (Addressing Dasam Guru) An established beggar in your street is the emperor of both the worlds,

column2: ਅਸੀਰਿ ਖ਼ਤਿ ਤੁਰਾ, ਹਾਜਤਿ ਰਹਾਈ ਨੇਸਤ॥੩॥
column5: The captive of the ecstasy of the love of your burgeoning hair is not just looking for resurrection. (7) (3)

column2: ਕੁਦਾਮ ਦੀਦਹ, ਕਿ ਦਰ ਵੈ ਸਵਾਦਿ ਨੂਰਿ ਤੂ ਨੇਸਤ॥
column5: Which is that eye that does not have the glow of Your glory?

column2: ਕੁਦਾਮ ਸੀਨਹ, ਕਿ ਊ ਮਖ਼ਜ਼ਨਿ ਇਲਾਹੀ ਨੇਸਤ॥੪॥
column5: Which is that body (chest) that doesn't have godly treasures in it? (7) (4)

column2: ਫ਼ਿਦਾਇ ਊ ਸ਼ੌ, ਵ ਉਜ਼ਰੇ ਮਖ਼੍ਵਾਹ, ਐ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: Goyaa says, Do not hesitate or make excuses but sacrifice yourself for Him,

column2: ਕਿ ਦਰ ਤ਼ਰੀਕ਼ਤਿ-ਮਾ, ਜਾਇ ਉਜ਼ਰ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹੀ ਨੇਸਤ॥੫॥੭॥
column5: Because in the vocabulary of lovers (of true devotees), there is no word like 'excuse'. (7) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੮॥ ਅਜ਼ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਚਸ਼ਮ, ਆਂ ਬੁਤਿ ਨਾਂ-ਮਿਹਰਬਾਂ ਗੁਜ਼ਸਤ॥
column5: A non-compassionate cruel beloved passed through before my eyes;

column2: ਜਾਨਾਂ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ, ਤਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਰਹੇ ਦੀਦਹ, ਜਾਂ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੧॥
column5: As the beloved went through, it was as if my life (heart and soul) passed away through my eyes. (8) (1)

column2: ਰੰਗਸ਼ ਕਬੂਦ ਕਰਦ, ਵ ਦਿਲਸ਼ ਪੁਰ ਸ਼ਰਾਰਹ ਸਾਖ਼ਤ॥
column5: The smoke from my continued sighs rose high and deep through the sky,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬਸਕਿ ਦੂਦਿ, ਆਹਿ ਮਨ, ਅਜ਼ ਆਸਮਾਂ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੨॥
column5: That they changed its very complexion into teal-blue color and charred its heart. (8) (2)

column2: ਮਾ ਰਾ ਬਾ-ਯਕ ਇਸ਼ਾਰਾਇ ਅਬਰੂ, ਸ਼ਹੀਦ ਕਰਦ॥
column5: With one pointer with his eyebrows, he simply (metaphorically) murdered (martyred) us,

column2: ਅਕਨੂੰ ਇ਼ਲਾਜ ਨੇਸਤ, ਕਿ ਤੀਰ ਅਜ਼ ਕਮਾਂ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੩॥
column5: But there is no treatment now when the arrow has been shot from the bow. (8) (3)

column2: ਯੱਕ ਦਮ ਬ-ਖ਼੍ਵੇਸ਼ ਰਾਹਿ; ਨਾ ਬੁਰਦਮ, ਕਿ ਕੀਸਤਮ॥
column5: Goyaa says, I cannot attain nor understand my reality as to who I am,

column2: ਐ ਵਾਇ, ਨਕ਼ਦਿ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਅਮ, ਰਾਇਗਾਂ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੪॥
column5: Alas ! I have simply wasted all my life's assets.? (8) (4)

column2: ਹਰਗਿਜ਼ ਬ-ਸੈਰਿ ਰੋਜ਼ਾਇ ਰਿਜ਼ਵਾਂ, ਨ ਮੇ ਰਵਦ॥
column5: Goyaa says, If someone ever happens to pass through the street of the Beloved,

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਕਸੇ ਕਿ ਜਾਨਿਬਿ, ਕੂਇ ਬੁੱਤਾਂ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੫॥੮॥
column5: He would then never go for a stroll (which will be beneath the above pleasure) even in the heavenly garden.? (8) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੯॥ ਬਦਰ, ਦਰ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਰੁਖ਼ਤ, ਸ਼ਰਮਿੰਦਹ ਅਸਤ॥
column5: The moon is abashed when compared to your (beautiful) face,

column2: ਬਲਕਿ, ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦਿ ਜਹਾਂ; ਹਮ ਬੰਦਹ ਅਸਤ॥੧॥
column5: In fact, even the world's sun is also mortified before your radiance, O Guru! its glow and light is subservient to yours. (9) (1)

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਾ, ਹਰਗਿਜ਼ ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼, ਨ ਦੀਦ॥
column5: Goyaa: My eyes have never recognized anyone other than the Akaalpurakh.

column2: ਐ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ਾ ਚਸ਼ਮੇ, ਕਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਬੀਨਿੰਦਹ ਅਸਤ॥੨॥
column5: Blessed are the eyes which can possibly see the Almighty.? (9) (2)

column2: ਮਾ ਨ ਮੇ-ਲਾਫ਼ੇਮ, ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ੁਹਦੋ ਰਿਆ॥
column5: I do not brag about my meditation or sanctimony,

column2: ਗਰ ਗੁਨਾਹਗਾਰੇਮ, ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਿੰਦਹ ਅਸਤ॥੩॥
column5: But if I am ever guilty of this sin, the Waaheguru is all-Forgiving.? (9) (3)

column2: ਦੀਗਰੇ ਰਾ, ਅਜ਼ ਕੁਜਾ, ਆਰੇਮ ਮਾ॥
column5: Where can we find another one,

column2: ਸ਼ੋਰ ਦਰ ਆ਼ਲਮ, ਯਕੇ ਅਫ਼ਗੰਦਹ ਅਸਤ॥੪॥
column5: When there is so much of noise and ado about the Only One. (9) (4)

column2: ਹ਼ਰਫ਼ਿ ਗ਼ੈਰ, ਅਜ਼ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਨਿਆਇਦ ਹੇਚਗਾਹ॥
column5: No word, other than the Naam of the Waaheguru ever comes to the lips of Goyaa,

column2: ਬਰ ਲਬਿ ਗੋਯਾ, ਕਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਿੰਦਹ ਅਸਤ॥੫॥੯॥
column5: because of His divine attribute being All-forgiving. (9) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੦॥ ਦਰਮਿਆਨਿ ਬਜ਼ਮਿ ਮਾ, ਜੁਜ਼ ਕਿੱਸਾਇ ਜਾਨਾਨਾ ਨੇਸਤ॥
column5: In (the chamber of my heart) our gathering, no sermon or discourse other than that about Akaalpurakh is delivered,

column2: ਬੇ-ਹ਼ਿਜਾਬਾ ਅੰਦਰੀਂ ਮਜਲਿਸ, ਕਿ ਕਸ ਬੇਗਾਨਾ ਨੇਸਤ॥੧॥
column5: Come in and join this congregation. There is no stranger here (in the secrecy of this rendezous). (10) (1)

column2: ਬੁ-ਗਜ਼ਰ ਅਜ਼ ਬੇਗਾਨਗੀਹਾ, ਓ ਬਖ਼ੁਦ ਸ਼ਉ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ॥
column5: Without worrying about others' personalities, try to understand your own;

column2: ਹਰ-ਕਿ ਬਾ-ਖ਼ੁਦ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ ਸ਼ੁਦ, ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਬੇਗਾਨਹ ਨੇਸਤ॥੨॥
column5: Anyone who has understood his own self is no longer stranger to the Akaalpurakh. (10) (2)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕ਼ਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਹਰ ਕਿਰਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ, ਹਮਾਂ ਸਾਹ਼ਿਬ-ਦਿਲਸਤ॥
column5: Anyone who has a longing to meet the Creator is the master of his own self.

column2: ਕਾਰਿ ਹਰ ਦਾਨਾ ਨਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ, ਕਾਰਿ ਹਰ ਦੀਵਾਨਹ ਨੇਸਤ॥੩॥
column5: This kind of determination does not belong to any intelligent person nor to any maniac. (10) (3)

column2: ਨਾਸਿਹਾ, ਤਾ ਚੰਦ ਗੋਈ, ਕਿੱਸਾਹਾਇ ਵਾਅ਼ਜ਼ੋ ਪੰਦ॥
column5: O sermonizer! How long shall you continue to deliver your sermons?

column2: ਬਜ਼ਮਿ ਮਸਤਾਂ ਅਸਤ ਜਾਇ, ਕਿੱਸਾ ਓ ਅਫ਼ਸਾਨਾ ਨੇਸਤ॥੪॥
column5: This is a group of inebriated drunks (of Waaheguru's Naam): It is not a place for telling stories and tales. (10) (4)

column2: ਈਂ ਮਤ਼ਾਇ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼, ਬ-ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਸਾਹ਼ਿਬਾਨਿ-ਦਿਲ ਬਵਦ॥
column5: This divine treasure is only with the men of heart, the masters of their minds,

column2: ਚੂੰ ਬ-ਸ਼ਹ਼ਰਾ ਮੇ ਰਵੀ, ਦਰ ਗੋਸ਼ਾਇ ਵੀਰਾਨਹ ਨੇਸਤ॥੫॥
column5: Why are you wandering in the wilderness? He does not abide in the nooks and corners of the desolate and ruined places. (10) (5)

column2: ਈਂ ਮਤ਼ਾਇ ਸ਼ੌਕ਼ਿ ਰਾ, ਅਜ਼ ਅ਼ਾਸ਼ਿਕਾਨਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਬਖ਼੍ਵਾਹ॥
column5: Ask the true devotees of Waaheguru about the treasures of fondness for Him;

column2: ਜਾਂ ਕਿ ਦਰ ਜ਼ਾਨਸ਼, ਬ-ਜੁਜ਼ ਨਕ਼ਸ਼ਿ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਜਾਨਾਨਹ, ਨੇਸਤ॥੬॥
column5: Because, there is nothing else but their concentration on His facial features all their lives. (10) (6)

column2: ਚੰਦ ਮੇ-ਗੋਈ ਤੁ ਐ ਗੋਯਾ, ਖ਼ਮੁਸ਼ ਸ਼ੌ ਜ਼ੀਂ ਸੁਖ਼ਨ॥
column5: O Goyaa! How long are you going to indulge in such discussions; it is time for you to shut up;

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕ਼ਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਮੁਨਹ਼ਸਿਰ, ਬਰ ਕਾਅ਼ਬਾ-ਓ ਬੁੱਤਖ਼ਾਨਾ ਨੇਸਤ॥੭॥੧੦॥
column5: A keenness of the desire for Waaheguru is not confined either in Kaaba or a temple. (10) (7)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੧॥ ਦਿਲ ਅਗਰ ਦਰ ਹਲਕ਼ਾਇ, ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਦੁਤਾਂ, ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥
column5: If my heart can survive (the yearning) passing through tresses of his double curled hair,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਤਨ, ਵਜ਼ ਚੀਨ ਓ ਮਾਚੀਨ ਓ ਖ਼ੁਤਾ, ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੧॥
column5: Then I will understand that it can go through sensitive countries like China without any trouble. (11) (1)

column2: ਬਾਦਿਸ਼ਾਹੀ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆ਼ਲਮ, ਯੱਕ ਨਿਗਾਹਿ ਰੂਇ ਤੂ॥
column5: Just one glimpse of your face is equivalent to the kingdom of both the worlds,

column2: ਸਾਇਆਇ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਤੋ, ਅਜ਼ ਬਾਲਿ ਹੁਮਾ, ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੨॥
column5: The shadow of your lock of hair has gone past the shadow of the wings of Phoenix, the mystical bird (which is supposed to bring good luck). (11) (2)

column2: ਈਂ ਬਿਸਾਤਿ ਉ਼ਮਰ ਰਾ ਦਰਯਾਬ, ਕੀਂ ਬਾਦਿ ਸਬਾ॥
column5: Make efforts to understand and realize the expansive territory of life,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਕੁਜਾ ਆਮਦ ਨ-ਦਾਨਮ, ਵਜ਼ ਕੁਜਾ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੩॥
column5: Because I have no idea of the direction of the morning (youths) breeze from where it came or where it was headed to. (11) (3)

column2: ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹੀਇ ਈਂ ਜਹਾਂ, ਜੁਜ਼ ਸ਼ੋਰੋ ਗ਼ੌਗਾ ਬੇਸ਼ ਨੇਸਤ॥
column5: In the eyes of that ascetic who has absolutely no personal axe to grind,

column2: ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਦਰਵੇਸ਼ੇ ਕਿ ਊ, ਅਜ਼ ਮੁੱਦਆ਼ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੪॥
column5: The kingdom of this world is nothing else but a confusing noise. (11) (4)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤਨ-ਹਾ, ਚਿਹ ਮੇ ਪੁਰਸੀ, ਦਰੀਂ ਦਹਰਿ ਖ਼ਰਾਬ॥
column5: What kind of questions you want to ask to go through this desolate country (the world),

column2: ਬਾਦਿਸ਼ਾਹ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤੋ, ਹਮ ਗਦਾ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੫॥
column5: The kings have gone through it and so have the ascetics as well. (11) (5)

column2: ਸ਼ਿਅਰਿ ਗੋਯਾ, ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ-ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ ਅਸਤ, ਚੂੰ ਆਬਿ-ਹਯਾਤ॥
column5: The couplets of Goyaa are capable to grant life like the divine nectar,

column2: ਬਲਕਿ ਦਰ ਪਾਕੀਜ਼ਗੀ, ਜ਼ਿ ਆਬਿ ਬਕ਼ਾ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੬॥੧੧॥
column5: In fact, they are more effective in chastity than even the elixir of eternal life. (11) (6)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੨॥ ਇਮ-ਸ਼ਬ ਬ-ਤਮਾਸ਼ਾਇ, ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਯਾਰ ਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ॥
column5: Tonight, Goyaa, the connoisseur of love, can go for a glimpse of beloved,

column2: ਸੂਇ ਬੁਤਿ ਆ਼ਸ਼ਿਕ-ਕੁਸ਼ਿ ਅ਼ੱਯਾਰ ਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ॥੧॥
column5: He can go to the annihilating murderer of the lovers. (metaphorically) (12) (1)

column2: ਦਰ ਕੂਚਾਇ ਇ਼ਸ਼ਕ਼, ਗਰ-ਚਿ ਮੁਹ਼ਾਂਲਸਤ ਰਸੀਦਨ॥
column5: Even though it is difficult to reach the passage of love and devotion,

column2: ਮਨਸੂਰ ਸਿਫ਼ਤ ਬਰ ਕ਼ਦਮਿ ਦਾਰ ਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ॥੨॥
column5: It is still possible if one is prepared to put his foot on the crucifix like Mansoor. (12) (2)

column2: ਐ ਦਿਲ, ਬਸੂਇ ਮਦਰਸਹ, ਗਰ ਮੈਲ ਨ ਦਾਰੀ॥
column5: O mind! If you do not have the motive to go the educational school or you are scared of the teacher,

column2: ਬਾਰੇ, ਬ-ਸੂਇ ਖ਼ਾਨਾਇ ਖ਼ੁੱਮਾਰ, ਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ॥੩॥
column5: You may not, but, at least, you should be able to go towards the bar. (12) (3)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਖ਼ਾਤਿਰਮ, ਅਜ਼ ਅਜ਼ਕਿ ਤੋ, ਸ਼ੁਦ ਰਸ਼ਕਿ ਗੁਲਿਸਤਾਂ॥
column5: When my heart, due to my deep affection for You, has become envy of the blossomed garden,

column2: ਬੇਹੂਦਾ ਚਿਰਾ ਜਾਨਿਬਿ ਗੁਲਜ਼ਾਰ ਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ॥੪॥
column5: Then, how can it ever even think of going to the flower beds. (12) (4)

column2: ਐ ਦਿਲ ਚੁ ਸ਼ੁਦੀ ਵਾਕਿਫ਼ਿ, ਅਸਰਾਰਿ ਇਲਾਹੀ॥
column5: O my mind! When you become familiar with the mysteries of the Lord,

column2: ਦਰ ਸੀਨਹ-ਇ, ਆਂ ਮਖ਼ਜ਼ਨਿ ਅਸਰਾਰ ਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ॥੫॥
column5: Then, only You, the repository of mysteries, can get into my body. (12) (5)

column2: ਸਦ ਰੋਜ਼ਾਇ ਰਿਜ਼ਵਾਂਨਸਤ, ਚੂ ਦਰ ਖ਼ਾਨਾ ਸ਼ੁਗੁਫ਼ਤਹ॥
column5: When hundreds of gardens are blossoming inside the house, the body,

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਬ-ਚਿਹ ਸੂਇ, ਦਰੋ ਦੀਵਾਰ, ਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ॥੬॥੧੨॥
column5: Goyaa says, then how can anyone go to any other structures? (12) (6)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੩॥ ਦੀਦੀ, ਆਖ਼ਿਰ ਤ਼ਾਲਿਬਿ ਮੌਲਾ, ਰਹਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥
column5: Bhai Sahib telling the worldly people, You have eventually seen that the seekers of Akaalpurakh adopted the only path to attain Him,

column2: ਹ਼ਾਸਿਲਿ ਉ਼ਮਰਿ ਗਿਰਾਮੀ ਰਾ, ਅਜ਼ੀਂ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੧॥
column5: Then, you have reaped full benefits from this precious life. (13) (1)

column2: ਹੇਚਕਸ ਬੇਰੂੰ ਨ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ, ਅਜ਼ ਸਵਾਦਿ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਤੋ॥
column5: No individual is outside the circumference of your ravishing locks of hair,

column2: ਈਂ ਦਿਲਿ ਦੀਵਾਨਹ-ਅਮ, ਆਖ਼ਿਰ ਹਮੀਂ ਸੌਦਾ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੨॥
column5: And, my enchanted mind is also twirled into the same mania. (13) (2)

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਆਂ ਸਰਵਿ ਰਵਾਂ, ਹਰਗਿਜ਼ ਨਿਆਇਦ ਦਰ ਨਜ਼ਰ॥
column5: Since the time his beautiful tall and hefty torso has penetrated into my eyes,

column2: ਤਾਂ ਕ਼ੱਦਿ ਰਾਅ਼ਨਾਇ ਓ, ਦਰ ਦੀਦਾਇ-ਮਾ, ਜਾ-ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੩॥
column5: I have not been able to recognize any one else except his living-cypress-like graceful personality. (13) (3)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਨਿਦਾਏ ਨਾਕ਼ਾਇ ਲੈਲਾ, ਦਿਲ ਸ਼ੋਰੀਦਹ ਆਮ॥
column5: Just hearing the ringing of the bell hung in the neck of Laila's camel, my heart went crazy (because it was the sign of Laila's arrival),

column2: ਹਮਚੂੰ ਮਜਨੂੰ ਮਸਤ ਗਸ਼ਤੋ, ਰਹਿ ਸੂਇ ਸਹ਼ਰਾ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੪॥
column5: And, like Majnoo, it became ecstatic and ran towards the wilderness of the jungle. (13) (4)

column2: ਖੁਸ਼ ਨ ਮੇ-ਆਇਦ ਮਰਾ, ਹ਼ਰਫੇ ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼॥
column5: Ever since, his love-story has abided in my heart,

column2: ਤਾਂ ਹ਼ਦੀਸਿ ਇਸ਼ਕਿ ਊ, ਅੰਦਰ ਦਿਲਮ ਮਾਵਾ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੫॥
column5: I have no taste for anything other than his true remembrance in every fiber of my body. (13) (5)

column2: ਤਾਂ ਬਿਆਈ ਯਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ, ਬਹਿਰਿ ਨਿਆਜ਼ਿ ਖ਼ਿਦਮਤਤ॥
column5: My diamond-showering eyes are preserving lustrous gems similar to the delicate poppy flowers,

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਗੌਹਰ-ਬਾਰਿ ਮਾ, ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਲੂਲੂਏ ਲਾਲਾ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੬॥
column5: So that during your momentary visit, I could waive them over your precious head in sacrifice. (13) (6)

column2: ਮੇ ਬਰਾਇਦ ਜਾਨਿ ਮਨ, ਇਮ ਰੋਜ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਰਾਹਿ ਦੋ ਚਸ਼ਮ॥
column5: Today, my life is ending through both of my eyes,

column2: ਨੌਬਤਿ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਊ, ਤਾਂ ਵਾਅ਼ਦਾਦਿ ਫ਼ਰਦਹ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੭॥
column5: However, the chance of only one glimpse of him has been deferred to the doomsday.? (13) (7)

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਹ਼ਮਦਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਨਿਆਇਦ, ਬਰ ਜ਼ਬਾਨਮ ਹੇਚਗਾਹ॥
column5: Nothing else but the praises of the Lord come up on my lips,

column2: ਹ਼ਾਸਿਲਿ ਈਂ ਉ਼ਮਰ ਰਾ, ਆਖ਼ਿਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਗੋਯਾ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੮॥੧੩॥
column5: Ultimately, Goyaa's heart has reaped full benefits of this life. (13) (8)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੪॥ ਦਿਲਿ ਮਨ, ਦਰ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕ਼ਿ ਯਾਰ ਬੁਸੋਖ਼ਤ॥
column5: My heart is charred because of my separation from my beloved,

column2: ਜਾਨਿ ਮਨ, ਬਹਿਰਿ ਆਂ ਨਿਗਾਰ ਬਸੋਖ਼ਤ॥੧॥
column5: And my life and spirit are ablaze and reduced to ashes (in memory of) for my handsome master. (14) (1)

column2: ਆਂ ਚੁਨਾਂ ਸੋਖ਼ਤਮ, ਅਜ਼ਾਂ ਆਤਿਸ਼॥
column5: I was burnt in that fire so much,

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਬੁਸ਼ੁਨੀਦ, ਚੂੰ ਚਿਨਾਰ ਬੁਸੋਖ਼ਤ॥੨॥
column5: That anyone who heard about this got burnt too like a pine tree. (14) (2)

column2: ਮਨ ਨ ਤਨਹਾ ਬੁਸੋਖ਼ਤਮ, ਦਰ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥
column5: I am not the only one who was putrefied in the fire of love,

column2: ਹਮਹ ਆ਼ਲਮ, ਅਜ਼ੀਂ ਸ਼ਰਾਰ ਬੁਸੋਖ਼ਤ॥੩॥
column5: Rather, the entire world was burnt down with this spark. (14) (3)

column2: ਸੋਖ਼ਤਨ, ਦਰ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕ਼ਿ ਆਤਿਸ਼ਿ ਯਾਰ॥
column5: To get burnt in the 'flames of separation' of one's beloved,

column2: ਹਮਚੁਨੀਂ, ਕੀਮੀਆ ਬਕਾਰ ਬੁਸੋਖ਼ਤ॥੪॥
column5: Is like alchemy, substance that turns any metal into gold, getting minced (burnt) in fire into ashes. (14) (4)

column2: ਆਫ਼ਰੀਂ ਬਾਦ, ਬਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: Blessed is the heart of Goyaa

column2: ਕਿ, ਬ-ਉਮੀਦ ਰੂਇ ਯਾਰ ਬੁਸੋਖ਼ਤ॥੫॥੧੪॥
column5: That it got reduced to ashes only in the expectation of a glimpse of the face of his beloved. (14) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੫॥ ਅਜ਼ ਦੋ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਸਤ ਸ਼ੁਅਲਾ; ਅਲਗ਼ਿਆਸ॥
column5: Would some one please shield me from (the glare) of his imbued ravishing eyes,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਲਬੋ ਦਹਨਿ ਸ਼ੱਕਰਖ਼ਾ, ਅਲ-ਗ਼ਿਆਸ॥੧॥
column5: And, protect me from his sugar cubes-chewing mouth and lips. (15) (1)

column2: ਵਾਏ ਬਰ ਨਫ਼ਸੇ, ਕਿ ਬੇਹੂਦਹ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥
column5: I regret that moment that passed purposelessly,

column2: ਅਲਗ਼ਿਆਸ ਅਜ਼ ਗ਼ਫ਼ਲਤਿ ਮਾ, ਅਲਗ਼ਿਆਸ॥੨॥
column5: I also regret my carelessness, and my negligence to let the opportunity to slip by. (15) (2)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਨਿਜ਼ਾਇ਼ ਕੁਫ਼ਰੋ ਦੀਂ, ਦਿਲ ਬਰਹਮ ਅਸਤ॥
column5: My heart and soul are frustrated and grieved at the spat on account of blasphemy and religion

column2: ਬਰ ਦਰਿ ਦਰਗਾਹਿ ਮੌਲਾ, ਅਲਗ਼ਿਆਸ॥੩॥
column5: I would look for anyone who would save me at the door of the abode of Akaalpurakh.(Would someone save me when I have brought a plea at the door of the Creator.)' (15) (3)

column2: ਲੋਲੀਆਨਿ ਸੋਖ਼, ਆ਼ਲਮ ਦਿਲੇ ਰਬੂਦ॥
column5: Playful, sportive and arrogant so called lovers have looted and conned the world.

column2: ਮੇ ਕੁਨਮ ਅਜ਼ ਦਸਤਿ ਆਂਹਾ, ਅਲਗ਼ਿਆਸ॥੪॥
column5: I am crying for mercy that I, too, have been exploited by them and are begging so that someone would please save me. (15) (4)

column2: ਕੈ; ਜ਼ਿ ਦਸਤਿ ਖ਼ੰਜਰਿ ਮਿਜ਼ਗ਼ਾਨਿ ਊ॥
column5: How can Goyaa keep quiet from the dagger-like eyelids of the Master Guru;

column2: ਮੇ-ਸ਼ਵਦ ਖ਼ਾਮੋਸ਼ ਗੋਯਾ, ਅਲਗ਼ਿਆਸ॥੫॥੧੫॥
column5: I am still yelling for help. Someone would kindly save me. (15) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੬॥ ਮਸਤ ਰਾ, ਬਾ-ਜਾਮਿ ਰੰਗੀਂ, ਇਹਤਿਆਜ॥
column5: Just as a drunk is only looking for and is concerned with a wine glass with ruby-colored drink (wine or alcohol),

column2: ਤਿਸ਼ਨਹ ਰਾ, ਬ-ਆਬਿ ਸ਼ੀਰੀਂ, ਇਹਤਿਆਜ॥੧॥
column5: Similarly, a thirsty person needs a glass of cold sweet water to quench his thirst, a glass of wine is not relevant. (16) (1)

column2: ਸੁਹ਼ਬਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ-ਹ਼ੱਕ਼, ਬਸ ਅਨਵਰੱਸਤ॥
column5: The company of the devotees of Akaalpurakh is saturated with radiance;

column2: ਤਾਲਿਬਾਂ ਰਾ, ਹਸਤ ਚੰਦੀਂ, ਇਹਤਿਆਜ॥੨॥
column5: This is all the association that the seekers of Waaheguru really need. (16) (2)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਤਬੱਸੁਮ ਕਰਦਾਈ, ਗੁਲਸ਼ਨ ਜਹਾਂ॥
column5: One can make this world a beautiful garden with one's smile,

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਦੀਦਸ਼, ਕੈ ਬ-ਗੁਲਚੀਂ, ਇਹਤਿਆਜ॥੩॥
column5: Why would one need a gardener, after he had a glimpse of heavenly Him?(16) (3)

column2: ਯਕ ਨਿਗਾਹਿ ਲੁਤ਼ਫ਼ਿ ਤੋ ਦਿਲ ਮੇ-ਬੁਰਦ॥
column5: One of your loving and affectionate glances is enough to take away my breath,

column2: ਬਾਜ਼ ਮੇ-ਦਾਰਮ, ਅਜ਼ਾਂ ਈਂ, ਇਹਤਿਆਜ॥੪॥
column5: But, still, I plead before Him for mercy and this is what I need the most. (16) (4)

column2: ਨੇਸਤ ਗੋਯਾ ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਤੋ, ਦਰ ਦੋ ਜਹਾਂ॥
column5: Goyaa is addressing the Guru: I have no one else but you in both the worlds.

column2: ਬਾ ਤੋ ਦਾਰਮ, ਅਜ਼ ਦਿਲੋ ਦੀਂ, ਇਹਤਿਆਜ॥੫॥੧੬॥
column5: In fact, I need my heart and soul (faith) (want to live) only for you.(16) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੭॥ ਐ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਅੰਬਰੀਨਿ ਤੋ, ਗੋਯਾ ਨਿਕ਼ਾਬਿ ਸੁਬਹ਼॥
column5: Your firmament like lock of hair is just like a camouflage for the morning,

column2: ਪਿਨਹਾਂ ਚੁ ਜ਼ੇਰਿ ਅਬਰਿ ਸਿਆਹ, ਆਫ਼ਤਾਬਿ ਸੁਬਹ਼॥੧॥
column5: Like a morning sun having been eclipsed under the black clouds. (17) (1)

column2: ਬੇਰੂੰ ਬਰਾਇਦ ਆਂ ਮਹਿ ਮਨ, ਚੂੰ ਜ਼ਿ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਬਿ ਸੁਬਹ਼॥
column5: When my moon, while waking up from his day-sleep, came out,

column2: ਸਦ ਤ਼ਾਅ਼ਨਾ ਮੇ-ਜ਼ਨਦ, ਬ-ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਆਫ਼ਤਾਬਿ ਸੁਬਹ਼॥੨॥
column5: It was as if he was imprecating the posture of the morning sun. (17) (2)

column2: ਬਾ-ਦੀਦਹ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਬਨਾਕ, ਚੋ ਬੇਰੂੰ ਬਰ-ਆਮਦੀ॥
column5: When you, the Guru, emerged with your sleepy eyes from your Bed of love,

column2: ਸ਼ਰਮਿੰਦਾ ਗਸ਼ਤ ਅਜ਼ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਤੋ, ਆਫ਼ਤਾਬਿ ਸੁਬਹ਼॥੩॥
column5: The morning sun was chagrined and abashed to compare itself with the radiance of your face. (17) (3)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਮਕ਼ੱਦਮਿ ਸ਼ਰੀਫ਼, ਜਹਾਂ-ਰਾ ਦਿਹਦ ਫ਼ਰੋਗ਼॥
column5: When the slumbering morning sun takes off his veil from its face,

column2: ਚੋ ਬਰ-ਕਸ਼ਦ ਨਿਕ਼ਾਬ, ਜ਼ਿ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਆਫ਼ਤਾਬਿ ਸੁਬਹ਼॥੪॥
column5: With its fortunate arrival, it brings (day)light to the whole world. (17) (4)

column2: ਬੇਦਾਰੀ ਅਸਤ, ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗਾਇ ਸਾਹ਼ਿਬਾਨਿ ਸ਼ੌਕ਼॥
column5: The whole life of worldly people is an unending night-long vigil and is an ever lasting amnesia,

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਹ਼ਰਾਮ ਕਰਦਮ, ਅਜ਼ ਆਇੰਦਹ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਬਿ ਸੁਬਹ਼॥੫॥੧੭॥
column5: Goyaa says, However, for me, sleeping during the wee hours of the morning will be forbidden, sinful and morally wrong from now on. (17) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੮॥ ਮੇ-ਬੁਰਦ, ਦੀਨੋ ਦਿਲਮ ਰਾ, ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਸ਼ੋਖ਼॥
column5: This playful eye attracts and takes away my breath, heart and faith,

column2: ਮੇ-ਕਸ਼ਦ, ਅਜ਼ ਚਾਹਿ ਗ਼ਮ ਹਾ, ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਸ਼ੋਖ਼॥੧॥
column5: And it is the same lively eye that brings me out of my worries and sorrowful times. (18) (1)

column2: ਕਾਕੁਲਿ ਊ ਫ਼ਿਤਨਹ ਜ਼ਨ, ਈਂ ਦਰ ਅ਼ਾਲਮ ਅਸਤ॥
column5: One of his locks of hair can create a calamity and disaster in the world,

column2: ਰੌਨਕਿ ਅਫ਼ਜ਼ਾਇ ਜਹਾਂ, ਈਂ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਸ਼ੋਖ਼॥੨॥
column5: And just one of his eyes can bless the whole world with prosperity. (18) (2)

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਪਾਇ, ਰੌਸ਼ਨੀ-ਇ ਦਿਲ ਬਵਦ॥
column5: Goyaa is praying, Wish my heart becomes the dust of the feet of my beloved (Guru) - humility,

column2: ਹ਼ਾਦੀਏ ਰਾਹਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਈਂ, ਚਸ਼ਮੇ ਸ਼ੋਖ਼॥੩॥
column5: And my sportive eye steers my way towards the Akaalpurakh. (18) (3)

column2: ਕੈ ਕੁਨਦ, ਸੂਇ ਗੁਲੋ ਨਰਗਿਸ, ਨਿਗਾਹ॥
column5: Anyone who has tasted the savor of that mirthful eye (of the Guru),

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਦੀਦਹ, ਲੱਜ਼ਤਿ ਆਂ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਸ਼ੋਖ਼॥੪॥
column5: Will never care to look at the Narcissus flower again. (18) (4)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿਰਾ ਗੋਯਾ, ਗ਼ੁਬਾਰਿ ਦਿਲ ਬਸ਼ੁਸਤ॥
column5: Goyaa says, Anyone who has had a glimpse of that sprightly eye (of the Guru) even once,

column2: ਯਕ ਨਿਗਾਹੇ ਆਂ ਕਿ, ਦੀਦਹ ਆਂ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਸ਼ੋਖ਼॥੫॥੧੮॥
column5: would have all his suspicions and illusions completely dispelled.? (18) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੧੯॥ ਬਹੋਸ਼ ਬਾਸ਼, ਕਿ ਹੰਗਾਮਿ ਨੌਬਹਾਰ ਆਮਦ॥
column5: Come back to your senses and rejoice! It is the time for the onset of the new spring season,

column2: ਬਹਾਰ ਆਮਦੋ, ਯਾਰ ਆਮਦੋ, ਕ਼ਰਾਰ ਆਮਦ॥੧॥
column5: The spring is here, my beloved, the Guru, has arrived, and now my heart is serene and at peace.? (19) (1)

column2: ਦਰੂਨਿ ਮਰਦੁਮਕਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਮ, ਜ਼ਿ ਬਸਕਿ ਜਲਵਾਗਰੱਸਤ॥
column5: My (Guru's) splendor and refulgence have penetrated so much in the pupil of my eye,

column2: ਬਹਰ ਤ਼ਰਫ਼ ਕਿ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਕੁਨਮ, ਰੂਇ ਯਾਰ ਆਮਦ॥੨॥
column5: That wherever and in whichever direction, it may look, it sees only the loving face of my beloved, the Guru. (19) (2)

column2: ਬ-ਹਰ ਤ਼ਰਫ਼ ਕਿ ਰਵਦ, ਦੀਦਹ ਮੇ ਰਵਮ, ਚਿ ਕੁਨਮ॥
column5: I am the slave of my eyes; I turn in the direction in which my eyes direct me and are gazing. What can I do?

column2: ਦਰੀਂ ਮੁਕ਼ਦਮਹ ਮਾਰਾ, ਚਿਹ ਇਖ਼ਤਿਆਰ ਆਮਦ॥੩॥
column5: In fact, what control do we have in this path of love? (19) (3)

column2: ਖ਼ਬਰ ਦਿਹੰਦ ਬ-ਯਾਰਾਨਿ ਮੁਦੱਈ, ਕਿ ਇਮਸ਼ਬ॥
column5: Some claimant friends got the news from some one that Manssor,

column2: ਅਨਲਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਜ਼ਦਹ ਮਨਸੂਰ, ਸੂਇ ਦਾਰ ਆਮਦ॥੪॥
column5: yelling that he was the Lord, was seen proceeding towards the scaffold tonight. (19) (4)

column2: ਖ਼ਬਰ ਦਿਹੰਦ ਬ-ਗੁਲਹਾ, ਕਿ ਬਸ਼ੁਗੁਫ਼ਤੰਦ ਹਮਹ॥
column5: Inform all the flowers to begin blossoming, because

column2: ਅਜ਼ੀਂ ਨਵੇਦ, ਕਿ ਆਂ ਬੁਲਬੁਲਿ ਹਜ਼ਾਰ ਆਮਦ॥੫॥
column5: This good news came from the thousand singing nightingales. (19) (5)

column2: ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਬ-ਮਾਂਦ, ਜ਼ਿ ਗ਼ੈਰਤ ਜੁਦਾ, ਵ ਮਨ ਹੈਰਾਂ॥
column5: Goyaa says, The tongue has become dumb out of shame and the heart is distraught on its own instance;

column2: ਹ਼ਦੀਸਿ ਸ਼ੌਕ਼ਿ-ਤੋ, ਅਜ਼ ਬਸ-ਕਿ, ਬੇ-ਸ਼ੁਮਾਰ ਆਮਦ॥੬॥
column5: Who can complete the story of my unlimited fondness for You, the Guru? (19) (6)

column2: ਖ਼ਿਆਲਿ ਹਲਕ਼ਾਇ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਤੋ, ਮੇ-ਕੁਨਦ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: Goyaa says, I keep entangling myself on the curls of the lock of your hair, O Guru!

column2: ਅਜ਼ੀਂ ਸਬੱਬ, ਕਿ ਦਿਲ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕ਼, ਬੇ-ਕ਼ਰਾਰ ਆਮਦ॥੭॥੧੯॥
column5: Because, my mind that keeps yearning with intense desire to see you is able to attain peace and stability.? (19) (7)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੦॥ ਤ਼ਬੀਬ ਆ਼ਸ਼ਿਕਿ ਬੇਦਰਦ ਰਾ, ਦਵਾ ਚਿ ਕੁਨਦ॥
column5: What can a doctor prescribe as a medication for a patient suffering from a keen love?

column2: ਤੁਰਾ ਕਿ ਪਾਏ ਬਵਦ ਲੰਗ, ਰਹਿਨੁਮਾ ਚਿ ਕੁਨਦ॥੧॥
column5: How can a pilot, the Guru, help to show the right path when we, ourselves have a crippled condition with lame legs? (20) (1)

column2: ਜਮਾਲਿ ਓ ਹਮਹ ਜਾ, ਬੇ-ਹ਼ਿਜਾਬ ਜਲਵਾਗਰ ਅਸਤ॥
column5: All his (Guru's) glow and grace are visible without a camouflage,

column2: ਕਿ ਤੁ ਦਰ ਹ਼ਿਜਾਬਿ ਖ਼ੁਦੀ, ਯਾਰਿ ਮਹਿ-ਲਿਕ਼ਾ ਚਿ ਕੁਨਦ॥੨॥
column5: When we are under the garb of ego, what can even a moon-like serene face do for us? (20) (2)

column2: ਤੁਰਾ ਕਿ, ਨੇਸਤ ਬਯਕ ਗੂਨਹ, ਖ਼ਾਤ਼ਿਰਿ ਮਜਮੂਅ਼॥
column5: One who does not have any momentary direction or stability in his mind,

column2: ਮੁਕ਼ਾਮਿ ਅਮਨੋ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼, ਗੋਸ਼ਾਇ ਫ਼ਜ਼ਾ, ਚਿ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩॥
column5: What can a placid spot or a quiet nook of a mansion do for him? (20) (3)

column2: ਬਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਬਦਰਕ਼ਾਇ ਇਸ਼ਕ, ਕੈ ਰਸੀ ਬਾ ਦੋਸਤ॥
column5: How can you reach the court of the Beloved without a preceptor of love?

column2: ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਜਜ਼ਬਾਇ ਸ਼ੋਕ਼ਿ ਤੋ, ਰਹਿਨੁਮਾ ਚਿ ਕੁਨਦ॥੪॥
column5: What can a guide do to help if you lack the desire and the emotion?? (20) (4)

column2: ਚੁ ਸੁਰਮਾਇ ਦੀਦਹ ਕੁਨੀ, ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਿਦ, ਐ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: O Goyaa ! As long as you are able to use the sacred dust of the feet of the Guru as a collyrium for your eyes,

column2: ਜਮਾਲਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਨਿਗਰੀ, ਬਾ ਤੋ ਤੂਤੀਆ ਚਿ ਕੁਨਦ॥੫॥੨੦॥
column5: You would be able to see the grace and refulgence of the Creator. What other use is the collyrium for you? (20) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੧॥ ਸਬਾ, ਚੂੰ ਹਲਕ਼ਾਹਾਇ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਊ ਰਾ, ਸ਼ਾਨਹ ਮੇ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ॥
column5: When the easterly breeze combs through the curls of his tresses,

column2: ਅਜਬ ਜ਼ੰਜੀਰ, ਅਜ਼ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਦਿਲਿ ਦੀਵਾਨਹ, ਮੇ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ॥੧॥
column5: It is as if it is making a strange chain link for my insane mind. (21) (1)

column2: ਨ ਦਾਨਿਸਤਮ, ਅਜ਼ ਰੋਜ਼ਿ ਅਜ਼ਲ, ਈਂ ਨਕ਼ਸ਼ਿ ਆਦਮ ਰਾ॥
column5: We have not understood the significance of the human body since the dawn of creation, beginning of the time,

column2: ਕਿ ਨੱਕ਼ਾਸ਼, ਅਜ਼ ਬਰਾਏ ਮਾਂਦਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ, ਖ਼ਾਨਾ ਮੇ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ॥੨॥
column5: That, the Lord created this body for His own abode. (21) (2)

column2: ਦਿਲਿ ਆ਼ਸ਼ਿਕ, ਬ-ਅੰਦਕ ਫ਼ੁਰਸਤਿ, ਮਾਸ਼ੂਕ਼ ਮੇ ਗਰਦਦ॥
column5: The heart of a lover becomes that of the beloved's in a short time;

column2: ਸਰਾ ਪਾ ਜਾਂ ਸ਼ਵਦ, ਹਰ ਕਸ ਕਿ, ਬਾ ਜਾਨਾਨਹ ਮੇ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ॥੩॥
column5: Anyone who is on good terms with the beloved becomes the heart and soul from feet to the head (all over his body). (21) (3)

column2: ਮੁਗੋ ਅਜ਼ ਹ਼ਾਲਿ ਲੈਲਾ, ਬਾ-ਦਿਲਿ ਸ਼ੋਰੀਦਾਇ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: Why are you running after (every) mean person for a morsel of bread?

column2: ਕਿ ਸ਼ਰਹੇ ਕਿੱਸਾਇ ਮਜਨੂੰ, ਮਰਾ ਦੀਵਾਨਹ ਮੇ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ॥੪॥
column5: You know well that the greed for just one grain makes one a prisoner. (21) (4)

column2: ਬਰਾਏ ਗਿਰਦਾ-ਇ ਨਾਂ, ਗਿਰਦਿ ਹਰ ਦੂਨਾ, ਚਿ ਮੇ ਗਰਦੀ॥
column5: O Goyaa! Do not narrate the circumstances of Lailaa to any deranged mind,

column2: ਤ਼ਮਅ਼ ਦੀਦੀ, ਕਿ ਆਮਦ ਰਾ, ਅਸੀਰਿ ਦਾਨਹ ਮੇ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ॥੫॥੨੧॥
column5: Because, I become insane just after listening to the tale of Majnoo. It is just apt for an insane (for Guru's love) like me. (21) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੨॥ ਹਿਜ਼ਦਹ ਹਜ਼ਾਰ ਸਿਜਦਹ, ਬ-ਸੂਇ ਤੋ ਮੇ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥
column5: Addressing the Guru: People prostrate eighteen thousand times with their faces towards You

column2: ਹਰਦਮ ਤ਼ਵਾਫ਼ਿ, ਕਾਅ਼ਬਾ-ਇ ਕੂਇ ਤੋ, ਮੇ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੧॥
column5: And they circumambulate all the times the street of Your Kaabaa, the sacred place. (22) (1)

column2: ਹਰ ਜਾ ਕਿ ਬਿਨਿਗਰੰਦ, ਜਮਾਲਿ ਤੋ ਬਿਨਿਗਰੰਦ॥
column5: Wherever they see, they see your (the Guru's) elegance and radiance,

column2: ਸਾਹ਼ਿਬਿ ਦਿਲਾਂ, ਨਜ਼ਾਰਾਇ ਰੂਇ ਤੋ, ਮੇ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੨॥
column5: O Knower of the innermost feelings of their hearts! They see a glimpse of your face. (22) (2)

column2: ਜਾਂ ਰਾ ਨਿਸਾਰਿ, ਕ਼ਾਮਤਿ ਰਾਅ਼ਨਾ ਤੁ, ਕਰਦਹ-ਅੰਦ॥
column5: They, the people, have sacrificed their lives for your handsome personality and grand stature,

column2: ਦਿਲ-ਹਾਇ ਮੁਰਦਾ, ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਬੂਇ ਤੋ, ਮੇ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੩॥
column5: And, with your redolence, they can revive the courage in (morally and physically) dead minds. (22) (3)

column2: ਆਈਨਾਇ, ਖ਼ੁਦਾਏ-ਨੁਮਾ ਹਸਤ, ਰੂਇ ਤੋ॥
column5: O Guru! Your face is the mirror through which they can get Lord's glimpse,

column2: ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼, ਜ਼ਿ-ਸ਼ੀਸ਼ਾ-ਇ ਰੂਇ ਤੋ ਮੇ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੪॥
column5: And, they do get His glimpse through the mirror of your face. Even the Garden of heaven is envious of this. (22) (4)

column2: ਤੀਰਾ-ਦਿਲਾਂ, ਕਿ ਚਸ਼ਮ ਨ ਦਾਰੰਦ, ਮੁਤ਼ਲਕ਼ਨ ਅੰਦ॥
column5: The corrupt-minded-peolple who do not have the proper vision,

column2: ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦ ਰਾ, ਮੁਕ਼ਾਬਿਲਿ ਰੂਇ ਤੋ, ਮੇ-ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੫॥
column5: take the liberty of putting the sun in front of your elegant face. (22) (5)

column2: ਮਸਤਾਨਿ ਸ਼ੌਕ਼ ਗ਼ੁਲਗ਼ੁਲਹ ਦਾਰੰਦ, ਦਰ ਜਹਾਂ॥
column5: In their exuberance for their love for your affection, they sacrifice thousands of the worlds.

column2: ਸਦ ਜਾਂ ਫ਼ਿਦਾਇ, ਯੱਕ ਸਰਿ ਮੂਇ ਤੋ, ਮੇ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੬॥
column5: In fact, they sacrifice hundreds of lives just for one lock of your hair. (22) (6)

column2: ਦਰ ਪਰਦਾਇ ਜਮਾਲਿ ਤੋ, ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਸ਼ੁਦਹ ਜਹਾਂ॥
column5: When people talk of the notoriety and fame of your face,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਰ ਤ਼ਰਫ਼, ਕਿ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਰੂਏ ਤੋ, ਮੇ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੭॥
column5: Then, in the garb of your refulgence, the whole world gets illuminated and fragrance floats around. (22) (7)

column2: ਆਸ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਾਗਾਨਿ ਸ਼ੌਕ਼ਿ ਤੋ, ਗੋਯਾ ਸਿਫ਼ਤ ਮੁਦਾਮ॥
column5: Like Goyaa, those with wounds of your love and captivated by your devotion,

column2: ਆਵਾਜ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼-ਕਲਾਮ, ਜ਼ਿ ਬੂਇ ਤੋ, ਮੇ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੮॥੨੨॥
column5: Always tune up their voices into melodies with your fragrance. (22) (8)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੩॥ ਐ ਗਰਦਸ਼ਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਤੋ, ਕਿ ਅੱਯਾਮ ਨ ਦਾਰਦ॥
column5: O Guru, my good friend! Radiance of your eyes cannot be matched by the light of the day.

column2: ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦਿ ਫ਼ਲਕ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਰੁਖ਼ਤ, ਨਾਮ ਨ ਦਾਰਦ॥੧॥
column5: Even the sun in the sky is no match to the refulgence of your face. (23) (1)

column2: ਸੱਯਾਦਿ ਕ਼ਜ਼ਾ, ਅਜ਼ ਪਏ ਦਿਲ ਬੁਰਦਨਿ ਆ਼ਸ਼ਿਕ਼॥
column5: To capture the heart of the beloved hunter of the death,

column2: ਜੁਜ਼ ਹਲਕਾਇ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਤੋ, ਦਿਗਰ ਦਾਮ ਨ ਦਾਰਦ॥੨॥
column5: There is no better snare like the noose of your ravishing lock of hair. (23) (2)

column2: ਈਂ ਉ਼ਮਰਿ ਗਿਰਾਂ ਮਾਇਆ, ਗ਼ਨੀਮਤ ਸ਼ੁਮਰ ਆਖ਼ਿਰ॥
column5: This priceless life that we have been bestowed with should be considered as blessed,

column2: ਮਾ ਸੁਬਹ ਨ ਦੀਦੇਮ, ਕਿ ਊ ਸ਼ਾਮ ਨ-ਦਾਰਦ॥੩॥
column5: Because, we have not yet seen a morning (youth) that did not have an evening (old age). (23) (3)

column2: ਤਾ ਚੰਦ ਦਿਲਾਸਾ ਕੁਨਮ, ਈਂ ਖ਼ਾਤਿਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਰਾ॥
column5: O Guru, heart of hearts! How long can I keep consoling my mind?

column2: ਬੇ-ਦੀਦਨਿ ਰੂਇ ਤੋ, ਦਿਲ ਆਰਾਮ ਨ ਦਾਰਦ॥੪॥
column5: The fact is that I do not get any succor or comfort without getting a glimpse of your handsome face. (23) (4)

column2: ਈਂ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਗੌਹਰ-ਬਾਰ, ਕਿ ਦਰਿਯਾ ਸ਼ੁਦਹ ਗੋਇਆ॥
column5: The gem-showering eye, O Goyaa, has become deep as an ocean,

column2: ਬੇ-ਰੂਇ ਦਿਲ ਆਰਾਏ ਤੋ, ਆਰਾਮ ਨ-ਦਾਰਦ॥੫॥੨੩॥
column5: The mind is not consoled without your comforting glimpse. (23) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੪॥ ਤਾ ਲਾਅ਼ਲਿ ਜਾਂ ਫ਼ਿਜ਼ਾਇ ਤੋ, ਗੋਯਾ ਨ ਮੇ-ਸ਼ਵਦ॥
column5: O Guru! Until your life-extending red lips do not utter some expression,

column2: ਦਰਮਾਨਿ ਦਰਦਿ ਮਾਸਤ, ਕਿ ਪੈਦਾ ਨ ਮੇ-ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੧॥
column5: The cure for our pains and agonies cannot be produced. (24) (1)

column2: ਲਬਿ-ਤਿਸ਼ਨਹ ਰਾ, ਜ਼ਿ-ਆਬਿ ਲਬਤ, ਹਸਤ ਆਰਜ਼ੂ॥
column5: My thirsty lips are yearning for the elixir coming out of your lips,

column2: ਤਸਕੀਨਿ ਮਾ, ਜ਼ਿ ਖ਼ਿਜ਼ਰੋ ਮਸੀਹ਼ਾ, ਨ ਮੇ-ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੨॥
column5: I cannot be satisfied either by eternity-bestowing Khizar or dead-reviving Maseehaa. (24) (2)

column2: ਦਾਰੇਮ, ਦਰਦਿ ਦਿਲ, ਕਿ ਮਰਊਰਾ, ਇ਼ਲਾਜ ਨੇਸਤ॥
column5: I am stricken with such a heart ailment that doesn't have any cure.

column2: ਤਾ ਜਾਂ ਨ ਮੇ ਦਿਹੇਮ, ਮੁਦਾਵਾ, ਨ ਮੇ-ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੩॥
column5: I will be not be cured and be comfortable until I surrender my life. (24) (3)

column2: ਗੁਫ਼ਤਮ, ਕਿ ਜਾਂ-ਦਿਹਮ, ਇਵਜ਼ਿ ਯੱਕ ਨਿਗਾਹਿ ਤੋ॥
column5: I said, I can give my life for just a single glimpse of yours.

column2: ਗੁਫ਼ਤਾ ਮਿਆਨਿ ਮਾ ਓ ਤੋ, ਸੌਦਾ ਨ ਮੇ-ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੪॥
column5: He responded, We cannot have a deal between us on these terms. (24) (4)

column2: ਅੰਦਰ ਹਵਾਇ, ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਗਿਰਹ-ਗੀਰ ਮਹਿਵਸ਼ਾਂ॥
column5: My mind is twisted under the yearning for the locks of hair of the moon-like beautiful and serene personality,

column2: ਮਨ ਮੇ-ਰਵਮ, ਗਿਰਹ ਜ਼ਿ ਦਿਲਮ, ਵਾ ਨ-ਮੇਸ਼ਵਦ॥੫॥
column5: I realize this. However, no one except you, the Guru, can unravel these knots. (24) (5)

column2: ਬਾ ਸਾਹ਼ਿਲਿ ਮੁਰਾਦ, ਕੁਜਾ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ ਸ਼ਵੇਮ॥
column5: Until our eyes become an ocean (full of tears) in your remembrance,

column2: ਤਾ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਾ, ਬ-ਯਾਦਿ ਤੋ, ਦਰਿਯਾ ਨ ਮੇ-ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੬॥
column5: We cannot be aware of the intended sense of much smaller limits of even a river-bank. (24) (6)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ, ਦਰ ਇੰਤਜ਼ਾਰਿ ਤੋ, ਚਸ਼ਮਮ ਸਫ਼ੈਦ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥
column5: Goyaa says, My eyes have become pale and blind waiting for your arrival,in expectation of seeing you,

column2: ਮਨ ਚਿ ਕੁਨਮ, ਕਿ ਬੇ ਤੋ, ਦਿਲਾਸਾ ਨ ਮੇ-ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੭॥੨੪॥
column5: What can I do? No one else can give me any solace and console me. (24) (7)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੫॥ ਚੂੰ, ਮਾਹਿ ਦੋ ਹਫ਼ਤਹ, ਰੂ ਨੁਮਾਈ, ਚਿ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥
column5: What would happen if you would show your full-moon-like gracious face?

column2: ਇਮਸ਼ਬ ਮਹਿ-ਮਨ, ਅਗਰ ਬਿਆਈ, ਚਿ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੧॥
column5: O my beloved! There would be just no cause of any damage if you would kindly show your face tonight. (25) (1)

column2: ਈਂ ਜੁਮਲਹ ਜਹਾਂ, ਅਸੀਰਿ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਤ ਗਸ਼ਤਹ॥
column5: The whole world is enchanted and captivated with just one lock of your hair.

column2: ਯੱਕ ਲਹ਼ਜ਼ਹ, ਅਗਰ ਗਿਰਹ ਕੁਸ਼ਾਈ, ਚਿ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੨॥
column5: What would you lose and what would be the harm if you would open this mystery (knot) just for one moment? (25) (2)

column2: ਆ਼ਲਮ ਹਮਹ ਗਸ਼ਤਹ ਅਸਤ, ਬੇ ਤੋ ਤਾਰੀਕ਼॥
column5: The entire world fell into darkness without you,

column2: ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦ ਸਿਫ਼ਤ, ਅਗਰ ਬਿਰਾਈ, ਚਿ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੩॥
column5: What would you lose if you were to come out like the sun? (25) (3)

column2: ਯੱਕ ਲਹ਼ਜ਼ਹ ਬਿਆ, ਓ ਦਰ ਦਰ ਚਸ਼ਮਮ, ਬਿ-ਨਸ਼ੀਂ॥
column5: Goyaa says: Kindly come even for a moment and make my eyes your abode.

column2: ਦਰ ਦੀਦਹ ਨਿਸ਼ਸਤਹ, ਦਿਲ-ਰੁਬਾਈ, ਚਿ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੪॥
column5: O you, the enticer of my heart! What would be the harm if you were to abide in my eyes just for a short time? (25) (4)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਿੰਦੂਏ, ਖ਼ਾਲਤ ਕਿ ਬਰੂਅ਼ਤ, ਸ਼ੈਦਾ ਅਸਤ॥
column5: What would be the harm if you could sell your black moled-torso to me,

column2: ਬਿ-ਫ਼ਰੋਸ਼ੀ, ਅਗਰ ਬ ਨਕ਼ਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਈ, ਚਿ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੫॥
column5: who is crazy for your divine face in exchange for the whole world? (25) (5)

column2: ਦਰ ਦੀਦਹ ਤੂਈ, ਓ ਮਨ ਬਹਰ ਕੂ ਜੋਯਾਂ॥
column5: You are the light of my eyes and are abiding in them. Then whom am I looking for?

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਪਰਦਾਇ ਗ਼ੈਬ, ਰੂ-ਨਮਾਈ ਚਿ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੬॥
column5: What would be the harm if you could emerge out of the invisible veil and show me your beautiful face? (25) (6)

column2: ਗੋਯਾਸਤ, ਬ ਹਰ ਤ਼ਰਫ਼ ਸੁਰਾਗ਼ਤ ਜੋਯਾਂ॥
column5: Goyaa says, I am lost on your trail and am trying to look for you (the Guru) in every nook and corner,

column2: ਗਰ ਗ਼ੁੰਮ-ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਰਾ, ਰਹਿਨੁਮਾਈ ਚਿ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੭॥
column5: What would you lose if you were to direct this strayed and lost person towards the right path. (25) (7)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੬॥ ਕ਼ਦਮ ਆਂ ਬਿਹ, ਕਿ ਓ ਰਾਹਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ, ਪੈਮੂਦਹ ਮੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥
column5: A footstep taken towards the path of Truth is a worthwhile one,

column2: ਜ਼ਬਾਨੇ ਬਿਹ, ਕਿ ਦਰ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ, ਆਸੂਦਹ ਮੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੧॥
column5: And the tongue that invokes and savors the meditation of His Naam is blessed. (26) (1)

column2: ਬਹਰ ਸੂਇ, ਕਿ ਮੇ-ਬੀਨਮ ਬ ਚਸ਼ਮਮ, ਮਾਸਵਾ ਨਾਇਦ॥
column5: Whenever and wherever I look, nothing penetrates into my eyes,

column2: ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾ ਨਕ਼ਸ਼ਿ ਊ, ਦਰ ਦੀਦਾਇ ਮਾ, ਬੂਦਹ ਮੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੨॥
column5: In fact, it is His features and impressions which pervade and are imprinted into my eyes all the times. (26) (2)

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਫ਼ੈਜ਼ਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਿਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ, ਮਰਾ ਮਾਅ਼ਲੂਮ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਆਖ਼ਿਰ॥
column5: It is the blessing of a complete and true Guru that made me aware (of this reality),

column2: ਕਿ ਦਾਇਮ ਮਰਦੁਮਿ ਦੁਨਿਯਾ, ਬ-ਗ਼ਮ-ਆਲੂਦਹ ਮੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੩॥
column5: That the worldly people are inseparable from sorrows and worries. (26) (3)

column2: ਜ਼ਹੇ, ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਦਿਲਿ, ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਜ਼ਮੀਰਿ, ਆ਼ਰਿਫ਼ਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ॥
column5: How fortunate is the person with a radiant heart and soul that is thoroughly and fully enlightened,

column2: ਕਿਹ ਬਰ ਦਰਗਾਹਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਪੇਸ਼ਾਨੀਇ ਊ, ਸੂਦਹ ਮੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੪॥
column5: And whose forehead constantly bows in the court of Waaheguru. (26) (4)

column2: ਬ-ਕੁਰਬਾਨੀ ਸਰਿ ਕੂਇਸ਼ ਬ-ਗਿਰਦੋ ਦਮ ਮਜ਼ਨ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: O Goyaa! Keep circling around his territory expecting to offer a sacrifice without bragging about it,

column2: ਇਸ਼ਾਰਤਹਾਇ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਊ, ਮਰਾ ਫ਼ਰਮੂਦਹ ਮੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੫॥੨੬॥
column5: I am just waiting for a simple signal and pointer of his eyes. (26) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੭॥ ਹਜ਼ਾਰ ਤਖ਼ਤਿ ਮੁਰੱਸਾ, ਫ਼ਿਤਾਦਹ ਦਰ ਰਾਹ ਅੰਦ॥
column5: There are thousands of studded peacock thrones littered in your way,

column2: ਕ਼ਲੰਦਰਾਨਿ ਤੋ, ਤਾਜੋ ਨਗ਼ੀਂ ਨ ਮੇ-ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹੰਦ॥੧॥
column5: But your devout followers stupefied by your grace have absolutely no desire for any crowns or gems. (27) (1)

column2: ਫ਼ਨਾਹ ਪਜ਼ੀਰ ਬਵਦ, ਹਰ ਚਿ ਹਸਤ ਦਰ ਆ਼ਲਮ॥
column5: Everything in this world is destructible and ceases to exist (ultimately),

column2: ਨ ਆ਼ਸ਼ਿਕਾਂ, ਕਿ ਅਜ਼ ਅਸਰਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ਼, ਆਗਾਹ ਅੰਦ॥੨॥
column5: But the lovers never get destroyed because they know the secrets of love. (27) (2)

column2: ਤਮਾਮ ਚਸ਼ਮ ਤਵਾਂ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਪਏ ਨਜ਼ਾਰਾਇ ਊ॥
column5: All eyes were anxiously intent for a glimpse of the Guru,

column2: ਹਜ਼ਾਰ ਸੀਨਹ ਜ਼ਿ ਸ਼ੌਦਾਏ ਹਿਜਰ, ਮੇ ਕਾਹੰਦ॥੩॥
column5: And thousands of minds are getting submerged (like in quick sand) in their anxiety of separation (from the Guru). (27) (3)

column2: ਤਮਾਮ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਦੁਨਿਯਾ, ਬ-ਯੱਕ ਨਿਗਾਹ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ੰਦ॥
column5: Believe me! That even his mendicant is the emperor of emperors,

column2: ਯਕੀਂ ਬਿਦਾਂ, ਕਿ ਗਦਾਯਾਨਿ ਕੂਇ ਓ ਸ਼ਾਹੰਦ॥੪॥
column5: Because, he could endow the wealth of the world to anyone with just one of his glances. (27) (4)

column2: ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾਂ ਸੁਹ਼ਬਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼, ਤ਼ਲਬ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: O Goyaa! Always look for the company of the devotees of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਕਿ ਤ਼ਾਲਿਬਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ, ਵਾਸਿਲਾਨਿ ਅ਼ੱਲਾਹ ਅੰਦ॥੫॥੨੭॥
column5: Because His seekers are always connected with Him. (27) (5)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੮॥ ਗਰ ਦਸਤਿ ਮਨ, ਹਮੇਸ਼ਹ ਪਏ ਕਾਰ, ਮੇ ਰਵਦ॥
column5: Although my hands and feet are occupied in my mundane activities,

column2: ਮਨ ਚਿ ਕੁਨਮ, ਕਿ ਦਿਲ ਬਸੂਇ ਯਾਰ, ਮੇ ਰਵਦ॥੧॥
column5: But what can I do, (because I am helpless) my mind is constantly thinking of my Beloved. (28) (1)

column2: ਆਵਾਜ਼ਿ ਲਨਤਰਾਨੀ, ਬ-ਹਰਦਮ ਬਗੋਸ਼ਿ ਦਿਲ॥
column5: Although the voice of 'One cannot see' keeps echoing in our ears all the times,

column2: ਮੂਸਾ ਮਗਰ ਬਦੀਦਨਿ ਦੀਦਾਰ, ਮੇ-ਰਵਦ॥੨॥
column5: But still Moses kept on going to get a glimpse of the Lord. (28) (2)

column2: ਈਂ ਦੀਦਹ ਨੇਸਤ, ਆਂ ਕਿ ਅਜ਼ੋ ਅਸ਼ਕ ਮੇ-ਚਿਕਦ॥
column5: This is not the eye that will let out any tears,

column2: ਜਾਮਿ ਮੁਹ਼ੱਬਤਅਸਤ, ਕਿ ਸਰਸ਼ਾਹ ਮੇ ਰਵਦ॥੩॥
column5: In fact, the cup of love and devotion is always full to the brim. (28) (3)

column2: ਦਿਲਦਾਰ ਓ ਦਿਲ, ਜ਼ਿ ਬਸਕਿ ਯਕੇ ਅੰਦ ਦਰ ਵਜੂਦ॥
column5: The heart and the beloved are so much intertwined with each other,

column2: ਜਾਂ ਦਿਲ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਹ, ਜਾਨਿਬਿ ਦਿਲਦਾਰ ਮੇ ਰਵਦ॥੪॥
column5: That this is the reason that it always keeps running towards (seeking) the latter. (28) (4)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਕੌਨ, ਗਰਦਨਿ ਊ, ਸਰ-ਬੁਲੰਦ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥
column5: Any one who rushes towards the crucifix like Mansoor

column2: ਮਨਸੂਰ ਵਾਰ, ਹਰ ਕਿ ਸੂਇ ਦਾਰ, ਮੇ ਰਵਦ॥੫॥
column5: Will have his neck and head high with pride in both the worlds. (29) (5)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ ਦੋਸਤ, ਹਕ਼ੀਕ਼ੀ ਹਯਾਤ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ॥
column5: Goyaa says, I have found the real life in the remembrance of my beloved,

column2: ਦੀਗਰ, ਚਿਰਾ ਬਕੂਚਾਇ ਖ਼ੁੰਮਾਰ, ਮੇ ਰਵਦ॥੬॥੨੮॥
column5: Why should I now have any reason to visit the tavern or the pub? (29) (6)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੨੯॥ ਕੀਸਤ ਇਮਰੋਜ਼, ਕਿ ਸੌਦਾਇ ਨਿਗਾਰੇ ਦਾਰਦ॥
column5: Is there someone today who is madly in love for a glimpse of his beloved?

column2: ਬਾਦਿਸ਼ਾਹ ਹਸਤ, ਦਰੀਂ ਦਹਿਰ, ਕਿ ਯਾਰੇ ਦਾਰਦ॥੧॥
column5: Anyone who has a true friend (beloved) in this world is a king. (29) (1)

column2: ਦਾਨਮ, ਐ ਸ਼ੋਖ਼, ਕਿ ਖ਼ੂਨਿ ਦੋ ਜਹਾਂ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਰੇਖ਼ਤ॥
column5: O lively lover ! I know that you will be involved in making the two worlds bleed,

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਸਤਿ ਤੋ, ਇਮਰੋਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਮਾਰੇ ਦਾਰਦ॥੨॥
column5: Because your intoxicated and charming eye is full of alcoholic drink today (metaphorically). (29) (2)

column2: ਦਾਮਨਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਰਾ, ਖ਼ੂਨਿ ਜਿਗਰ, ਰੰਗੀਂ ਕਰਦ॥
column5: The blood from my heart has reddened my eyelids (like a wounded lover),

column2: ਦਿਲਿ ਦੀਵਾਨਾਇ ਮਾ, ਤੁਰਫ਼ਹ ਬਹਾਰੇ ਦਾਰਦ॥੩॥
column5: Showing that a strange spring has sprung up in my mad heart due to intense love. (29) (3)

column2: ਸਾਯਾਇ ਤੂਬਾ-ਏ-ਫ਼ਿਰਦੌਸ, ਨ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹਦ ਹਰਗਿਜ਼॥
column5: Any one, like Manssor, who has achieved even a shadow of the scaffold or crucifix,

column2: ਹਰਕਿ ਮਨਸੂਰ ਸਿਫ਼ਤ, ਸਾਯਾਇ ਦਾਰੇ ਦਾਰਦ॥੪॥
column5: Would never have any desire either for the heavens or for the shade of the heavenly tree. (29) (4)

column2: ਰੂਇ ਗੁਲਗੂਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ, ਐ ਸ਼ਮਾਅ਼ ਬਰ ਅਫ਼ਰੋਜ਼ ਦਮੇ॥
column5: O flame of the lamp! Keep your rose-like red-flowered face lit for a little while,

column2: ਦਿਲਿ ਪਰਵਾਨਾ ਓ ਬੁਲਬੁਲ, ਬ-ਤੋ ਕਾਰੇ ਦਾਰਦ॥੫॥
column5: Because the moth and the nightingale have some business with you. (29) (5)

column2: ਬ-ਹਰ ਦੀਵਾਨਹ, ਅਗਰ ਸਿਲਸਿਲਾ-ਹਾ, ਮੇ ਸਾਜ਼ੰਦ॥
column5: Goyaa says, Although chains are made to strangle every madly-in-love person,

column2: ਦਿਲਿ ਗੋਯਾ ਬ-ਖ਼ਮਿ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼, ਕ਼ਰਾਰੇ ਦਾਰਦ॥੬॥੨੯॥
column5: Yet my heart is being choked in the noose of (Guru's) lock of hair. (29) (6)

column2: ਗ਼ਜ਼ਲ ੩੦॥ ਕਸੇ ਬਹਾਲਿ ਗ਼ਰੀਬਾਨਿ ਬੇ-ਨਵਾ ਨ-ਰਸਦ॥
column5: No one listens nor cares for the plight of the poor travelers.

column2: ਰਸੀਦਹਏਮ ਬਜਾਇ, ਕਿ ਬਾਦਿਸ਼ਾਹ ਨ ਰਸਦ॥੧॥
column5: However, I have attained a stage where even the kings failed to reach.? (30) (1)

column2: ਹਜ਼ਾਰ ਖ਼ੁਲਦਿ ਬਰੀਂ ਰਾ, ਬ-ਨੀਮ ਜੌ, ਨ-ਖ਼ਰੰਦ॥
column5: (The true devotees) will not purchase even thousands of lofty heavens for just a grain or two of barley,

column2: ਅਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ ਹੇਚ ਬਿਦਾਂ, ਕੂਇ ਦਿਲਰੁਬਾ ਨ ਰਸਦ॥੨॥
column5: Because none of these heavens are capable to lead me to the abode of my Beloved. (30) (2)

column2: ਤ਼ਬੀਬਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ਼, ਚੁਨੀਂ ਗੁਫ਼ਤਾ ਅਸਤ, ਮੇ-ਗੋਯੰਦ॥
column5: It is said, according to the doctor of love, that no one, except the Waaheguru, knows the pain and suffering of separation.

column2: ਬਹ਼ਾਲਿ ਦਰਦਿ ਗ਼ਰੀਬਾਂ, ਬਜੁਜ਼ ਖੁਦਾ, ਨ ਰਸਦ॥੩॥
column5: No one listens to the sad stories of the destitute (has a cure to their pains and sorrows). (30) (3)

column2: ਬਰਾਇ ਰੌਸ਼ਨੀਇ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਦਿਲ, ਅਗਰ ਖ਼੍ਵਾਹੀ॥
column5: If you want to see the light for your eyes of your heart, then understand,

column2: ਬਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਦਰਗਾਹਿ ਓ, ਹੇਚ ਤੂਤੀਆ ਨ ਰਸਦ॥੪॥
column5: That no better collyrium than the dust of the foyer of the Beloved. (30) (4)

column2: ਬਯਾਦਿ ਦੋਸਤ, ਤਵਾਂ ਉ਼ਮਰ ਰਾ, ਬਸਰ ਬੁਰਦਨ॥
column5: A person should spend his whole life in the memory of his Beloved,

column2: ਕਿ ਦਰ ਬਰਾਬਰਿ-ਆਂ, ਹੇਚ ਕੀਮੀਆ ਨ ਰਸਦ॥੫॥
column5: Because, there is no other medicine compared to this treatment. (30) (5)

column2: ਤਮਾਮ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਗੇਤੀ, ਫ਼ਿਦਾਇ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਦਰਸ਼॥
column5: I wish I could sacrifice the entire wealth of this world and my life itself for Him,

column2: ਕਿ ਤਾ ਫ਼ਿਦਾਸ਼ ਨ ਗਰਦਦ, ਕਸੇ ਬਜਾ ਨ ਰਸਦ॥੬॥
column5: (He is such an Entity) that unless I do so and surrener completely, I cannot reach Him, the destination. (30) (6)

column2: ਫ਼ਿਦਾਇ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਦਰਸ਼ ਮੇ ਸ਼ਵਦ, ਅਜ਼ਾਂ ਗੋਯਾ॥
column5: Goyaa says, I am willing to sacrifice myself for the dust of His threshhold,

column2: ਕਿ ਹਰਕਿ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਨ ਗਰਦਦ, ਬ-ਮੁੱਦਆ਼ ਨ ਰਸਦ॥੭॥੩੦॥
column5: Because, unless I do so, I can never attain my goal. It is impossible to reach Him without complete humility. (30) (7)

column2: ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਦਰਗਹਿ ਊ ਕੀਮੀਆ ਗਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: Although a handful of dust of Akaalpurakh's abode can make a healing drug,

column2: ਹਰ ਗ਼ਦਾ ਰਾ ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹਿ ਹਫ਼ਤ-ਕਿਸ਼ਵਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੧॥੧॥
column5: It can also elevate every mendicant to be the king of seven countries. (31) (1)

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਦਰਗਾਹਿ ਤੂ ਸਦ ਤਾਜ ਅਸਤ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਫ਼ਰਕਿ ਮਨ
column5: The dust of Your court shines the forehead like hundereds of crown jewels,

column2: ਆਸੀਅਮ ਗਰ ਦਿਲ ਹਵਾਏ ਤਾਜੋ ਅਫ਼ਸਰ ਮੂ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੧॥੨॥
column5: It would be a great sin if my mind still desires to have pearls and thrones. (31) (2)

column2: ਕੀਮੀਆਗਰ ਗਰ ਜ਼ਿ ਮਿਸ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ ਤੀਲਾਇ ਦੂਰ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: If a chemist can convert copper into gold, then it is not impossible for

column2: ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਹੱਕ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਰਾ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦਿ ਅਨਵਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੧॥੩॥
column5: The heavenly pond to convert one grain of its soil into a radiant sun. (31) (3)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਆਰਫ਼ ਮੁਯੱਸਰ ਗਰ ਸ਼ਵਦ ਈਂ ਅਜਬ ਦਾਂਅ
column5: If you can meet the Providence, consider it as a miracle;

column2: ਈਂ ਤਨਤ ਰਾ ਤਾਲਬਿ ਹੱਕ, ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਅਕਬਰ ਮੇ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੧॥੪॥
column5: In fact, search for Him is the highst prize for his seekers. (31) (4)

column2: ਸ਼ਿਅਰਿ ਗੋਯਾ ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਕੂ ਬਿਸ਼ਨਵਦ ਅਜ਼ ਜਾਨੋ ਦਿਲ
column5: Anyone who listens to the poetry of Goyaa with his heart and soul,

column2: ਕੈ ਦਿਲਸ਼ ਪਰਵਾਏ ਲਾਅਲਿ ਦੁਕਾਨਿ ਗੌਹਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੧॥੫॥
column5: He will not then care for the gems and pearls in a jewelers shop. (31) (5)

column2: ਮਿਸਲਿ ਦਹਾਨਿ ਤੰਗਿ ਤੂ ਤੰਗ ਸ਼ਕਰ ਨ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ
column5: A sugar bubble is no match to the beauty and touch of your closed lips,

column2: ਈ ਮਿਸਲ ਰਾ ਕਿ ਗੁਫ਼ਤਮ ਜ਼ੀਂ ਖ਼ੂਬਤਰ ਨ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੩੨॥੧॥
column5: This is not a clich?. It is the truth unsurpassed. This is the best example that I can present here. (32) (1)

column2: ਬਾ ਹਿਜਰ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ ਸ਼ੌ ਗਰ ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਵਸਾਲੀ
column5: If You are yearning for a meeting with Him, then get used to 'separation',

column2: ਰਹ ਕੈ ਬਰੀ ਬਮੰਜ਼ਲ ਤਾ ਰਾਹਬਰ ਨ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੩੨॥੨॥
column5: Separation is the only guide. If there is no one to steer your way, how can you ever reach your destination? (32) (2)

column2: ਦਾਮਾਨਿ ਚਸ਼ਮ ਮਗੁਜ਼ਾਰ ਅਜ਼ ਦਸਤ ਹਮਚੂ ਮਿਜ਼ਗਾਂ
column5: Do not let go the hem of the (hands of) eyelids just as the eyelids are holding on to the eyes until,

column2: ਤਾ ਜੇਬਿ ਆਰਜ਼ੂਹਾ ਪੁਰ ਅਜ਼ ਗੁਹਰ ਨ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੩੨॥੩॥
column5: Your pocket of wishes is not filled with diamonds and pearls. (32) (3)

column2: ਸ਼ਾਖ਼ਿ ਉਮੀਦਿ ਆਸ਼ਕਿ ਹਰਗਿਜ਼ ਸਮਰ ਨਹਿ ਗੀਰਦ
column5: The branch (of the tree) of hope of a lover never blossoms unless

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਅਸ਼ਕਿ ਆਬਿ ਮਿਜ਼ਗਾਂ ਤਾਂ ਸਬਜ਼-ਤਰ ਨਹਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੩੨॥੪॥
column5: It receives irrigation from the drops of water (tears) of the eyelids. (32) (4)

column2: ਐ ਬੁਅਲਫ਼ਜ਼ੂਲ ਗੋਯਾ ਅਜ਼ ਇਸ਼ਕਿ ਊ ਮੱਜ਼ਨ ਦਮ
column5: O you dunce Goyaa! Why are you engaging in useless talk? Do not brag about your love for him, the Guru,

column2: ਕੋ ਪਾ ਨਹਦ ਦਰੀਂ ਰਹਿ ਆਂ ਰਾ ਸਰ ਨਹਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੩੨॥੫॥
column5: Because, only those who have already had their heads severed from their bodies are entitled to tread this path. (32) (5)

column2: ਗੁਲੇ ਹੋਲੀ ਬਬਾਗ਼ੇ ਦਹਰ ਬੂ ਕਰਦ
column5: The aroma of spring flowers of Holi festival makes the whole garden, the world, full of special fragrance.

column2: ਲਬੇ ਚੂੰ ਗੁੰਚਹ ਰਾ ਫ਼ਰਖੰਦਾ ਖ਼ੂ ਕਰਦ॥੩੩॥੧॥
column5: And gave the blossoming bud-like lips a pleasant disposition. (33) (1)

column2: ਗੁਲਾਬੋ ਅੰਬਰੋ ਮੁਸ਼ਕੋ ਅਬੀਰੇ
column5: The Akaalpurakh spread the roses, the sky, the fragrance of musk and

column2: ਚੂ ਬਾਰਾਨਿ ਬਾਰਸ਼ੇ ਅਜ਼ ਸੂ ਬਸੂ ਕਰਦ॥੩੩॥੨॥
column5: sandalwood all over like the raindrops. (33) (2)

column2: ਜ਼ਹੇ ਪਿਚਕਾਰੀਏ ਪੁਰ ਜ਼ਾਅਫ਼ਰਾਨੀ
column5: How beautiful and effective is a saffron filled squirt pump?

column2: ਕਿ ਹਰ ਬੇਰੰਗ ਰਾ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ਰੰਗੋ ਬੂ ਕਰਦ॥੩੩॥੩॥
column5: That it converts even the discolored and the ugly into colorful and aromatic. (33) (3)

column2: ਗੁਲਾਲੇ ਅਫ਼ਸ਼ਾਨੀਏ ਦਸਤੇ ਮੁਬਾਰਿਕ
column5: With the throwing of powdered red color by his holy hands on me

column2: ਜ਼ਮੀਨੋ ਆਸਮਾਂ ਰਾ ਸੁਰਖ਼ੁਰੂ ਕਰਦ॥੩੩॥੪॥
column5: It made both the earth and the heaven all crimsen colored for me. (33 ) (4)

column2: ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਗਸ਼ਤ ਰੰਗੀਨ ਅਜ਼ ਤੁਫ਼ੈਲਸ਼
column5: With His grace, both the worlds began to bathe in colorful dispositions,

column2: ਚੁ ਸ਼ਾਹਮ ਜਾਮਾ ਰੰਗੀਂ ਦਰ ਗੁਲੂ ਕਰਦ॥੩੩॥੫॥
column5: When he put shiny clothes suitable for only rich people on my neck. (33) (5)

column2: ਕਸੇ ਕੂ ਦੀਦ ਦੀਦਾਰੇ ਮੁਕੱਦਸ
column5: Anyone who was fortunate to get a sacred glimpse of Him, the Guru,

column2: ਮੁਰਾਦੇ ਉਮਰ ਰਾ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਨਿਕੋ ਦਰਦ॥੩੩॥੬॥
column5: Take it that he, surely, was able to fulfill his lifelong desire. (33) (6)

column2: ਸ਼ਵਦ ਕੁਰਬਾਨ ਖ਼ਾਕੇ ਰਾਹੇ ਸੰਗਤ
column5: Goyaa says, If I could sacrifice myself for the dust of the path traversed by noble souls,

column2: ਦਿਲੇ ਗੋਯਾ ਹਮੀਂ ਰਾ ਆਰਜ਼ੂ ਕਰਦ॥੩੩॥੭॥
column5: This is all that I have wished and persevered all my life. My life's ambition would be fulfilled. (33) (7)

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਵਸਫ਼ਸ਼ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼
column5: A musical description of the Master's great qualities and kudos is very savory to the tongue of a human being,

column2: ਨਾਮਿ ਊ ਅੰਦਰ ਦਹਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੪॥੧॥
column5: How palatable is it when His Naam is recited through the mouth of a human being? (34) (1)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਸੇਬਿ ਜ਼ਿ ਨਖ਼ਦਾਨੇ ਸ਼ੁਮਾ
column5: Wonderful! How beautiful is the dimple in your chin that is red and white like an apple?

column2: ਮੇਵਾ ਚੂੰ ਦਰ ਬੋਸਤਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੪॥੨॥
column5: Where can one find such a sweet and tasty fruit in the garden? (34) (2)

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਾ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਜ਼ਿ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਸ਼ੁਮਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: My eyes have light only because they can see your glimpse,

column2: ਜਾਂ ਨਿਸਾਰਿਸ਼ ਬਸਕਿ ਆਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੪॥੩॥
column5: In your glimpse, there is enormous comfort; that is why I am willing to sacrifice myself for it. (34) (3)

column2: ਸੁੰਬਲੇ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਤੂ ਦਿਲਿ ਰਾ ਬੁਰਦ ਅਸਤ
column5: The scented locks of your hair have captivated my mind and soul,

column2: ਆਂ ਲਬਿ ਲਾਅਲਿ ਤੂ ਜਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੪॥੪॥
column5: And it is hanging near your ruby red lips. It is so sensuous and relishable. (34) (4)

column2: ਲੱਜ਼ਤਿ ਗੋਯਾ ਨਹਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਬਿਹ ਅਜ਼ਾਂ
column5: O Goyaa! There is no other larger pleasure or sweeter,

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਸ਼ਿਅਰੇ ਤੂ ਬ-ਹਿੰਦੁਸਤਾਂ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੪॥੫॥
column5: What the people of India derive singing your poetry. (34) (5)

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਸੂਇ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼
column5: For the spiritually enlightened persons, only His posture is joyful,

column2: ਆਸ਼ਿਕਾਂ ਰਾ ਕੂਏ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੫॥੧॥
column5: And for the lovers, the streets of their beloved are the way to happiness. (35) (1)

column2: ਕਾਕਲਿ ਊ ਦਿਲ ਫ਼ਰੇਬਿ ਆਲਮ ਅਸਤ
column5: His (guru's) locks of hair have captivated the hearts of the entire world;

column2: ਤਾਲਿਬਾਂ ਰਾ ਮੂਏ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੫॥੨॥
column5: In fact, his devotees are enamored with each and every hair on his head. (36) (2)

column2: ਰੌਜ਼ਾਇ ਬਾਗ਼ਿ ਇਰਮ ਕੁਰਬਾਂ ਕੁਨਮ
column5: I love his (Guru's) street so much that

column2: ਬਸਕਿ ਮਾ ਰਾ ਕੂਇ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੫॥੩॥
column5: I will swap it anytime and sacrifice even the garden of heaven for it. (35) (3)

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਮੀ ਗਰਦਮ ਜ਼ਿ ਬੂਇ ਮਕਦਮਸ਼
column5: I get completely revived with the aroma of his holy feet, blessed arrival,

column2: ਬਸਕਿ ਮਾ ਰਾ ਬੂਇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੫॥੪॥
column5: That is why I relish that fragrance so much. (35) (4)

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਕਿ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼
column5: How tasteful and savoray is even the talk of the thought and remembrance of Akaalpurakh?

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਹਮਾ ਮੇਵਾ ਕਿ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੫॥੫॥
column5: This happens to be by far the sweetest of all fruits (35) (5)

column2: ਆਬ-ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ੀ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਆਲਮ ਮੇ ਸ਼ਵੀ
column5: If you aspire the pursuit for this kind of ambition,

column2: ਗਰ ਤੁਰਾ ਈਂ ਆਰਜ਼ੂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੫॥੬॥
column5: Then, you would be the one who would bestow the divine nectar to the whole world. (35) (6)

column2: ਸ਼ੇਅਰਿ ਗੋਯਾ ਬੇਸ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੀਰੋ ਸ਼ਕਰ
column5: The poetry of Goyaa is such a fruit in India

column2: ਮੇਵਾ ਦਰ ਹਿੰਦੁਸਤਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਲਜ਼ੀਜ਼॥੩੫॥੭॥
column5: That it is proclaimed as far sweeter than even sugar and milk. (35) (7)

column2: ਜ਼ ਫ਼ੈਜ਼ਿ ਮਕਦਮਤ ਐ ਅਬਰੂਇ ਫ਼ਸਲਿ ਬਹਾਰ
column5: O eyebrows (sprouts) of the crop of spring season! With the grace of your advent,

column2: ਜਹਾਂ ਚੂ ਬਾਗ਼ਿ ਇਰਮ ਪੁਰ ਸ਼ੁਦਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਗ਼ੁਲਜ਼ਾਰ॥੩੬॥੧॥
column5: The entire world is filled with flowers like the garden of heavens. (36) (1)

column2: ਤਬੱਸਮਿ ਤੂ ਜਹਾਂ ਰਾ ਹੱਯਾਤ ਮੀ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਦ
column5: O Guru! Your beautiful smile bestows and instills life to the world,

column2: ਕਰਾਰ ਦੀਦਾਇ ਸਾਹਿਬ-ਦਿਲਾਂ ਪੁਰ-ਇਸਰਾਰ॥੩੬॥੨॥
column5: And, this gives tranquility and stability to the mystical eyes of the saints and Pirs.? (36) (2)

column2: ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਇਸ਼ਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹੀਚ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਕਾਇਮ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: There is no ever-lasting love or devotion other than the love of Waaheguru,

column2: ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਆਸ਼ਿਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਹਮਾ ਫ਼ਨਾਹ ਪਿੰਦਾਰ॥੩੬॥੩॥
column5: And, one should deem everyone other than the devotees of Waaheguru as destructible. (36) (3)

column2: ਬਹਰ ਤਰਫ਼ ਕਿ ਨਿਗਾਹੇ ਕੁਨੀ ਰਵਾਂ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ੀ
column5: In whichever direction you look, you bestow and instil new life and spirit,

column2: ਨਿਗਾਹਿ ਤੁਸਤ ਕਿ ਦਰ ਹਰ ਤਰਫ਼ ਬਵਦ ਜਾਂਬਾਰ॥੩੬॥੪॥
column5: It is your vision only that blesses showers of new life everywhere. (36) (4)

column2: ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਕਿ ਦਰ ਹਮਾ ਹਾਲਸਤ ਹਾਜ਼ਰੋ ਨਾਜ਼ਿਰ
column5: Akaalpurakh is omnipresent at every place under all circumstances and with everyone all the times,

column2: ਕੁਜਾਸਤ ਦੀਦਾ ਕਿ ਬੀਨਦ ਬਹਰ ਤਰਫ਼ ਦੀਦਾਰ॥੩੬॥੫॥
column5: However, where is such an eye that is capable of visualizing His presence in every nook and corner? (36) (5)

column2: ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਆਰਫ਼ਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਕਸੇ ਨਜਾਤ ਨ-ਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: No one other than those dedicated to the love of God has ever been redeemed.

column2: ਅਜ਼ਲ ਜ਼ਮੀਨੋ ਜ਼ਮਾਂ ਰਾ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤਾ ਦਰ ਮਿਨਕਾਰ॥੩੬॥੬॥
column5: The 'death' has captured both the 'earth' and the 'time' with its sharp beak. (36) (6)

column2: ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਬਵਦ ਬੰਦਾਇ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਗੋਯਾ
column5: Goyaa says, A devotee of Akaalpurakh becomes immortal,

column2: ਕਿ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਬੰਦਗੀਅਸ਼ ਨੀਸਤ ਦਰ ਅਹਾਂ ਆਸਾਰ॥੩੬॥੭॥
column5: Because, without his meditation, no one else will ever leave a sign behind in this world. (36) (7)

column2: ਮਨ ਅਜ਼ ਜਵਾਂ ਕਿ ਪੀਰ ਸ਼ੁਦਮ ਦਰ ਕਿਨਾਰਿ ਉਮਰ
column5: I became old from young in the lap of the 'age',

column2: ਐ ਬਾ-ਤੋ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ ਮਰਾ ਦਰ ਕਿਨਾਰਿ ਉਮਰ॥੩੭॥੧॥
column5: How beautiful was my life that I spent in Your company! I owe the happiness of this journey to your grace!? (37) (1)

column2: ਦਮਹਾਇ ਮਾਂਦਾ ਰਾ ਤੂ ਚੁਨੀਂ ਮੁਗ਼ਤਨਮ ਸ਼ੁਮਾਰ
column5: Consider the remaining breaths of your life as blessed,

column2: ਆਖ਼ਿਰ ਖ਼ਿਜ਼ਾਂ ਬਰ ਆਵੁਰਦ ਈਂ ਨੌ ਬਹਾਰਿ ਉਮਰ॥੩੭॥੨॥
column5: Because, it will be the autumn (old age) that will bring the spring (season of youth) of your life one day. (37) (2)

column2: ਹਾਂ ਮੁਗਤਨਮ ਸ਼ੁਮਾਰ ਦਮੇ ਰਾ ਬ-ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: Yes, consider that moment as blessed that is spent in remembering God,

column2: ਚੂੰ ਬਾਦ ਮੀਰਵਦ ਜ਼ਿ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਦਰ ਸ਼ੁਮਾਰ ਉਮਰ॥੩੭॥੩॥
column5: Otherwise, the whole life, while counting your breaths, will disappear just like the air, while we look on. (37) (3)

column2: ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਰਵਾਂ ਚੂ ਕਾਫ਼ਲਾਇ ਮੌਜ ਪੈ ਬ ਪੈ
column5: The stream of life is flowing like an ever-moving caravan of tides of time,

column2: ਆਬੇ ਬਿਨੋਸ਼ ਯੱਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ੂਇ ਬਾਰਿ ਉਮਰ॥੩੭॥੪॥
column5: If possible, then try to have a momentary sip with every breath from this stream of life (37) (4)

column2: ਸਦ ਕਾਰ ਕਰਦਾਈ ਕਿ ਨਯਾਇਦ ਬਕਾਰਿ ਤੂ
column5: Goya says, You have indulged in hundreds of vain chores in life that will not be of any eventual use,

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਬਿਕੁਨ ਕਿ ਬਾਜ਼ ਬਿਆਇਦ ਬਕਾਰਿ ਉਮਰ॥੩੭॥੫॥
column5: Therefore, engage yourself in such activities that will be useful again and hereafter (37) (5)

column2: ਮਾ ਕਿ ਦੀਦੇਮ ਸਰਿ ਕੂਇ ਤੂ ਐ ਮਹਿਰਮਿ ਰਾਜ਼
column5: O Knower of all mysteries! We, who have seen the higher end of Your street,

column2: ਵਜ਼ ਹਮਾ ਰੂਇ ਫ਼ਗੰਦੇਮ ਸਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਨਿਆਜ਼॥੩੮॥੧॥
column5: Did bow and put our head down on the dust of the area in utter humility and turned ourselves away from everything else. (38) (1)

column2: ਤਾ ਬਗਰਦੇ ਸਰਿ ਕੂਇ ਤੂ ਬਗਰਦੀਦ ਆਮ
column5: Since I made my visits to your street as a commonplace,

column2: ਰੋਜ਼ਾਇ ਖ਼ੁਲਦੀ ਬਰੀਂ ਰਾ ਬਿਕੁਨਮ ਪਾ ਅੰਦਾਜ਼॥੩੮॥੨॥
column5: I have rejected the highest garden of heavens and considered it only as the floor under your doorsill. (38) (2)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ਮਿ ਕਾਕਲਿ ਮੁਸ਼ਕੀਂ ਦਿਲੋ ਦੀਂ ਬੁਰਦ ਅਜ਼ਮਾ
column5: The waves and the curls of your fragrant locks took away my heart and soul,

column2: ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਉਮਰ ਹਮੀਂ ਬੂਦ ਅਜ਼ੀਂ ਉਮਰਿ-ਦਰਾਜ਼॥੩੮॥੩॥
column5: And, this was the highest treasure gathered through my long life. (38) (3)

column2: ਮੁਸਹਫ਼ਿ ਰੂਇ ਤੂ ਹਾਫ਼ਿਜ਼ ਹਮਾ ਰਾ ਹਮਾ ਦਰ ਹਾਲ
column5: Your face's sight is that sacred text that protects everyone under all circumstances.

column2: ਖ਼ਮਿ ਅਬਰੂਇ ਤੂ ਮਹਿਰਾਬਿ ਦਿਲ ਅਹਿਲਿ ਨਿਮਾਜ਼॥੩੮॥੪॥
column5: A arcged wrinkle in Your eyebrow is the alcove of the mosque (meditation) in the minds of Your devotees. (38) (4)

column2: ਦਿਲਿ ਮਨ ਬੇ ਤੂ ਚੂਨਾਸਤ ਚਿਹ ਗੋਇਦਾ ਗੋਯਾ
column5: Goyaa says, How can I explain the condition of my mind when I am separated from you?

column2: ਹਮਚੂੰ ਆਂ ਸ਼ਮਆ ਕਿ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ-ਅਜ਼ ਸੋਜ਼ੋ ਗੁਦਾਜ਼॥੩੮॥੫॥
column5: It is like a lamp that always has to burn and melt its passions. (38) (5)

column2: ਬੇ ਤੂ ਆਲਮ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਹੈਰਾਨਸਤੋ ਬਸ
column5: O Guru! The whole world is bewildered and is confused without you,

column2: ਸੀਨਾ ਅਜ਼ ਹਿਜਰਿ ਤੂ ਬਿਰਯਾਂਸਤੋ ਬਸ॥੩੯॥੧॥
column5: My heart and soul are getting charred and getting cooked on a grill like Kabab due to your separation. (39) (1)

column2: ਤਾਲਿਬ ਮੌਲਾ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: Any seeker of God lives for ever (He is remembered for ever),

column2: ਬਰ ਜ਼ਬਾਨਸ਼ ਨਾਮਿ ਸੁਬਹਾਨ ਅਸਤੋ ਬਸ॥੩੯॥੨॥
column5: Because, he meditates and has only Naam of the Omnipotent on his tongue. (39) (2)

column2: ਖਾਲਿ ਮੁਸ਼ਕੀਨਸ਼ ਦਿਲਿ ਆਲਮ ਰਬੂਦ
column5: The fragrant black speckle, the mole, of your cheek, has enamored the entire world,

column2: ਕੁਫ਼ਰਿ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਸ਼ ਦਾਮਿ ਈਮਾਨਸਤੋ ਬਸ॥੩੯॥੩॥
column5: And, your locks of hair are just like a snare for the faith and the religion and nothing else.(39) (3)

column2: ਜ਼ੂਦ ਬਿਨੁਮਾ ਆਂ ਰੁਖਿ ਚੂੰ ਆਫ਼ਤਾਬ
column5: O Guru! Kindly show me your sun-like face as soon as possible,

column2: ਈਂ ਇਲਾਜਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਗਿਰੀਆਨਸਤੋ ਬਸ॥੩੯॥੪॥
column5: Because, this is the only cure for my tearful eyes and nothing else. (39) (4)

column2: ਦਿਲ ਨਿਸਾਰੀ ਕਾਮਤਿ ਰਾਅਨਾਈਇ ਊ
column5: My heart and soul is simply captivated for his handsome stature and gait,

column2: ਜਾਂ ਫ਼ਿਦਾਈ ਜਾਨਿ ਜਾਨਾਂ ਨਸਤੋ ਬਸ॥੩੯॥੫॥
column5: And, my life is for sacrifice to the dust of my beloved's feet. (39) (5)

column2: ਗਰ ਬਿ-ਪੁਰਸੀ ਹਾਲਿ ਗੋਯਾ ਯੱਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ
column5: Alas! I wish you had asked Goyaa even for a moment, How are you?

column2: ਈਂ ਇਲਾਜਿ ਦਰਦਿ ਹਰਮਾਨਸਤੋ ਬਸ॥੩੯॥੬॥
column5: Because, this is the only remedy for my painfully tortured heart. (39) (6)

column2: ਮੁਦਾਮ ਬਾਦਾ-ਕਸ਼ੋ ਸੂਫੀ ਓ ਸਫ਼ਾ ਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼
column5: After getting drunk (with His Naam), one should become pious and chaste,

column2: ਤਮਾਮਿ ਜ਼ੁਹਦ ਸ਼ੌ ਵਰਿੰਦਿ ਬੀਨਵਾ ਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼॥੪੦॥੧॥
column5: One should be inebriated and become indifferent to life and an embodiment of meditation.? (40) (1)

column2: ਬਸੂਇ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਮਿਅਫ਼ਗਨ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਕਿ ਬੇ-ਬਸਰੀ
column5: You should not cast your eyes just even to look towards any one else; this will be an act of blindness;

column2: ਤਮਾਮਿ ਚਸ਼ਮ ਸ਼ੌ ਵ ਸੂਇ ਦੋਸਤ ਵਾ ਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼॥੪੦॥੨॥
column5: You should become an eye from head to toe, and aim your vision only towards your Beloved. (40) (2)

column2: ਬ-ਗਿਰਦਿ ਕਾਮਤਿ ਆਂ ਸ਼ਾਹਿ ਦਿਲਰੁਬਾ ਮੀ ਗਰਦ
column5: Circumambulate around the torso of heart-stealing king, the Guru,

column2: ਅਸੀਰਿ ਹਲਕਾਇ ਆਂ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਮੁਸ਼ਕ ਸਾ ਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼॥੪੦॥੩॥
column5: And, assume yourself as the prisoner of the knot of the fragrant lock of his hair. (40) (3)

column2: ਨ ਗੋਇਮਤ ਕਿ ਸੂਇ ਦੈਰ ਯਾ ਹਰਮ ਮੀ ਰੌ
column5: I am not asking anyone to go to the temple or the Muslin shrine.

column2: ਬਹਰ ਤਰਫ਼ ਕਿ ਰਵੀ ਜਾਨਿਬਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼॥੪੦॥੪॥
column5: I am simply suggesting that no matter where you decide to go, you should always keep your face towards the Almighty. (40) (4)

column2: ਬਸੂਇ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਚੂ ਬੇਗ਼ਾਨਗਾਂ ਚਿਹ ਮੀ ਗਰਦੀ
column5: Turning away from me like a stranger, why are you are paying attention to my rivals?

column2: ਹਮੀ ਜ਼ਿ ਹਾਲਿ ਦਿਲਿ ਖ਼ਸਤਾ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ ਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼॥੪੦॥੫॥
column5: Just look towards me even for a short while and get familiar with the condition of this broken heart. (40) (5)

column2: ਮੁਦਾਮ ਸ਼ਾਕਿਰੋ ਸ਼ਾਦਾਬ ਚੂੰ ਦਿਲਿ ਗੋਯਾ
column5: Goya says, Become content and pleasant like my heart, And do not become indifferent after having your desires fulfilled.

column2: ਤਮਾਮਿ ਮੁਤਲਿਬੋ ਫ਼ਾਰਿਗ਼ ਜ਼ਿ ਮੁਦਆ ਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼॥੪੦॥੬॥
column5: In fact, rid yourself of all purpose and pursuits.(This way, one can attain the real goal) (40) (6)

column2: ਹਮਾ ਰਾ ਸੀਨਾ ਬਿਰੀਆਨਸਤੋ ਬਿਰੀਆਨਸਤੋ ਬਿਰੀਆਨਸ਼
column5: Hearts of everyone in deep love are scorched and charred,

column2: ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਆਂ ਦੀਦਾਰ ਹੈਰਾਂਸਤੋ ਹੈਰਾਨਸ਼॥੪੧॥੧॥
column5: Both the worlds are amazed and even desperately restless for his glimpse. (41) (1)

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਕੂਇ ਤੂ ਕਾਂ ਸੁਰਮਾਇ ਅਹਿਲਿ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ
column5: The dust of your street is like a collyrium for the eyes of those with divine sight,

column2: ਨਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਇਲਾਜੇ ਬਿਹ ਬਰਾਇ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਗਿਰੀਆਨਸ਼॥੪੧॥੨॥
column5: And for the tearful eyes, there is no other better cure than this. (41) (2)

column2: ਮਹੋ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦ ਗਿਰਦਿ ਕੂਇ ਊ ਗਰਦੰਦ ਰੂਜੌ ਸ਼ਬ
column5: Both the moon and the sun keep circling around His (God/Guru) abode night and day,

column2: ਅਜਾਇਬ ਰੌਸ਼ਨੀ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਿ ਦੋ-ਆਲਮ ਹਸਤ ਅਹਿਸਾਨਸ਼॥੪੧॥੩॥
column5: It is His blessing that He has bestowed them the capability of providing light to both the worlds. (41) (3)

column2: ਬ-ਹਰ ਸੂਇ ਕਿ ਮੀ ਬੀਨਮ ਜਮਾਲਸ਼ ਜਲਵਾਗਰ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ
column5: Wherever I see, I find His comeliness and splendor all over.

column2: ਜਹਾਂ ਆਸ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਾ ਓ ਸ਼ੈਦਾ ਮਦਾਮ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ੁਲਫਿ ਪਹਿਚਾਨਸ਼॥੪੧॥੪॥
column5: The whole world is anxious and daft because of His curly lock of hair. (41) (4)

column2: ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਜੇਬਿ ਜ਼ਮੀਂ ਪੁਰ ਲੂਲੂਇ ਲਾਲਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਅਸ਼ਕਿ ਮਨ
column5: Goyaa says, The pockets of the earth are filled with the pearls like tears from my eyes.

column2: ਜਹਾਂ ਬਿਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤਾ ਅਮ ਗੋਯਾ ਬ ਯਾਦਿ ਲਾਅਲਿ ਖ਼ੰਦਾਨਕ॥੪੧॥੫॥
column5: I have captured the whole world when I remembered the smile from his red lips. (41) (5)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸ ਸ਼ਨੀਦਾ ਅਸਤ ਜ਼ਿ ਤੂ ਗ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਗੂਇ ਖ਼ਾਸ
column5: Anyone who has listened to Guru's enchanted words during his blessed company,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਸਦ ਗ਼ਮਿ ਸ਼ਦੀਦ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਜ਼ੂਦ ਤਰ ਖ਼ਲਾਸ॥੪੨॥੧॥
column5: Gets redeemed from hundreds of dire sorrows just in moments. (42) (1)

column2: ਆਬਿ ਹਯੱਾਤਿ ਮਾ ਸਖ਼ੁਨਿ ਪੀਰਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਅਸਤ
column5: The word of a complete and perfect Guru is like a nectar-talisman for all of us.

column2: ਦਿਲਹਾਇ ਮੁਰਦਾ ਰਾ ਬਿਕੁਨਦ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਓ ਖ਼ਲਾਸ॥੪੨॥੨॥
column5: It can revive and give salvation to demoralized and half dead minds.(42) (2)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦ-ਨਮਾਈਏ ਤੂ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਸਤ ਦੂਰ ਤਰ
column5: God Almighty is miles farther away from the deception of our ego,

column2: ਬੀਨੀ ਦਰੂਨਿ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਸ਼ਵੀ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦੀ ਖ਼ਲਾਸ॥੪੨॥੩॥
column5: If we were to do some introspection, we can get rid of this vanity. (42) (3)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਸਾਲਕਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਰਾ ਬਾ ਕੁਨੀ ਤੂ ਖ਼ਿਦਮਤੇ
column5: If you do service to the holy and noble souls,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਕੈਦਿ ਗ਼ਮਿ ਜਹਾਂ, ਬ-ਸ਼ਵਦ ਜਾਨਿ ਤੋ ਖ਼ਲਾਸ॥੪੨॥੪॥
column5: You, then, can get rid of all worldly agonies and sorrows. (42) (4)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਤੂ ਦਸਤਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਹਿਰਸ ਕੋਤਾਹ ਕੁਨ
column5: O Goyaa! You must withdraw your hands from desire and greed,

column2: ਤਾ ਅੰਦਰੂਨਿ ਖ਼ਾਨਾ ਬੀਨੀ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਖ਼ਾਸ॥੪੨॥੫॥
column5: So that you could realize the eclat of Almighty all within yourself. (42) (5)

column2: ਬਿਆ ਚੂ ਸਰਵਿ ਖ਼ਰਾਮਾਂ ਦਮੇ ਬ-ਸੈਰਿ ਰਿਆਜ਼
column5: Like the carefree pace of cypress tree, if you, the Guru, could visit the garden even for a moment,

column2: ਸੂਇ ਤੂ ਦੀਦਾਇ ਮਾ-ਰਾ ਨਿਗਾਹ ਗਸ਼ਤ ਬਿਆਜ॥੪੩॥੧॥
column5: My eyes (of my soul) have completely exhausted themselves waiting for this for your arrival. (43) (1)

column2: ਬਰਾਇ ਰੇਸ਼ਿ ਦਿਲਮ ਖ਼ੰਦਾਇ ਤੂ ਮਰਹਮ ਬਸ
column5: Just one smile of yours works as the unguent for my wounded (broken) heart,

column2: ਤਬੱਸੁਮਿ ਲਬਿ ਲਾਅਲਤ ਦਵਾਏ ਹਰ ਇਮਰਾਜ਼॥੪੩॥੨॥
column5: And the smile from your ruby red lips is the cure of all of my ills. (43) (2)

column2: ਨਿਗਾਹ ਕਰਦ ਵਾ ਮਤਾਇ ਦਿਲਮ ਬ-ਗ਼ਾਰਤ ਬੁਰਦ
column5: He directed his vision towards me just for once, and stole all my inner assets;

column2: ਬੁਰੀਦ ਜੇਬਿ ਦਿਲਮ ਰਾ ਬਿ ਗ਼ਮਜ਼ਾ ਚੂੰ ਮਿਕਰਾਜ਼॥੪੩॥੩॥
column5: With his skewed looks, he took away my heart, as if someone had cut my pockets with a pair of scissors. (43) (3)

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਫ਼ੈਜ਼ਿ ਮਕਦਮਤ ਐ ਨੌ-ਬਹਾਰਿ ਗੁਲਸ਼ਨਿ ਹੁਸਨ
column5: O new spring season of the garden of elegance and radiance! With the blessings of your arrival,

column2: ਜਹਾਂ ਚੂੰ ਬਾਗ਼ਿ ਇਰਮ ਕਰਦਾਈ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਫ਼ਿਆਜ਼॥੪੩॥੪॥
column5: You have converted this world into a heavenly garden of Paradise. How great is the grantor of such a boon! (43) (4)

column2: ਚਿਰਾ ਬਹਾਲਤਿ ਗੋਯਾ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਨਮੀ ਫ਼ਿਗਨੀ
column5: Goyaa says, Why don't you look towards my pitiable condition just for once?

column2: ਕਿ ਯੱਕ ਨਿਗਾਹਿ ਤੂ ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਮੁਰਾਦਿ ਅਹਿਲਿ ਇਗ਼ਰਾਜ਼॥੪੩॥੫॥
column5: Because, for the needy and indigent people, your one look fulfills all their desires and wishes. (43) (5)

column2: ਬਸਕਿ ਮਾ ਰਾ ਹਸਤ ਬਾ ਤੋ ਇਰਤਬਾਤ
column5: O Guru! We have a special and close relationship with you.

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਕਦੂਮਿ ਤੁਸਤ ਦਰ ਆਲਮ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਤ॥੪੪॥੧॥
column5: Your advent and music of your footsteps has filled the whole world with overall happiness. (44) (1)

column2: ਫ਼ਰਸ਼ ਕਰਦਮ ਦਰ ਕਦੂਮਿ ਰਾਹਿ ਤੂ
column5: I have spread my blossoming heart and wide-open eyes as a carpet

column2: ਦੀਦਾ ਓ ਦਿਲ ਰਾ ਕਿ ਬੂਦਾ ਦਰ-ਬਸਾਤ॥੪੪॥੨॥
column5: on the path of your arrival. (44) (2)

column2: ਬਰ ਫ਼ਕੀਰਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਰਹਿਮੇ ਬਕੁਨ
column5: You should be kind and benevolent to the mendicant-devotees of the Lord,

column2: ਤਾ ਦਰੀਂ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਬ-ਯਾਬੀ ਇੰਬਸਾਤ॥੪੪॥੩॥
column5: So that you may get ample happiness in this world. (44) (3)

column2: ਦਾਇਮਾ ਦਿਲ ਰਾ ਬਸੂਇ ਹੱਕ ਬਿਆਰ
column5: lways keep your heart and soul directed towards the love of Waaheguru,

column2: ਤਾ ਬ-ਆਸਾਂ ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰੀ ਜ਼ੀਂ ਪੁਲਿ ਸਰਾਤ॥੪੪॥੪॥
column5: So that you can spend your mundane life in this world with ease. (44) (4)

column2: ਨੀਸਤ ਆਸੂਦਾ ਕਸੇ ਦਰ ਜ਼ੇਰਿ ਚਰਖ਼
column5: No one under this sky is happy, content and prosperous.

column2: ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰ ਐ ਗੋਯਾ ਅਜ਼ੀਂ ਕੁਹਨਾ ਰੁਬਾਤ॥੪੪॥੫॥
column5: O Goyaa! You should pass through and leave this old boarding house behind with caution. (44) (5)

column2: ਬ-ਹਰ ਕੁਜਾ ਕਿ ਰਵੀ ਜਾਨਿ ਮਨ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਾਫ਼ਿਜ਼
column5: O my beloved (Guru)! May lmighty be your Protector wherever you choose

column2: ਬ-ਬੁਰਦਾਈ ਦਿਲਿ ਈਮਾਨਿ ਮਨ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਾਫ਼ਿਜ਼॥੪੫॥੧॥
column5: You have taken away my heart and faith; May Almighty be your protector every where.? (45) (1)

column2: ਬਿਆ ਕਿ ਬੁਲਬੁਲੋ ਗੁਲ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਇੰਤਜ਼ਾਰਿ ਤੂ ਅੰਦ
column5: Both the nightingale and the flowers are awaiting your arrival, O Guru!

column2: ਦਮੇ ਬਜਾਨਿਬਿ ਬੁਸਤਾਨਿ ਮਨ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਾਫ਼ਿਜ਼॥੪੫॥੨॥
column5: Kindly drop in my garden for just a moment and may the Lord be your protector wherever you choose to prevail. (45) (2)

column2: ਨਮਕ ਜ਼ਿ ਲਾਲਿ ਲਬਤ ਰੇਜ਼ ਬਰਦਿਲਿ ਰੇਸ਼ਮ
column5: Kindly sprinkle some salt on my wounded heart from your red lips,

column2: ਪਸ਼ੀਦ ਜ਼ਿ ਸੀਨਾਇ ਬਿਰੀਆਨਿ ਮਨ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਾਫ਼ਿਜ਼॥੪੫॥੩॥
column5: And singe my Kabaab-like charred heart. May Providence be your protector wherever you decide to prevail.? (45) (3)

column2: ਚਿ ਖ਼ੁੱਸ਼ ਬਵਦ ਕਿ ਖ਼ਰਾਮਤ ਕੱਦਤ ਚੂ ਸਰਵਿ ਬੁਲੰਦ
column5: How nice it would be if your Cyprus-like tall and slim stature

column2: ਦਮੇ ਬਸੂਇ ਗੁਲਿਸਤਾਨਿ ਮਨ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਾਫ਼ਿਜ਼॥੪੫॥੪॥
column5: Would visit my garden for a walk even for a moment! May the Providence be your Protector wherever you choose to be! (45) (4)

column2: ਬਿਆ ਬ-ਮਰਦਮਕਿ ਦੀਦਾ ਅਮ ਕਿ ਖ਼ਾਨਾਇ ਤੁਸਤ
column5: Goyaa says, Kindly come! Come and abide in the pupils of my eyes,

column2: ਦਰੂਨਿ ਦੀਦਾਇ ਗਿਰੀਆਨਿ ਮਨ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਾਫ਼ਿਜ॥੪੫॥੫॥
column5: Because, your abode happens to be within my crying tearful eyes. May God be with you.(45)(5)

column2: ਐ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਤੂ ਰੌਨਿਕਿ ਬਾਜ਼ਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਮਆ
column5: O Guru! Your face is the reason for the brightness and glamor of the lamp,

column2: ਅਸ਼ਕਿ ਰੇਜ਼ਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਗੌਹਰ-ਬਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਮਆ॥੪੬॥੧॥
column5: And, it is because of you, that the pearl-showering eyes of the (candle) lamp is shedding tears. (46) (1)

column2: ਮਹਰਮ ਹਰਫ਼ਾਤਿ ਊ ਰਾ ਗਸ਼ਤਾ ਅਸ਼ਤ
column5: When you secret qualities became known,

column2: ਅਸ਼ਕ ਮੇ ਰੇਜ਼ਦ, ਦਿਲਿ ਅਫ਼ਗਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਮਾਅ॥੪੬॥੨॥
column5: The wounded delicate heart of the lamp was shedding tears. (46) (2)

column2: ਹਰ ਕੁਜਾ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਚਰਾਗੇ ਕਰਦਾ ਅੰਦ
column5: Wherever the people have lit a lamp,

column2: ਯੱਕ ਗੁਲੇ ਬੂਦ ਅਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਗੁਲਜ਼ਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਮਆ॥੪੬॥੩॥
column5: Consider that to be a floweer of the lamp's garden. (46) (3)

column2: ਤਾ ਕਿਹ ਬਰ-ਅਫ਼ਰੋਖ਼ਤੀ ਰੁਖ਼ਸਾਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ
column5: Since the time, you have shown the elegance of your face,

column2: ਮੀ ਸ਼ਵਦ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਿ ਤੂ ਸਦ ਬਾਰ ਸ਼ਮਆ॥੪੬॥੪॥
column5: The candle lamp, out of endearment, is offering itself as a sacrifice for you hundreds of times. (46) (4)

column2: ਗਿਰਦਿ ਰੁਖ਼ਸਾਰਿ ਤੂ ਅਜ਼ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਨਿਸਾਰ
column5: In order to sacrifice themselves hundred times for your elegant face,

column2: ਜਾਂ ਬਰੀਜ਼ਦ ਦੀਦਾਹਾਇ ਜ਼ਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਮਆ॥੪੬॥੫॥
column5: The tearful eyes of the candle lamp are pouring their life. (46) (5)

column2: ਬਸਕਿ ਇਮਸ਼ਬ ਨਾਮਦੀ ਅਜ਼ ਇੰਤਜ਼ਾਰ
column5: You did not show up tonight when the light of candle had intense expectation of your arrival,

column2: ਸੋਖ਼ਤ ਮਹਿਫਲ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਆਤਿਸ਼ ਬਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਮਆ॥੪੬॥੬॥
column5: Then the fire-sprinkling eye of the lamp burned down the entire gala assembly.? (46) (6)

column2: ਸੁਬਹ ਦਮ ਗੋਯਾ ਤਮਾਸ਼ਿਾਇ ਅਜੀਬ
column5: Goyaa says, How wonderful and unusual is the sight in the early morning,

column2: ਜੁਮਲਾ ਆਲਮ ਖ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਾ ਓ ਬੇਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਮਆ॥੪੬॥੭॥
column5: When the whole world is sleeping but the sleeping lamp alone is absolutely awake. (46) (7)

column2: ਸਾਕੀਆ ਬਰਖ਼ੇਜ਼ ਵ ਹਾਂ ਪੁਰ ਕੁਨ ਅੱਯਾਗ
column5: O bartender ! Please get up and fill up the glass of my drink,

column2: ਤਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਨੋਸ਼ਿ ਊ ਕੁਨਮ ਰੰਗੀਂ ਦਿਮਾਗ॥੪੭॥੧॥
column5: So that, with it, I can change the complexion of my thought and brain into a colorful one. (47) (1)

column2: ਹਲਕਾਇ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਤ ਦਿਲਮ-ਰਾ ਬੁਰਦਾ ਬੂਦ
column5: The noose of your lock of hair captured my heart and blew it away.

column2: ਯਾਫ਼ਤਮ ਅਜ਼ ਪੇਚ ਪੇਚਿ ਊ ਸੁਰਾਗ਼॥੪੭॥੨॥
column5: I was searching the same message of truth in each curl twist by twist. (47) (2)

column2: ਈਂ ਵਜੂਦੇ ਖ਼ਾਕੋ ਆਬੋ ਆਤਿਸ਼ ਅਸਤ
column5: This body of dust is the interplay of fire and water,

column2: ਡਰ ਵਜੂਦੇ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ ਹਰ ਚਾਰ ਬਾਗ਼॥੪੭॥੩॥
column5: You can relay your light from your own candle. (47) (3)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੁਆਇ ਪਰਤਵਿ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਪਾਕ
column5: From the radiant rays of Your chaste glimpse,

column2: ਸਦ ਹਜ਼ਾਰਾਂ ਹਰ ਤਰਫ਼ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਚਿਰਾਗ਼॥੪੭॥੪॥
column5: Hundreds and thousands of lamps were lighted every where. (47) (4)

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਊ ਕੁਨ ਯਾਦਿ ਊ ਗੋਯਾ ਮੁਦਾਮ
column5: O Goyaa! You should always remember Him, and meditate only on His Naam.

column2: ਤਾ ਬਯਾਬੀ ਅਜ਼ ਗ਼ਮਿ ਆਲਮ ਫ਼ਰਾਗ॥੪੭॥੫॥
column5: So that you could be redeemed and free from the worries here and the hereafter. (47) (5)

column2: ਗਰ ਜ਼ਿ ਰਾਹਿ ਸਾਜ਼ੀ ਸੀਨਾ ਸਾਫ਼
column5: If, in the interest of your love for your Beloved, you can cleanse your mind of the (five) vices without any doubt or suspicion,

column2: ਜ਼ੂਦ ਬੀਨੀ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ ਰਾ ਬੇ ਗ਼ੁਜ਼ਾਫ਼॥੪੮॥੧॥
column5: Then, without any exaggeration, you will find your real self soon. (48) (1)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦੀ ਤੂ ਦੂਰ ਗਸ਼ਤਾ ਚੂੰ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: We have been driven farther away from the Almighty due to our egos;

column2: ਦੂਰ ਕੁੱਨ ਖ਼ੁਦ-ਬੀਨੀ ਓ ਬੀਂ ਬੇ-ਗ਼ਿਲਾਫ਼॥੪੮॥੨॥
column5: Therefore, get rid of your vanity; do not be driven by the whims of your mind, then you can see clearly the Reality, the Beneficient. (48) (2)

column2: ਆਸ਼ਿਕਾਂ ਦਾਰੰਦ ਚੂੰ ਇਸ਼ਕਿ ਮੁਦਾਮ
column5: The real lovers (of God) are always imbued in His love,

column2: ਦਮ ਮਜ਼ਨ ਦਰ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾ ਐ ਮਰਦਿ ਲਾਫ਼॥੪੮॥੩॥
column5: O you bragger! Do not boast before them about love and devotion. (48) (3)

column2: ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰ ਅਜ਼ ਲੱਜ਼ਤਿ ਈਂ ਖ਼ਮਸਾ ਹਵਾਸ
column5: You should give up the sensual pleasures of all the five sense organs,

column2: ਤਾ ਬਯਾਬੀ ਬਜ਼ਤੇ ਅਜ਼ ਜਾਮਿ ਸਾਫ਼॥੪੮॥੪॥
column5: So that you could really taste the savor of a clean glass of holy elixir. (48) (4)

column2: ਗਰ ਬਜੋਈ ਰਾਹਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਰਾ ਮੁਦਾਮ
column5: Goyaa says, We should always be seeking and on the look out for the path of our Satguru,

column2: ਤਾ ਸ਼ਵੀ ਗੋਯਾ ਮੁੱਬਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ਿਲਾਫ਼॥੪੮॥੫॥
column5: So that, by traversing in the opposite direction, we may not lose our way; we can be redeemed from (the sin of) double-mindedness and dilemma. (48) (5)

column2: ਰਬੂਦ ਮਕਦਮਿ ਵਸਲਸ਼ ਜ਼ਿ ਮਨ ਇਨਾਨਿ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕ
column5: When His (Guru's) arrival time came closer, I lost control of the reins of the pains of separation,

column2: ਦਿਹੇਮ ਤਾ ਬਕੁਜਾ ਸ਼ਰਹਾ ਦਾਸਤਾਨਿ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕ॥੪੯॥੧॥
column5: and what transpired in my mind after this is a sad story; (49) (1)

column2: ਨ ਬੂਦ ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਤੂ ਦਰ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਚਸ਼ਮੇ ਅਬਰੂਏਮ
column5: There is no one else in my eyes and eyebrows except you, the Guru,

column2: ਨ ਯਾਫ਼ਤੇਮ ਦਰਾਂ ਜਾ ਦਿਗਰ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕ॥੪੯॥੨॥
column5: That is why I did not any sign of separation, except myself. (49) (2)

column2: ਹਨੂਜ਼ ਹਿਜਰ ਨਿਆਲੂਦਾ ਬੂਦ ਵਸਲਿ ਤੁਰਾ
column5: The (pains of) 'separation' has not yet realized the (euphoria of) 'meeting',

column2: ਸ਼ਨੀਦਾਇਮ ਸੁਖ਼ਨਿ ਵਸਲ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ਬਾਨਿ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕ॥੪੯॥੩॥
column5: I have been hearing stories about 'oneness and meeting' from 'separation'. (49) (3)

column2: ਚੁਨਾਂ ਜ਼ਿ ਹਿਜਰਤ ਆਤਿਸ਼ ਫ਼ਿਤਾਦਾ ਦਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਮਨ
column5: Ever since your 'separation' stoked such a fire in my heart, flaring it up

column2: ਕਿ ਬਰਕਿ ਨਾਲਾਇ ਮਨ ਸੋਖ਼ਤ ਖ਼ਾਨਿਮਾਨਿ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕ॥੪੯॥੪॥
column5: That my wails and supplications fell on the abode of 'separation' (like a lightening) and burnt it down to ashes. (49) (4)

column2: ਚਿ ਕਰਦਾ-ਸਤ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕਿ ਤੂ ਬਰ ਸਰਿ ਗੋਯਾ
column5: Disunion from you has put Goyaa in such an unusual state of mind

column2: ਕਿ ਦਰ ਸ਼ੁਮਾਰ ਨਿਆਇਦ ਮਰਾ ਬਿਆਨਿ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕ॥੪੯॥੫॥
column5: that he has related this painful saga continuously so many times that there is no count to it and my thought stands still. (49) (5)

column2: ਬਿਸ਼ਨੌ ਅਜ਼ ਮਨ ਹਰਫ਼ੇ ਅਜ਼ ਰਫ਼ਤਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ
column5: Please listen from me about the demeanor of 'love',

column2: ਤਾ ਬ-ਯਾਬੀ ਲੱਜ਼ਤ ਅਜ਼ ਗੁਫ਼ਤਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੧॥
column5: So that you may begin to savor the taste of romantic tale. (50) (1)

column2: ਇਸ਼ਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਹਰ ਕਿ ਰਾ ਮਿਸਮਾਰ ਕਰਦ
column5: Even if the deep love for the Almighty ruins someone's worldly life,

column2: ਮੁਗ਼ਤਨਮ ਦਾਨਦ ਸਰੂਰਿ ਕਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੨॥
column5: He still considers this divine pleasure far superior than anything else. (50) (2)

column2: ਆਂ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਦਮ ਕੁ ਬਯਾਦਸ਼ ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰਦ
column5: That moment and breath is blessed that is spent in His remembrance.

column2: ਸਰ ਹਮਾ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਕੂ ਰਵਦ ਦਰ ਕਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੩॥
column5: And only that head is fortunate that dedicates and sacrifices itself to the path of devotion. (50) (3)

column2: ਸਦ ਹਜ਼ਾਰਾਂ ਜਾਂ ਬ-ਕਫ਼ ਦਰ ਰਾਹਿ ਊ
column5: Thousands of devotees, having risked their lives, are standing and

column2: ਈਸਤਾਦਾ ਤਕੀਆ ਬਰ ਦੀਵਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੪॥
column5: Reclining against the wall of the passage to His abode. (50) (4)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਦਰ ਰਾਹਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਬੇ-ਅਦਬ
column5: Anyone who has been sacrilegious in the Divine path,

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਮਨਸੂਰਸ਼ ਸਜ਼ਦ ਬੇ ਦਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੫॥
column5: Like Manssor, the crucifix (of love) is the befitting punishment for him. (50) (5)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਦਿਲ ਕੂ ਜ਼ਿ ਇਸ਼ਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਪੁਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: Blessed is the heart that is imbued with its love for Akaalpurakh;

column2: ਖ਼ਮ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਪੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਫ਼ਲਕ ਅਜ਼ ਬਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੬॥
column5: In fact, it is the (heavy) weight of intense devotion that has bent the back of the heavenly sky. (50) (6)

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਮਾਨੀ ਦਾਇਮਾ ਐ ਨੇਕ ਖ਼ੂ
