column5: O kind-hearted person and a person of resolve! If you could listen intently to just one note of the musical instrument (harp) of love and devotion,

column2: ਬਿਸ਼ਨਵੀ ਗਰ ਜ਼ਮਜ਼ਮਾ ਅਜ਼ ਤਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੭॥
column5: You will become immortal forever. (50) (7)

column2: ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹਾਂ ਸਲਤਨਤ ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਾਸ਼ਤੰਦ
column5: The kings abrogated their entire empires, So that

column2: ਤਾ ਸ਼ਵੰਦ ਆਂ ਮਹਿਰਮਿ ਅਸਰਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੮॥
column5: They could understand and realize the mysteries and tempo of love. (50) (8)

column2: ਮਰਹਮੇ ਜੁਜ਼ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਦੀਗਰ ਨ ਦੀਦ
column5: Anyone who has been struck by the love-bug (disease), like Goyaa,

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਗੋਯਾ ਹਰ ਕਿ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਬੀਮਾਰਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੫੦॥੯॥
column5: He has not seen any other ointment except submission and the meditation of (the Naam of) Waaheguru. (50) (9)

column2: ਤਾ ਆਫ਼ਰੀਦਾ ਅਸਤ ਮਰਾ ਆਂ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਪਾਕ
column5: The chaste Akaalpurakh, the Protector of all, has given me birth for the reason,

column2: ਜੁਜ਼ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਨਿਆਇਦ ਜ਼ਿ ਜਿਸਮਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ॥੫੧॥੧॥
column5: That nothing else but the Naam of the Beneficient should come out of this torso of dust. (51) (1)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਿਜਰਿ ਤੁਸਤ ਜਾਨੋ ਦਿਲਿ ਆਸ਼ਕਾਂ ਚੁਨੀਂ
column5: The state of the heart and soul of your lovers during their separation from you is such,

column2: ਚੂੰ ਲਾਲਾ ਦਾਗ਼ ਬਰ ਜਿਗਰੋ ਸੀਨਾ ਚਾਕ ਚਾਕ॥੫੧॥੨॥
column5: That their heart is scarred like a poppy flower and their soul torn apart. (51) (2)

column2: ਈਂ ਗੁਫ਼ਤਾ ਅਸਤ ਮਰਗ ਕਿ ਬੇ-ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਵਦ
column5: The time spent without remembering You has been called the 'death',

column2: ਚੂੰ ਸਾਇਆ ਤੂ ਹਸਤ ਨਦਾਰੇਮ ਹੀਚ ਬਾਕ॥੫੧॥੩॥
column5: But as long as I am blessed to be under your protection, I have no fear (of death). (51) (3)

column2: ਤਖ਼ਤੋ ਨਗੀਂ ਗੁਜ਼ਾਸ਼ਤਾ ਸ਼ਾਹਾਂ ਜ਼ ਬਹਿਰੇ ਤੂ
column5: The kings and emperors gave up their thrones and crowns for you, O Guru,

column2: ਬਿਕੁਸ਼ਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਰੁਖ਼ ਨਕਾਬ ਕਿ ਆਲਮ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਲਾਕ॥੫੧॥੪॥
column5: Please take off the veil from your face, because the world is lying dead (longing to see Your face). (51) (4)

column2: ਐ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਦਰਗਹਿ ਤੂ ਸ਼ਫ਼ਾ-ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਿ ਆਲਮ ਅਸਤ
column5: Your heavenly dust blesses the suffering world with healthiness, kindly

column2: ਰਹਿਮੇ ਬਿਕੁਨ ਬਹਾਲਿ ਗਰੀਬਾਨਿ ਦਰਦਨਾਕ॥੫੧॥੫॥
column5: Be merciful on the painful conditions of these poor strangers. (51) (5)

column2: ਦੁਨਿਆ-ਸਤ ਕਾਂ ਖ਼ਰਾਬ ਕੁਨਿ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਅਸਤ
column5: It is this world that destroys both the universes,

column2: ਦਾਰਾ ਬਖ਼ਾਕ ਰਫ਼ਤਾ ਓ ਕਾਰੂੰ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਲਾਕ॥੫੧॥੬॥
column5: Kings like blood-thirsty Daaraa were amalgamated with the dust and Kaaroon-like brave men were killed all in the avarice for this world. (51) (6)

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਮ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾ ਬੇ ਤੂ ਗੁਹਰ ਬਾਰ ਮੀ ਸ਼ਵਦ
column5: Goyaa says, Without you, O Guru! My eyes always keep dropping pearls like tears (in)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਮਿਸਾਲਿ ਦਾਨਾ ਕਿ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੋਸ਼ਾ-ਹਾਇ ਤਾਕ॥੫੧॥੭॥
column5: The same manner that grapes keep falling from their bunches of vines. (51) (7)

column2: ਐ ਕਮਾਲਿ ਤੋ ਕਮਾਲਸਤੋ ਕਮਾਲਸਤੋ ਕਮਾਲ
column5: Your marvels and feats of skill are perfect, in fact perfection of perfections,

column2: ਐ ਜਮਾਲਿ ਤੋ ਅਸ ਜਮਾਲਸਤੋ ਜਮਾਲਸਤੋ ਜਮਾਲ॥੫੨॥੧॥
column5: Your beauty is the queen of beauty, you are the beauty of beauties. (52) (1)

column2: ਆੈ ਕਿ: ਨਜ਼ਦੀਕੀ ਤੋ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਰੱਗ ਵ ਆਲਮ ਹੈਰਾਂ
column5: My vein of breath is nearest to your ire; this completely surprises my doctor.

column2: ਯਾਰਿ ਮਾ ਈਂ ਚਿਆਂ ਖ਼ਿਆਲਸਤੋ ਖ਼ਿਆਲਸਤੋ ਖ਼ਿਆਲ॥੫੨॥੨॥
column5: Still, what thoughts we nurture about our self, O what thoughts we cherish! (52) (2)

column2: ਮਨ ਨਦਾਨਮ ਕਿ ਕੁਦਾਮਮ ਕਿ ਕੁਦਾਮਮ ਕਿ ਕੁਦਾਮਮ
column5: Goyaa says, I do not know who I am, nor how I am,

column2: ਬੰਦਾਇ-ਊ ਏਮ ਵਾਊ ਹਾਫ਼ਿਜ਼ਿ ਮਨ ਦਰ ਰਮਾ ਹਾਲ॥੫੨॥੩॥
column5: I only know that I am the slave (creation) and prot?g? of the Almighty and that it is Him only who is my protector everywhere.? (52) (3)

column2: ਦਿਲਿ ਮਨ ਫ਼ਾਰਗ਼, ਵ ਦਰ ਕੂਏ ਤੋ, ਪਰਵਾਜ਼ ਕੁਨਦ,
column5: My heart and soul while snapping all its bondages flies to your street,

column2: ਗਰ ਜ਼ਿ ਰਾਹਿ ਕਰਮਿ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼, ਬ-ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ੀ ਪਰੋ ਬਾਲ॥੫੨॥੪॥
column5: It is your blessing that makes my wings to spread for this flight. (52) (4)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਹਾਲ ਬਜੁਜ਼ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਦਮ ਨ-ਜ਼ਨਦ
column5: The devotees of Akaalpurakh who have mastered the self do not utter any word other that His Naam from their mouth,

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਸ਼ ਹਮਾ ਆਵਾਜ਼ ਬਵਦ ਕੀਲੋ ਮਕਾਲ॥੫੨॥੫॥
column5: For them, any thing other than His meditation is just a farce and a meaningless debate. (52) (5)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲਿ ਮਾ ਬੰਦਗੀਅਤ ਫ਼ਰਮਾਇਦਾ
column5: My perfect Guru directs everyone to meditate the kaalpurakh,

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਕਾਲ ਮੁਬਾਰਿਕ ਕਿ ਕੁਨਦ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਹਾਲ॥੫੨॥੬॥
column5: Wonderful! How blessed is that word or expression that makes us His ardent adherent and leads to vanquishing the self. (52) (6)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ: ਗੋਇਦ ਤੌ ਚਿ: ਬਾਸ਼ੀ, ਵ ਚਿ: ਗੋਇਦ ਜੁਜ਼ਿ ਤੋ,
column5: Goyaa says, Every body is asking me, who are you? And what can I call you!

column2: ਗਸ਼ਤ ਹੈਰਾਂ ਹਮਾ ਆਲਮ ਹਮਾ ਦਰ ਐਨਿ ਜਮਾਲ॥੫੨॥੭॥
column5: The world is in the grip of perceptive eddy and everyone is searching for your majesty.? (52) (7)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਾਜ਼ਿਰ ਅਸਤ ਦਰ ਹਮਾ ਹਾਲ
column5: When Waaheguru is Omnipresent to shield us in all predicaments,

column2: ਤੂ ਚਿਰਾ ਮੀ ਜ਼ਨੀ ਦਿਗਰ ਪਰੋ ਬਾਲ॥੫੩॥੧॥
column5: Why are you then wasting your time in making other (useless) efforts? (53) (1)

column2: ਹਮਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਗੋ ਦਿਗਰ ਮਗੋ ਐ ਜਾਂ
column5: You should praise the Lord, O my heart and soul! Do not utter any other word,

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਕਾਲ ਬਾਸ਼ ਵ ਬੰਦਾਇ ਹਾਲ॥੫੩॥੨॥
column5: You should become a meditator of His Naam, and should become a true devotee of the Master. (53) (2)

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਦਮੇ ਕਿ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ
column5: A moment spent in an activity except in the remembrance of Waaheguru,

column2: ਈਂ ਜ਼ਵਾਲ ਅਸਤ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਅਹਿਲਿ ਕਮਾਲ॥੫੩॥੩॥
column5: In the eyes of noble souls, that is a complete waste and downfall. (53) (3)

column2: ਮਾ ਸਿਵਾ ਨੀਸਤ ਹਰ ਕੁਜਾ ਬੀਨੀ
column5: Wherever you see, there is nothing but Him,

column2: ਤੂ ਚਿਰਾ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਲੀ ਦਰ ਐਨਿ ਵਸਾਲ॥੫੩॥੪॥
column5: Then, why are you so negligent (in remembering Him) when the meeting with Him is so obvious and clear? (53) (4)

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਮਗੋ ਗੋਯਾ
column5: Goyaa! You should not utter any other word except the Naam of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਕਿ ਦਿਗਰ ਪੂਚ ਹਸਤ ਕੀਲੋ ਕਾਲ॥੫੩॥੫॥
column5: Because, every other discourse is absolutely frivolous, hollow and baseless. (53) (5)

column2: ਮਾ ਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਹਰ ਬੰਦਾਇ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਫ਼ਹਿਮੀਦਾ ਏਮ
column5: Goyaa says, I have recognized every human being created by God as God Himself,

column2: ਖ਼ੋਸ਼ੀਤਨ ਰਾ ਬੰਦਾਇ ਈਂ ਬੰਦਾਹਾ ਫ਼ਹਿਮੀਦਾ ਏਮ॥੫੪॥੧॥
column5: And, I consider myself a slave (servant) of all these slaves of Truth. (54) (1)

column2: ਮਰਦੁਮਾਨਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਾ ਰਾ ਏਹਤਿਆਜਿ ਸੁਰਮਾ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: My eyelids do not need any type of collyrium for existence,

column2: ਬਸਕਿ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਰਾਹਿ ਮਰਦੁਮ ਤੂਤੀਆ ਫ਼ਹਿਮੀਦਾ ਏਮ॥੫੪॥੨॥
column5: Because, I have always considered the dust of the path traversed by God's men as the apt collyrium.? (54) (2)

column2: ਹਰ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਸਰ ਬਰ ਜ਼ਮੀਂ ਦਾਰੇਮ ਅਜ਼ ਬਹਿਰੇ ਸਜੂਦ
column5: We prostrate our head on the ground in prayers every moment and breath,

column2: ਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ ਰੂਇ ਯਾਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਨੂਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਫ਼ਹਿਮੀਦਾ ਏਮ॥੫੪॥੩॥
column5: Because, we have considered the face of our beloved reflecting the refulgence of Almighty. (54) (3)

column2: ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹਾਂ ਰਾ ਫ਼ਕੀਰਾਂ ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹੀ ਦਾਦਾ ਅੰਦ
column5: God's holy men, the saints, have bestowed the kingdoms to the worldly kings,

column2: ਜ਼ਾਂ ਗਦਾਇ ਕੂਇ ਊ ਰਾ ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹ ਫ਼ਹਿਮੀਦਾ ਏਮ॥੫੪॥੪॥
column5: That is why I consider the noble souls (even lowly mendicants) in the street (abode) of my Beloved (Guru) as the kings (54) (4)

column2: ਮਾ ਨਮੀ ਖ਼ਾਹੇਮ ਮੁਲਕੋ ਮਾਲ ਰਾ ਗੋਯਾ ਅਜ਼ਾਂ
column5: Goyaa says, I have absolutely no desire or value for wealth and property, O Guru!

column2: ਸਾਇਆਇ ਜ਼ੁਲਫ਼ਿ ਤੁਰਾ ਬਾਲਿ ਹੁਮਾ ਫ਼ਹਿਮੀਦਾ ਏਮ॥੫੪॥੫॥
column5: Because, I have considered a shadow of a lock of your hair as the feather of Humaa, the Phoenix, the mythical bird whose shadow is supposed to bring luck. (54) (5)

column2: ਦਰੂੰ ਮਰਦਮੁਕਿ ਦੀਦਾ ਦਿਲਰੁਬਾ ਦੀਦਮ
column5: I have perceived the heart-abductor in the eyelids of the man of vision,

column2: ਬਹਰ ਤਰਫ਼ ਕਿ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਕਰਦਮ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ ਦੀਦਮ॥੫੫॥੧॥
column5: Then, wherever I cast a glance, I could only see my beloved Guru. (55) (1)

column2: ਬ-ਗਿਰਦਿ ਕਾਬਾ ਓ ਬੁਤਖ਼ਾਨਾ, ਹਰ ਦੋ ਗਰਦੀਦਮ,
column5: I have circumambulated both the places, the Kaabaa and the temple,

column2: ਦਿਗ਼ਰ ਨ-ਯਾਫ਼ਤਮ ਆਂ ਜਾ, ਹਮੀਂ ਤੁਰਾ ਦੀਦਮ॥੫੫॥੨॥
column5: I have not seen any one other than You anywhere. (55) (2)

column2: ਬ-ਹਰ ਕੁਜਾ ਕਿ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਕਰਦਮ ਅਜ਼ ਰਹਿ ਤਹਕੀਕ
column5: Wherever and whenever I saw with eyes of search and concentration,

column2: ਵਲੇ, ਬ-ਖ਼ਾਨਾ-ਇ ਦਿਲ, ਖ਼ਾਨਂ-ਇ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਦੀਦਮ॥੫੫॥੩॥
column5: I have always found the abode of Akaalpurakh within my heart. (55) (3)

column2: ਗਦਾਈ ਕਰਦਨਿ ਕੂਇ ਤੂ ਬਿਹ ਜ਼ਿ ਸੁਲਤਾਨੀਸਤ
column5: (The elation of) Begging in Your street, O Guru, is far better than any kingdom,

column2: ਖ਼ਿਲਾਫ਼ਤਿ ਦੋ ਜਹਾਂ ਤਰਕਿ ਮੁਦਆ ਦੀਦਮ॥੫੫॥੪॥
column5: The enjoyment I got after I gave up my vanity and self-pride, was that of being the chief of two worlds. (55) (4)

column2: ਮਰਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਰੂਜ਼ਿ ਅਜ਼ਲ ਆਮਦ ਈਂ ਨਿਦਾ ਗੋਯਾ
column5: Goyaa says, I heard the monition in my ears the very first day,

column2: ਕਿ ਇੰਤਹਾਇ ਜਹਾਂ ਰਾ ਦਰ ਇਬਤਦਾ ਦੀਦਮ॥੫੫॥੫॥
column5: That I have seen the end of the world in its beginning. (55) (5)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਦੋਸਤ ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਦੋਸਤ ਤਮੱਨਾ ਨਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ
column5: Goya says, I have no unfriendly expectations or desires from my friend and beloved,

column2: ਮਾ ਦਰਦਿ ਸਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਏਸ਼ ਮਦਾਵਾ ਨਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੬॥੧॥
column5: I am not seeking any cure even for the anguish of my mind. (56) (1)

column2: ਬੀਮਾਰ ਨਰਗਸੇਮ ਕਿ ਨਰਗਸ ਗ਼ੁਲਾਮਿ ਊ ਸਤ
column5: I am sick because of the Narcissus friend who has complete control on Narcissus itself as a slave,

column2: ਮਾ ਆਰਜ਼ੂਇ ਖ਼ਿਜ਼ਰੋ ਮਸੀਹਾ ਨਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੬॥੨॥
column5: I do not hanker either for Khizar or Messiah who could play their part as healers of this illness. (56) (2)

column2: ਹਰ ਜਾ ਕਿ ਦੀਦਾ ਏਮ ਜਮਾਲਿ ਤੂ ਦੀਦਾ ਏਮ
column5: Wherever I see, I see only the splendor of Your beauty,

column2: ਮਾ ਜੁਜ਼ ਜਮਾਲਿ ਦੂਸਤ ਤਮਾਸ਼ਾ ਨਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੬॥੩॥
column5: In fact, I do not look for any other show except the glow of my Beloved. (56) (3)

column2: ਬਾ ਯਾਰ ਹਮਦਮੇਮ ਨ ਬੀਨੇਮ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਊ
column5: When I am in the company of my beloved, I do not look at anyone else,

column2: ਮਾ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਬਰੂਇ ਕਸੇ ਵਾ ਨਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੬॥੪॥
column5: In fact, I do not even open my eyes in front of anyone else. (56) (4)

column2: ਪਰਵਾਨਾ-ਵਾਰ ਗ਼ਿਰਦਿ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਮਆ ਜਾਂ ਦਿਹੇਮ
column5: I sacrifice my life like a moth while fluttering around the oil-lamp,

column2: ਚੂ ਅੰਦਲੀਬ ਬੇਹੁਦਾ ਗ਼ੋਗ਼ਾ ਨਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੬॥੫॥
column5: But, I do not make any useless wails, screams and shrieks like a nightingale. (56) (5)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਖ਼ਮੋਸ਼ ਬਾਸ਼ ਕਿ ਸੌਦਾਇ ਇਸ਼ਕਿ ਯਾਰ
column5: Goyaa says to himself, Just keep quiet, do not utter even a word! The deal of my love for my Beloved is with my head,

column2: ਤਾਂ ਈਂ ਸਰਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਸਰਿ-ਖ਼ੁਦ ਵਾ ਨਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੬॥੬॥
column5: As long as this head is there, this deal will not be annulled. (56) (6)

column2: ਮਾ ਬਾ-ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ-ਏਮ
column5: ?I always spend my life's time in His remembrance; this life is meaningful only so long as we love the Truth,

column2: ਦਾਇਮ ਅਜ਼ ਇਹਸਾਨਿ ਊ ਸ਼ਰਮੰਿਦਾ-ਏਮ॥੫੭॥੧॥
column5: And, I am chagrined but eternally grateful of the enormous obligations and kindnesses bestowed on me by my Master.? (57) (1)

column2: ਖ਼ੁਦ-ਨਮਾ ਰਾ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਮਨਜ਼ੂਰ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: A self centered egoist does not accept nor believe in meditation,

column2: ਊ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾਂ ਸਾਹਿਬੋ ਮਾ ਬੰਦਾ-ਏਮ॥੫੭॥੨॥
column5: However, Akaalpurakh is always the Master and we, the worldly earthlings, His slaves for ever. (57) (2)

column2: ਦਰ ਵਜੂਦਿ ਖ਼ਾਕੀਆਂ ਪਾਕੀ ਅਜ਼ੋਸਤ
column5: These earthen dolls, human beings, have been made sacred only because of Him, since His own image abides in all of them,

column2: ਮਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਪਾਕ ਰਾ ਬੀਨਿੰਦਾ-ਏਮ॥੫੭॥੩॥
column5: And, I have perceived the All-Protective Lord, and remain imbued in His remembrance. (57) (3)

column2: ਮਾ ਬਪਾਇ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਸਰ ਅਫ਼ਗੰਦਾ-ਏਮ
column5: I have placed my head at the lotus feet of my great King Master,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਦਸਤ ਰਾ ਅਫ਼ਸ਼ਾਂਦਾ-ਏਮ॥੫੭॥੪॥
column5: And, I have washed my hands off from both the worlds, this and the next. (57) (4)

column2: ਨੀਸਤ ਦਰ ਹਰ ਚਸ਼ਮ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਨੂਰਿ ਊ
column5: There is nothing else except his refulgence in everyone's eye,

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਜੋਇੰਦਾ-ਏਮ॥੫੭॥੫॥
column5: That is why, I have always sought the company of saintly persons. (57) (5)

column2: ਮਾ ਚੂ ਜ਼ੱਰਾਇ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਪਾਇ ਊ ਸ਼ੁਦੇਮ
column5: Goyaa says, I have become a particle of the dust under His feet,

column2: ਤਾ ਬਦਾਮਨ ਦਸਤਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਅਫ਼ਗੰਦਾ ਏਮ॥੫੭॥੬॥
column5: Since I have got hold of the strings of His robe surrendered myself and sought and got His shield. (57) (6)

column2: ਕੀਸਤ ਗੋਯਾ ਜ਼ਾਕਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: Goyaa asks, Who is Goyaa? meditator of the Naam of kaalpurakh,

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦ ਜਹਾਂ ਰਖ਼ਸ਼ੰਦਾ ਏਮ॥੫੭॥੭॥
column5: That is the reason that he is shining in this world like the sun. (57) (7)

column2: ਮਾ ਬੰਦਾਇ ਇਸ਼ਕੇਮ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਰਾ ਨਾ-ਸ਼ਨਾਸੇਮ
column5: Goyaa says, I am a man of love and devotion; I do not recognize God;

column2: ਦੁਸ਼ਨਾਮ ਨ ਦਾਨੇਮ ਦੁਆ ਰਾ ਨਾ-ਸ਼ਨਾਸੇਮ॥੫੮॥੧॥
column5: I do not know blatant vulgar abuses and do not perceive blessings. (58) (1)

column2: ਆਸ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਾਇ ਆਨੇਮ ਕਿ ਆਸ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਾਇ ਮਾ ਹਸਤ
column5: Goyaa says, I am madly in love with my Beloved who is also captivated by me,

column2: ਮਾ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਨ ਦਾਨੇਮ ਓ ਗਦਾ ਰਾ ਨਾ-ਸ਼ਨਾਸੇਮ॥੫੮॥੨॥
column5: I do not give any credence to a king nor do I recognize a beggar. (58) (2)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਗੈਰਿ ਤੂ ਕਸ ਨੀਸਤ ਬਤਹਿਕੀਕ ਦਰੀਂਜ਼ਾ
column5: Goyaa says, The fact is that, in reality, after seeking and denouncing, there is no one else except You everywhere;

column2: ਈਂ ਤੱਫ਼ਰਕਾਇ ਮਾ ਓ ਸ਼ੁਮਾ ਰਾ ਨਾ-ਸ਼ਨਾਸੇਮ॥੫੮॥੩॥
column5: Therefore I do not recognize any barriers between you and me. (58) (3)

column2: ਸਰ ਪਾ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਪਾ ਸਰ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਦਰ ਰਾਹਿ ਮੁਹੱਬਤ
column5: In the self-annhilating path of love, one gets so enamored that the head becomes the feet and the feet the head in oneness;

column2: ਗੋਇਮ ਵ ਲੇਕਨ ਸਰੋ ਪਾ ਰਾ ਨ-ਸ਼ਨਾਸੇਮ॥੫੮॥੪॥
column5: This cliche is often repeated; However, we do not distinguish the difference between the roles of head and feet. (58) (4)

column2: ਮਾ ਨੀਜ਼ ਚੂ ਗੋਯਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਅਜ਼ਲ ਮਸਤ ਈਸਤਮ
column5: Drunk with ecstacy, we too, like Goyaa, have been derelict since the beginning of time,

column2: ਈਂ ਕਾਇਦਾਇ ਜ਼ੁਹਦੋ ਰਿਆ ਰਾ ਨਾ-ਸ਼ਨਾਸੇਮ॥੫੮॥੫॥
column5: We have been completely oblivious to the modus operandi of meditation or fakery. (58) (5)

column2: ਹਰ ਗਾਹ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਬਜਾਨਿਬਿ ਦਿਲਦਾਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ
column5: Whenever we open our eyes to look towards our Beloved Guru,

column2: ਦਰਿਆਇ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਚਸ਼ਮ ਗੁਹਰ-ਬਾਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੯॥੧॥
column5: Then the pearl-showering river-like eyes start streaming down with tears. (59) (1)

column2: ਹਰ ਜਾ ਕਿ ਦੀਦਾਏਮ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਯਾਰ ਦੀਦਾਏਮ
column5: Goyaa says, Wherever I have looked, I see only the face of my Beloved,

column2: ਮਾ ਕੈ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਬਜਾਨਿਬਿ ਅਗ਼ਯਾਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੯॥੨॥
column5: When did I ever have looked at a stranger, someone other than the Akaalpurakh Himself? (59) (2)

column2: ਜ਼ਾਹਿਦ ਮਰਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਦੀਦਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੂਬਾਂ ਮਨਆ ਮਕੁਨ
column5: O meditating saint! Please do not forbid me from looking at beautiful objects;

column2: ਮਾ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਬਸੂਇ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਯਾਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੯॥੩॥
column5: Because, I do not dare look at anyone else other than my true and loving Friend. (59) (3)

column2: ਮਾ ਜੁਜ਼ ਹਦੀਸਿ ਰੂਇ ਤੂ ਕੂਤੇ ਨਾ ਖ਼ੁਰਦਾਏਮ
column5: Goyaa says, I have never taken any other food stimulent except the discourse about Your lovely face,

column2: ਦਰ ਰਾਹਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਤਕਰਾਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੯॥੪॥
column5: While treading the path of love and affection, this has been enough, and I have been persistently asserting this. (59) (4)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਯਾਰ ਕਿ ਮਖ਼ਮੂਰ ਗਸ਼ਤਾਏਮ
column5: Goyaa says, I am inebriated with the intoxicating looks of my Beloved,

column2: ਕੈ ਖ਼ਾਹਸ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਰਾਬਿ ਪੁਰ ਅਸਰਾਰ ਮੀ ਕੁਨੇਮ॥੫੯॥੫॥
column5: Then, why should I ever long for a sip of mysterious alcoholic drink? (59) (5)

column2: ਨਮੀ ਗੁਜੰਦ ਬਚਸ਼ਮਮ ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਸ਼ਾਹਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ-ਪਸੰਦਿ ਮਨ
column5: Nothing permeates into my eyes except the king of my own choice;

column2: ਬਚਸ਼ਮਮ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਨਿਸ਼ਸਤ ਆਂ ਕਾਮਤਿ ਬਖ਼ਤਿ ਬੁਲੰਦਿ ਮਨ॥੬੦॥੧॥
column5: His tall and well-built God-given stature has become comely to my eyes. (60) (1)

column2: ਤਮਾਮੀ ਮੁਰਦਾਹਾ ਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਤਬੱਸੁਮ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਮੀ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ
column5: Goyaa says, He, the Guru, revives dead bodies into life with his smile,

column2: ਚੂ ਰੇਜ਼ਦ ਆਬ ਹੈਵਾਂ ਅਜ਼ ਦਹਾਨਿ ਗੁੰਚਾ-ਖ਼ੰਦਿ-ਮਨ॥੬੦॥੨॥
column5: When he showers elixir-like expressions from his blossoming close-lipped bud-like mouth. (60) (2)

column2: ਬਰਾਇ ਦੀਦਨਿ ਤੂ ਦੀਦਾਅਮ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਚਸ਼ਮਾਇ ਕੌਸ਼ਰ
column5: My eyes have become a source of an eternal fountain longing to have your glimpse;

column2: ਬਿਆ ਜਾਨਾਂ ਕਿ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਿ ਤੂ ਜਾਨਿ ਦਰਦ-ਮੰਦ ਮਨ॥੬੦॥੩॥
column5: Come my Beloved! My pitiable suffering sad life is willing to sacrifice itself for you. (60) (3)

column2: ਅਗਰ ਬੀਨੀ ਦਰੂਨਿ ਮਨ ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਕੁਜਾ ਯਾਬੀ
column5: If you, my Guru, would ever care to look deep into my heart, you will not find anything other than you there;

column2: ਕਿ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਤੂੰ ਨਬੂਦ ਦਰੂਨਿ ਬੰਦ ਬੰਦਿ ਮਨ॥੬੦॥੪॥
column5: Because, there is not even a mention of anyone except you in each and every limb of my body and every drop of my blood. (60) (4)

column2: ਮਨਮ ਯੱਕ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਗਿਲ ਗੋਯਾ ਦਰੂਨਮ ਨੂਰਿ ਓ ਲਾਮਅ
column5: Gayaa says, I am a just a fistful of dust, but my inner self is bright and satiated with the glow of eternal light of his rays,

column2: ਬਗ਼ਰਦਿਸ਼ ਦਾਇਮਾ ਗਰਦਦ ਦਿਲਿ ਪੁਰ ਹੋਸ਼ਮੰਦਿ ਮਨ॥੬੦॥੫॥
column5: Therefore, my alert and sane mind always echoes that message. (60) (5)

column2: ਬੇਵਫ਼ਾ ਨੀਸਤ ਕਸੇ ਗਰ ਤੂ ਵਫ਼ਾਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ
column5: Goyaa says, If you become faithful, then no one would betray you,

column2: ਵਕਤ ਆਨਸਤ ਕਿ ਬਰ ਵਕਤ ਖ਼ਬਰਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੧॥੧॥
column5: The time has come that you should be alert of this truth on time. (61) (1)

column2: ਜਾਂ ਅਗਰ ਹਸਤ ਸਿਰਿ ਕਦਮਿ ਜਾਨਾਂ ਕੁਨ
column5: If you are alive, then offer your heart as a sacrifice for His lotus feet,

column2: ਦਿਲ ਬ-ਦਿਲਦਾਰ ਬਿਦਿਹ ਜ਼ਾਕਿ ਤੂ ਦਿਲਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੧॥੨॥
column5: Present your heart and mind to your Beloved, so that you, yourself, would become a beloved. (61) (2)

column2: ਮੰਜ਼ਿਲਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਦਰਾਜ਼ ਅਸਤ ਬ-ਪਾ ਨਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ
column5: The journey of love and devotion is extremely long and arduous; it cannot be traversed by walking on foot,

column2: ਸਰ ਕਦਮ ਸਾਜ਼ ਕਿ ਤਾ ਦਰ ਰਹਿ ਆਂ ਯਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੧॥੩॥
column5: We should walk making our head as our feet, so that we can travel on the path towards our Beloved and the trek can be completed. (61) (3)

column2: ਗੁਫ਼ਤਗੂਇ ਹਮਾ ਕਸ ਦਰ ਖ਼ੋਰਿ ਇਦਰਾਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਅਸਤ
column5: The conversation of each one of us is based upon our perception and knowledge,

column2: ਲਭ ਫ਼ਰੋਬੰਦ ਕਿ ਤਾ ਮਹਿਰਮਿ ਅਸਰਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੧॥੪॥
column5: But you should keep your lips sealed so that you can realize and appreciate the truth about His mysteries. (61) (4)

column2: ਮੀ ਫ਼ਰੋਸ਼ਦ ਦਿਲਿ ਦੀਵਾਨਾਇ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਰਾ ਗੋਯਾ
column5: Goyaa says, I am presenting my infatuated mind for sale on the hope that

column2: ਬ-ਉਮੀਦਿ ਕਰਮਿ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਖ਼ਰੀਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੧॥੫॥
column5: You, the Guru, with your benevolence, might become its purchaser. (61) (5)

column2: ਸਾਕੀ ਮਰਾ ਤੋ ਜੁੱਰਾ-ਇ ਜਾਂ ਇਸਤਿਆਕ ਦੇਹ
column5: O bartender! Kindly put my beloved in the cup of life; the urge to survive,

column2: ਤਾ ਰੂਏ ਤੇ ਬੀਨਮ, ਦੂਰੀ ਫ਼ਰਾਕ ਦੇਹ॥੬੨॥੧॥
column5: So that I may live to see my beloved's face when I am free from separation. (62) (1)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਰ ਤਰਫ਼ ਬੀਨਮ, ਚੂੰ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਤੁਰਾ ਮੁਦਾਮ
column5: I look for your glimpse in every direction, but in vain

column2: ਬਾ ਦਿਲ ਮਰਾ ਖ਼ਲਾਸੀ, ਅਜ਼ ਅਫ਼ਤਰਾਕ ਦੇਹ॥੬੨॥੨॥
column5: O heart! Kindly release me from life so that I can surrender before separation sets in. (62) (2)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਬੇ ਤੋ ਹੇਚ ਨੇਸਤ ਚੂੰ ਬੀਨੇਮ ਬ-ਹਰ ਕੁਜਾਸਤ
column5: Everything is futile and every place is void without you, this is so everywhere,

column2: ਤਾ ਦੀਦਹ ਓ ਦਿਲਿ ਮਰਾ, ਤੋ ਇੰਤਫ਼ਾਕ ਦੇਹ॥੬੨॥੩॥
column5: Grant me the unity for my worldly heart and eyes that I can see you. (62) (3)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਸਾਫ਼ ਗਸ਼ਤ ਆਈਨਾ-ਇ ਦਿਲ ਅਜ਼ ਸਵਾਦਿ ਗ਼ਮ
column5: Scrape off the dross of sorrow from the mirror of my heart,

column2: ਬਾ ਵਸਲ ਖ਼ੁਦ-ਨਮਾਈ, ਰਿਹਾਈ ਜ਼ਿ ਬਾਂਕ ਦੇਹ॥੬੨॥੪॥
column5: So that I see only your reflection in it and with it, the terror of separation is ended (62) (4)

column2: ਗੋਇਆ ਬ-ਹਰ ਕੁਜਾ ਕਿ ਬ-ਬੀਨਮ ਜਮਾਲਿ ਤੋ
column5: Goyaa says, I should be able to see only you and your splendid colors,

column2: ਤਾ ਦਿਲਿ ਮਰਾ ਖ਼ਲਾਸੀਏ, ਅਜ਼ ਦਰਦਨਾਕ ਦੇਹ॥੬੨॥੫॥
column5: I seek release from tis bondage and the pain of separation. (62 ) (5)

column2: ਬੇਵਫ਼ਾ ਨੇਸਤ ਕਸੇ, ਗਰ ਤੂ ਵਫ਼ਾਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ
column5: If you believe in yourself, then no one shall be a non-believer or faithless,

column2: ਵਕਤ ਆਂਸੂ ਕਿ: ਬਰ ਵਕਤ, ਖ਼ਬਰਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੩॥੧॥
column5: Times are such that they will demand alertness every moment. (63) (1)

column2: ਜ਼ਾਂ ਅਗਰ ਹਸਤ, ਨਸਾਰਿ ਕਦਮਿ ਜਾਨਾਂ ਕੁਨ
column5: If you have life in you, you should sacrifice it at the feet of the beloved,

column2: ਦਿਲ ਬ-ਦਿਲਦਾਰ ਬਦਿਹ, ਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ: ਦਿਲਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੩॥੨॥
column5: O heart! You should offer yourself uninhibitingly to your beloved so that you would also be loved. (63) (2)

column2: ਮੰਜ਼ਲਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਦਰਾਜ਼ ਅਸਤ ਬਾ-ਪਾ ਨ-ਤਤਵਾਂ ਰਫ਼ਤ
column5: Destination of love is too far and long; it cannot be reached using the feet,

column2: ਸਰ ਕਦਮ ਸਾਜ਼, ਕਿ: ਤਾ ਦਰ ਰਾਹਿ ਆਂ ਯਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੩॥੩॥
column5: Sacrifice your head, make it your feet before you embark on the road to your beloved. (63) (3)

column2: ਗੁਫ਼ਤਗੂਏ ਹਮਾ ਕਸ ਦਰ ਇਦਰਾਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਅਸਤ
column5: Everyone indulges into conversation according to his wits,

column2: ਲਬ ਫ਼ਿਰੋਬੰਦ ਕਿ: ਤਾ ਮਹਰਮਿ ਇਸਰਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੩॥੪॥
column5: However, you should keep your lips tightly closed if you are anxious to share the secrets of the beloved. (63) (4)

column2: ਮੇ ਫ਼ਰੋਸ਼ਦ ਦਿਲਿ ਦੀਵਾਨਾ-ਇ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਰਾ, ਗੋਇਆ
column5: Goyaa says, I want to sell myself and my insane heart to my beloved,

column2: ਬਾ ਉਮੀਦਿ ਕਰਮਿ ਆਂ, ਕਿ: ਖ਼ਰੀਦਾਰ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੬੩॥੫॥
column5: With my faith in his grace and mercy, I am sure he will find it worthy of buying. (63) (5)

column2: ਰੁਬਾਈਆਂ
column5: 4-Line Verses

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸ ਜ਼ਿ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਤੂ ਕਦਮ ਅਜ਼ ਸਰ ਸਾਖ਼ਤ
column5: Everyone, with his zeal for you, started to walk on his head,

column2: ਬਰ ਨਹੁ ਤਬਕ ਚਰਖ਼ਿ ਅਲਮ ਸਰ ਅਫ਼ਰਾਖ਼ਤ
column5: And, he was able to pitch his flag over the nine skies,

column2: ਸ਼ੁਦ ਆਮਦਨਸ਼ ਮੁਬਾਰਿਕ ਰਫ਼ਤਨ ਹਮ
column5: Blessed is his coming to this world, and equally blessed is his departure,

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਆਂ ਕਸ ਕਿ ਰਾਹਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਬਿਸ਼ਨਾਖ਼ਤ॥੧॥
column5: Goyaa says, The one who has perceived the Lord. (1)

column2: ਕੂਰ ਅਸਤ ਹਰ ਆਂ ਦੀਦਾ ਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਨਭਸ਼ਨਾਖ਼ਤ
column5: Every such eye that did not recognize (the existence) of Akaalpurakh, can be considered as blind,

column2: ਈਂ ਉਮਰਿ ਗਿਰਾਂ ਮਾਯਾ ਬ-ਗ਼ਫ਼ਲਤ ਦਰਬਾਖ਼ਤ
column5: He wasted this precious life in negligence and carelessness,

column2: ਊ ਗਿਰੀਆਂ ਕੁਨਾਂ ਆਮਦ ਬ-ਹਸਰਤ ਮੁਰਦ
column5: He came (to this world) crying and passed away taking all his yearnings and unfulfilled hopes with him,

column2: ਅਫ਼ਸੋਸ ਦਰੀਂ ਆਮਦ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਕਾਰੇ ਨਭਸਾਖ਼ਤ॥੨॥
column5: Alas! He was unable to better his disposition in this cycle of birth and death. (2)

column2: ਈਂ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਤੂ ਖ਼ਾਨਾ ਦਾਰਿ ਜਾਨਾਨਸਤ
column5: This eye of yours is the abode of the Beloved virtuous friend,

column2: ਈਂ ਤਖ਼ਤਿ ਵਜੂਦਿ ਮਸਨਦਿ ਸੁਲਤਾਨਸਤ
column5: Your personality is the seat of His royal throne,

column2: ਹਰ ਬੂਅਲਹਵਸੇ ਬਸੂਇ ਊ ਰਾਹ ਨ ਬੁਰਦ
column5: Anyone immersed in greed and lust cannot reach the powerful Almighty,

column2: ਕਿ ਈਂ ਰਾਹ ਤਅੱਲਕਿ ਮੰਜ਼ਲਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਸਤ॥੩॥
column5: Because this path is meant for the brave and the valiant devotees of Waaheguru. (3)

column2: ਹਰ ਦਿਲ ਕਿ ਬਰਾਹਿ ਰਾਸਤ ਜਾਨਾਂ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: Every such heart and mind that spontaneously (by acquiring the virtues of) became the divine Beloved,

column2: ਤਹਿਕੀਕ ਬਿਦਾਂ ਕਿ ਐਨਿ ਜਾਨਾਂ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: Rest assured! That he became the embodiment of Akaalpurakh Himself,

column2: ਯੱਕ ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਫ਼ੈਜ਼ਿ ਰਹਿਮਤਸ਼ ਖ਼ਾਲੀ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: Not even a particle of dust is then without his benevolence and blessing,

column2: ਨੱਕਾਸ ਦਰੂਨਿ ਨਕਸ਼ ਪਿਨਹਾਂ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਅਸਤ॥੪॥
column5: And, the Sculptor-Painter is camouflaging Himself behind His colors. (4)

column2: ਈਂ ਆਮਦੋ ਰਫ਼ਤ ਜੁਜ਼ ਦਮੇ ਬੇਸ਼ ਨਬੂਦ
column5: This coming and departing in this world is no more than a moment,

column2: ਹਰ ਜਾ ਕਿ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਕੁਨੇਮ ਜੁਜ਼ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਨਬੂਦ
column5: Wherever we run our vision and see around, we see only our own images,

column2: ਮਾਂ ਜਾਨਿਬਿ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਚੂੰ ਂਨਿਗਾਹ ਬਿਕੁਨੇਮ
column5: How can we ever dare to look towards anyone else?

column2: ਚੂੰ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਤੂ ਹੀਚ ਕਸੇ ਪਸੋ ਪੇਸ਼ ਨਬੂਦ॥੫॥
column5: Because, there is no one else (except Waaheguru) in the front or rear who is supporting us. (5)

column2: ਹਰ ਬੰਦਾ ਕੂ ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ
column5: Every such person who is a seeker of the Divinity,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਜਹਾਂ ਰੁਤਬਾ-ਅੰਸ਼ ਊਲਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ
column5: Has a highest rank and status in both the worlds;

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਦੋ ਜਹਾਂ ਰਾ ਬ-ਜੌਏ ਬਿ-ਸਤਾਨੰਦ
column5: Goyaa says, They can acquire both the worlds in exchange for just a grain of barley,

column2: ਮਜਨੂੰਨਿ ਤੂ ਕੈ ਆਸ਼ਕਿ ਲੈਲਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੬॥
column5: When and how would I, Your beloved (like Majnoo was) become Your lover (like Lailaa)? (6)

column2: ਦਰ ਦਹਿਰ ਕਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਆਮਦਾ ਅੰਦ
column5: When men of God manifest themselves in this world,

column2: ਬਰ ਗ਼ੁਮ-ਸ਼ੁਦਗਾਨਿ ਰਹਿਨੁਮਾ ਆਮਦਾ ਅੰਦ
column5: They come as leaders to put the strayed people on to the right path;

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਅਗਰ ਈਂ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਤੂ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਾਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: Goyaa says, If your eye is desirous of the Almighty,

column2: ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਨੁਮਾ ਆਮਦਾ ਅੰਦ॥੭॥
column5: Then realize that the noble souls of God come to this earth only to reveal Him. (7)

column2: ਦਰ ਮਜ਼ਹਬਿ ਮਾ ਗ਼ੈਰ-ਪਰਸਤੀ ਨ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: People of our faith do not worship anyone else (other than Akaalpurakh),

column2: ਸਰ ਤਾ ਬਕਦਮ ਬਹੋਸ਼ ਓ ਮਸਤੀ ਨ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: They are always mindful and alert and never act or behave wantonly,

column2: ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਲਿ ਨਸ਼ਵੰਦ ਯਕ ਦਮ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: They are not negligent of the remembrance of Waaheguru even for one moment/breath,

column2: ਦੀਗਰ ਸੁਖ਼ਨ ਅਜ਼ ਬੁਲੰਦੋ ਪਸਤੀ ਨ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੮॥
column5: Additionally, they do not get involved in topics relating to higher or lower classes of the society. (8)

column2: ਯੱਕ ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਅਗਰ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਇਲਾਹੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ
column5: If one has even the slightest inkling of fondness for the Lord,

column2: ਬਿਹਤਰ ਕਿ ਹਜ਼ਾਰ ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ
column5: Then, that is far superior to thousands of kingdoms,

column2: ਗੋਯਾ-ਸਤ ਗ਼ੁਲਾਮਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼
column5: Goyaa says, I am the slave of my true Guru,

column2: ਈਂ ਖ਼ਤ ਨ ਮੁਹਤਾਜਿ ਗਵਾਹੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ॥੯॥
column5: And this written assertion does not need any witnesses. (9)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸ ਬ-ਜਹਾ ਨਸ਼ਵੋ ਨੁਮਾ ਮੀ ਖ਼ਾਹਦ
column5: Every human being in this world likes to progressively prosper and professionally advance,

column2: ਅਸਪੋ ਸ਼ੁਤਰੋ ਫ਼ੀਲੋ ਤਿਲਾ ਮੀ ਖ਼ਾਹਦ
column5: He/She has strong desire to acquire horses, camels, elephants and gold;

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸ ਜ਼ਿ ਬਰਾਇ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਚੀਜ਼ੇ ਮੀ ਖ਼ਾਹਦ
column5: Every one always longs to have something or the other for himself;

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਮੀ ਖ਼ਾਹਦ॥੧੦॥
column5: However, Goyaa says, I always supplicate to the Lord to bless me only with His remembrance. (10)

column2: ਪੁਰ ਗਸ਼ਤਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਸਰ ਤਾ ਬ-ਕਦਮ ਨੂਰ-ਉਲ-ਨੂਰ
column5: Goyaa says that His Guru is satiated with radiance and splendor from head to feet;

column2: ਆਈਨਾ ਕਿ ਦਰ ਵੈਨ ਬਵਦ ਹੀਚ ਕਸੂਰ
column5: He is a mirror that has no cracks;

column2: ਤਹਿਕੀਕ ਬਿਦਾਂ ਜ਼ਿ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲਾਂ ਦੂਰ ਬਵਦ
column5: Rest assured: That he shuns away from the careless and the negligent;

column2: ਊ ਦਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਕਰਦਾ ਜ਼ਹੂਰ॥੧੧॥
column5: And he manifests only in the hearts of holy and pious persons. (11)

column2: ਈਂ ਉਮਰਿ ਗਿਰਾਂ-ਮਾਯਾ ਕਿ ਬਰਬਾਦ ਸ਼ਵਦ
column5: This priceless life that is wasted for nothing,

column2: ਈਂ ਖ਼ਾਨਾਇ ਵੀਰਾਂ ਬ-ਚਿਹ ਆਬਾਦ ਸ਼ਵਦ
column5: And how can this desolate home be rehabilitated and settled?

column2: ਤਾ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਨਦਿਹਦ ਦਸਤ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ
column5: Goyaa says, (This can happen) Only when a complete Guru stands by your side and helps; And this may be the only way that

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਦਿਲਿ ਗ਼ਮਗੀਨ ਤੂ ਚੂੰ ਸ਼ਾਦ ਬਵਦ॥੧੨॥
column5: My heart filled with worries and griefs can be elevated to elation? (12)

column2: ਦਿਲਿ ਜ਼ਾਲਮਿ ਬ-ਕਸਦਿ ਕੁਸ਼ਤਨਿ ਮਾ-ਸਤ
column5: The fiendish has the intention to destroy us,

column2: ਦਿਲਿ ਮਜ਼ਲੂਮਿ ਮਨ ਬਸੂਇ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਸਤ
column5: On the other hand, my vulnerable mind is expecting help and relief from the Almighty;

column2: ਊ ਦਰੀਂ ਫ਼ਿਕਰ ਤਾਂ ਬਮਾ ਚਿਹ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: The brute is concerned as to what should He do with him?

column2: ਮਾ ਦਰੀਂ ਫ਼ਿਕਰ ਤਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਚਿਹ ਕੁਨਦ॥੧੩॥
column5: And, we are concerned as to how does the Almighty respond to our plea? (13)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਉਮਰ ਆਂ ਚਿਹ ਮਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾ ਏਮ
column5: What did we gain from this priceless life?

column2: ਦਰ ਹਰ ਦੋ-ਜਹਾਂ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾ ਏਮ
column5: We were able to achieve the remembrance of the Omnipotent in both the worlds;

column2: ਈਂ ਹਸਤੀਏ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ ਬਲਾ ਬੂਦ ਅਜ਼ੀਮ
column5: This personality of ours was a huge monster,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤੇਮ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾ ਏਮ॥੧੪॥
column5: When we gave up our ego and vanity, we attained the Ultimate, the Lord. (14)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਦਰਿ ਤੂ ਤੂਤੀਆ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾਏਮ
column5: We obtained collyrium from the dust of Your door;

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਆਂ ਨਸ਼ਵੋ ਨੁਮਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾਏਮ
column5: Because of which we gained the prosperity and progress;

column2: ਮਾ ਸਿਜਦਾ ਬਰ ਰੂਇ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਦੀਗਰ ਨਭਕੁਨੇਮ
column5: We have never bowed before anyone other than You,

column2: ਦਰ ਖ਼ਾਨਾਇ ਦਿਲ ਨਕਸ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾਏਮ॥੧੫॥
column5: We have discovered the symptoms of the Omnipresent within the abode of our heart. (15)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਖ਼ਬਰ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾਏਮ
column5: Goyaa says, I have been able to get the inkling about the kaalpurakh from His remembrance,

column2: ਈਂ ਜਾਮਿ ਲਬਾ-ਲਬ ਅਜ਼ ਕੁਜਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾਏਮ
column5: Otherwise, how could I have obtained this full-to-the-brim cup of devotion and affection?

column2: ਜੁਜ਼ ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਹੱਕ ਨਸੀਬਿ ਹਰ ਕਸ ਨ ਬਵਦ
column5: Except the seeker of Lord, no one else is destined to have

column2: ਈਂ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਨਾਯਾਬ ਕਿ ਮਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾਏਮ॥੧੬॥
column5: This valuable treasure that I have had the good fortunate to obtain. (16)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਤਾ ਕੈ ਦਰੀਂ ਸਰਾਏ ਮਾਦੂਅਮ
column5: Goyaa says, How long are you going to stay in this perishable world,

column2: ਗਾਹੇ ਲਾਜ਼ਮ ਸ਼ਵਦ ਵ ਗਾਹੇ ਮਲਜ਼ੂਮ
column5: That sometimes becomes so essential and other times so assigned?

column2: ਤਾ ਕੈ ਚੂ ਸਗਾਂ ਬਰ ਉਸਤਖ਼ਾਂ ਜੰਗ ਕੁਨੇਮ
column5: How long are we going to fight over bones like dogs ?

column2: ਦੁਨਿਆ ਮਾਲੂਅਮ ਅਹਿਲਿ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਮਾਲੂਅਮ॥੧੭॥
column5: We all know about this world and these worldly people, (how selfish and merciless they are). (17)

column2: ਗੋਯਾ ਅਗਰ ਆਂ ਜਮਾਲ ਦੀਦਨ ਦਾਰੀ
column5: Goyaa says, If you desire to see the splendor and glory of Waaheguru?

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਹਵਸ ਮੈਲਿ ਰਮੀਦਨ ਦਾਰੀ
column5: If you are trying to shun away from the sin of your greed and lust and have the stomach for meidtation?

column2: ਜ਼ੀਂ ਦੀਦਾ ਮਬੀਂ ਕਿ ਹਜ਼ਾਬ ਸਤ ਤੁਰਾ
column5: Then you should not see with these overt and ostensible eyes, because they are the obstacle for you;

column2: ਬੇ-ਦੀਦਾ ਬਿਬੀਂ ਹਰ ਆਂ ਚਿਹ ਦੀਦਨ ਦਾਰੀ॥੧੮॥
column5: In fact, you should try to introspect without your eyes whatever you want to see. (And you would be successful) (18)

column2: ਮੌਜੂਦ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਸਤ ਤੂ ਕਿਰਾ ਮੀ ਜੋਈ
column5: Akaalpurakh is Omnipresent. Then whom are you searching for?

column2: ਮਕਸੂਦ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਸਤ ਤੂ ਕੁਜਾ ਮੀ ਪੋਈ
column5: A meeting with the Benevolent is the aim of our life; where are you straying?

column2: ਈਂ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਜਹਾਂ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਤੁਸਤ
column5: Both these worlds are the symbols of your mastery and control;

column2: ਯਾਅਨੀ ਸੁਖ਼ਨ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ਬਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਮੀ ਗੋਈ॥੧੯॥
column5: That is, you are speaking the word of God through your own tongue. (19)

column2: ਮਬਰ ਐ ਬਾਦ ਖ਼ਾਕਮ ਅਜ਼ ਦਰਿ ਦੂਸਤ
column5: O wind! Do not blow away my dust from the door of my Beloved,

column2: ਦੁਸ਼ਮਨਮ ਸਰਜ਼ਨਸ਼ ਕੁਨਦ ਕਿ ਹਰ ਜਾਈਸਤ॥੧॥
column5: Otherwise, the adversary will slander (the claim) that He happens to be everywhere. (1)

column2: ਨੀਸਤ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਆਂ ਸਨਮ ਦਰ ਪਰਦਾਇ ਦੈਰੋ ਹਰਮ
column5: There is no one else other than the Waaheguru inside Kaabaa or the temple;

column2: ਕੈ ਬਵਦ ਆਤਿਸ਼ਿ ਦੋ ਰੰਗ ਅਜ਼ ਇਖ਼ਤਲਾਫ਼ਿ ਸੰਗ ਹਾ॥੨॥
column5: How can the fire assume two different colors due to differences in the basic structure and make-up of stones? (2)

column2: ਆਸਮਾਂ ਸਿਜਦਾ ਕੁਨਦ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਜ਼ਮੀਂ ਕਿ ਬਰ ਊ
column5: The sky is bowing before the earth,

column2: ਯੱਕ ਦੋ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਅਜ਼ ਪੈਏ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਬਿਨਸ਼ੀਨੰਦ॥੩॥
column5: Because of the fact, devotees of Akaalpurakh squat here for a moment or two in His meditation. (3)

column2: ਬਜ਼ੇਰਿ ਸਾਇਆਇ ਤੂਬਾ ਮੁਰਾਦ ਹਾ ਯਾਬੀ
column5: Under the shade of Kalap tree, one can have all his wishes fulfilled,

column2: ਬਜ਼ੇਰ ਸਾਇਆ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਯਾਬੀ॥੪॥
column5: However, under the shade (protection) of men of God, the saintly persons, one can attain God Himself. (4)

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਨਾਮਾ
column5: ZINDAGEE NAAMAA

column2: ਆਂ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਵੰਦਿ ਜ਼ਮੀਨੋ ਆਸਮਾਂ
column5: The Akaalpurakh is the Master of the earth and the skies,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਿ ਵਜੂਦਿ ਇਨਸੋ ਜਾਂ॥੧॥
column5: He is the one who bestows life to human beings and other living beings. (1)

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਰਾਹਸ਼ ਤੂਤੀਯਾਇ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਮਾਸਤ
column5: The dust on the path of Waaheguru serves like a collyrium to our eyes.

column2: ਆਬਰੂ ਅਫ਼ਜ਼ਾਇ ਹਰ ਸ਼ਾਹੋ ਗਦਾ ਸਤ॥੨॥
column5: In fact, He is the one who elevates the honor and esteem of every king and every saintly soul. (2)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿਹ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਦਾਯਮਾ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਊ
column5: Anyone who lives his life under a constant remembrance of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਰ ਦਮ ਬਵਦ ਇਰਸ਼ਾਦਿ ਊ॥੩॥
column5: Will always prompt and motivate others for the meditation of the Almighty. (3)

column2: ਗਰ ਤੂ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਬਾਸ਼ੀ ਮੁਦਾਮ
column5: If you can constantly and always stay imbued in Akaalpurakh's meditation,

column2: ਮੀ ਸ਼ਵੀ ਐ ਜਾਨਿ ਮਨ ਮਰਦਿ ਤਮਾਮ॥੪॥
column5: Then, O my heart and soul! You can become a complete and perfect person. (4)

column2: ਆਫਤਾਬਿ ਹਸਤ ਪਿਨਹਾਂ ਜ਼ੇਰਿ ਅਬਰ
column5: He, the Akaalpurakh, like the sun, is hidden behind the clouds of the material world,

column2: ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰ ਅਜ਼ ਅਬਰੋ ਨੁਮਾ ਰੁਖਿ ਹਮਚੂ ਬਦਰ॥੫॥
column5: Goyaa says, Kindly come out of the clouds and show me Your full-moon-like face. (5)

column2: ਈਂ ਤਨਤ ਅਬਰੇਸਤ ਦਰ ਵੈ ਆਫਤਾਬ
column5: This body of yours is like a cloud under which is hidden the Sun (God),

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਮੀਦਾਂ ਹਮੀਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਸਵਾਬ॥੬॥
column5: Remember to engage yourself in the divine devotion because this is the only purpose (fruit) of this life. (6)

column2: ਹਰਕਿ ਵਾਕਿਫ਼ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਅਜ਼ ਅਸਰਾਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: Whosoever has become cognizant of the mysteries of Waaheguru,

column2: ਹਰ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਜੁਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਨ ਦਾਰਦ ਮੁਦਆ॥੭॥
column5: He has no other purpose except to remember Him every moment of his life. (7)

column2: ਕਹ ਚਿਹ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਯਾਦਿ ਆਂ ਯਜ਼ਦਾਨਿ ਪਾਕ
column5: What is the truth? It is the memory of the Almighty that is the stark truth;

column2: ਕੈ ਬਿਦਾਨਦ ਕਦਰਿ ਊ ਹਰ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ॥੮॥
column5: In fact, how can a fistful of dirt or dust, the human body, appreciate His real value? (8)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਨੇਕਾਂ ਅਗਰ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਨਸੀਬ
column5: If you have the good fortune to have the company and association of the noble persons,

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਯਾਬੀ ਐ ਹਬੀਬ॥੯॥
column5: Then my friend! You would have attained eternal wealth. (9)

column2: ਦੌਲਤ ਅੰਦਰ ਖ਼ਿਦਮਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਉਸਤ
column5: This (God given) wealth is meant to be used in the service of His creations, the people;

column2: ਹਰਿ ਗਦਾ ਓ ਪਾਦਸ਼ਾਹ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਿ ਉਸਤ॥੧੦॥
column5: Every beggar and emperor is willing to sacrifice himself for it, the true wealth. (10)

column2: ਖ਼ੂਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਗੀਰ ਐ ਬ੍ਰਾਦਰ ਖ਼ੂਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: O brother! You should acquire the traits of those who constantly remember the Beneficent;

column2: ਦਾਯਮਾ ਮੀ ਗਰਦ ਗਿਰਦਿ ਕੂਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੧॥
column5: And, you should circle around the (street) locality they reside in again and again (so that you may get an opportunity to be blessed with their company.) (11)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿਹ ਗਿਰਦਿ ਕੂਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਗਰਦੀਦ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: If anyone keeps going around the streets of these noble souls,

column2: ਦਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਹਮ ਚੂ ਮਿਹਰੋ ਬਦਰ ਤਾਫ਼ਤ॥੧੨॥
column5: He would have attained the light and radiance of the sun and the moon in both the worlds. (12)

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਬੰਦਗੀ
column5: (We must realize) that meditation is an eternal treasure;

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀ ਕੁਨ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਕੁਨ ਬੰਦਗੀ॥੧੩॥
column5: Therefore, we should engage ourselves in meditation, worship and prayers before the Almighty. (13)

column2: ਦਰ ਲਿਬਾਸਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਸ਼ਾਹੀ ਤੁਰਾਹਸਤ
column5: The entire kingdom (of the world) is encompassed in the remembrance of (the Naam of) Waaheguru;

column2: ਦੌਲਤੇ ਅਜ਼ ਮਾਹ ਤਾ ਮਾਹੀ ਤੁਰਾਹਸਤ॥੧੪॥
column5: And, it is only His realm extending from the moon to the sun. (14)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿਹ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਅਜ਼ੂ ਨਾਦਾਂ ਬਵਦ
column5: Anyone who is oblivious and unmindful of (the existence of) Akaalpurakh, consider him as an idiot;

column2: ਗਰ ਗਦਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਵਗਰ ਸੁਲਤਾਂ ਬਵਦ॥੧੫॥
column5: No matter whether he is a beggar or the emperor king. (15)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਅਜ਼ ਹਮਾ ਬਾਲਾ ਤਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: The love of God is the loftiest of all the traits for us,

column2: ਸਾਯਾਇ ਊ ਬਰ ਸਰਿ ਮਾ ਅਫ਼ਸਰ ਅਸਤ॥੧੬॥
column5: And His shadow (on our heads) is like a tiara on our heads. (16)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਮਾਅਨੀਏ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: Devotion for Akaalpurakh is considered as Aremembrance of Him,

column2: ਕਾਂ ਤਲਿਸਮਿ ਚਸ਼ਮ ਮਾ ਰਾ ਕੀਮੀਆ ਸਤ॥੧੭॥
column5: Because, his enchanting glance (towards us) is like a healing medicine for all of us. (17)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਇ ਜਾਨਿ ਮਾ-ਸਤ
column5: The love of Waaheguru is the life of our heart and soul,

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਊ ਸਰਮਾਯਾਇ ਈਮਾਨਿ ਮਾ-ਸਤ॥੧੮॥
column5: And, meditation and remembrance of His Naam are the principle assets of our faith and religion. (18)

column2: ਰੂਜ਼ਿ ਜੁਮਆ ਮੋਮਨਾਨਿ ਪਾਕਬਾਜ਼
column5: Chaste and pious minded Muslims

column2: ਗਿਰਦ ਮੀ ਆਇੰਦ ਅਜ਼ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਨਿਮਾਜ਼॥੧੯॥
column5: Get together on Friday for their religious prayers. (19)

column2: ਹਮਚੁਨਾਂ ਦਰ ਮਜ਼ਹਬਿ ਮਾ ਸਾਧ ਸੰਗ
column5: In the same way, God's devotees in my religion come together in congregations of pious saints,

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਮੁਹੱਬਤ ਬਾ-ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਦਾਰੰਦ ਰੰਗ॥੨੦॥
column5: And have pleasant time getting elated in their love for Akaalpurakh. (20)

column2: ਗਿਰਦ ਮੀ ਆਇੰਦ ਦਰ ਮਾਹੇ ਦੋ ਬਾਰ
column5: They assemble at least twice a month

column2: ਬਹਿਰਿ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਖ਼ਾਸਾਇ ਪਰਵਰਦਗਾਰ॥੨੧॥
column5: In special remembrance of the Almighty. (21)

column2: ਆਂ ਹਜੂਮਿ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਕਿ ਅਜ਼ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: That assembly is blessed that is held only for remembering Akaalpurakh;

column2: ਆਂ ਹਜੂਮਿ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਕਿ ਅਜ਼ ਦਫ਼ਾਇ ਬਲਾ-ਸਤ॥੨੨॥
column5: That assembly is blessed that is held to dispel all our mental and physical troubles. (22)

column2: ਆਂ ਹਜੂਮਿ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਕਿ ਅਜ਼ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਯਾਦਿ ਊ-ਸਤ
column5: That congregation is fortunate that is held in commemoration of Waaheguru's (Naam);

column2: ਆਂ ਹਜੂਮਿ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਬੁਨਿਆਦਿ ਊ-ਸਤ॥੨੩॥
column5: That congregation is blessed that has its foundations only on Truth. (23)

column2: ਆਂ ਹਜੂਮਿ ਬਦ ਕਿ ਸ਼ੈਤਾਨੀ ਬਵਦ
column5: That group of persons is evil and dissolute where Satan/Devil is playing its role;

column2: ਆਕਬਤ ਅਜ਼ ਵੈ ਪਸ਼ੇਮਾਨੀ ਬਵਦ॥੨੪॥
column5: Such a group is defiled that lends itself to future repentance and pennitence. (24)

column2: ਈਂ ਜਹਾਨੋ ਆਂ ਜਹਾਂ ਅਫ਼ਸਾਨਾ ਈਸਤ
column5: The story of both the worlds, this and the next, is a fable,

column2: ਈਨੋ ਆਂ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ਿਰਮਨਸ਼ ਯੱਕ ਦਾਨਾ ਈਸਤ॥੨੫॥
column5: Because, these two are just a grain out of Akaalpurakh's total heaps of produce. (25)

column2: ਈਂ ਜਹਾਨੋ ਆਂ ਜਹਾਂ ਫ਼ੁਰਮਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: These two worlds are under the (constant) command of the true Waaheguru,

column2: ਔਲੀਆ ਓ ਅਬੀਆ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ॥੨੬॥
column5: And, the divine messengers and prophets are willing to sacrifice themselves for Him. (26)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਕਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ
column5: Any one who become firm practitioner of meditation of (Naam of) Akaalpurakh

column2: ਤਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਕਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ॥੨੭॥
column5: As long as the Entity exists, he, too, becomes immortal. (27)

column2: ਈਂ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਜ਼ੱਰਾਇ ਅਜ਼ ਨੂਰਿ ਊਸਤ
column5: Both these worlds are just a ray of the radiance and splendor of Waaheguru,

column2: ਮਿਹਰੋ ਮਾਹ ਮਸ਼ਅਲ-ਕਸ਼ਿ ਮਜ਼ਦੂਰਿ ਊਸਤ॥੨੮॥
column5: The moon and the sun, both serve Him as His torch-bearers. (28)

column2: ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਹਮੀਂ ਦਰਦਿ-ਸਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: The achievements in this world are nothing but a constant and severe headache,

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਗਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਜ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਗਾਓ ਖ਼ਰ ਅਸਤ॥੨੯॥
column5: Anyone who becomes oblivious of the Trinity is either a bull or a donkey. (29)

column2: ਗ਼ਫ਼ਲਤ ਅਜ਼ ਵੈ ਯੱਕ ਜ਼ਮਾਂ ਸਦ ਮਰਗ ਦਾਂ
column5: To be careless, negligent, sluggish and apathetic of the memory of Akaalpurakh even for a moment is equivalent to hundreds of deaths.

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਯਾਦ ਅਸਤ ਨਿਜ਼ਦਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਂ॥੩੦॥
column5: For those enlightened and knowledgeable ones of Waaheguru, His meditation and remembrance is indeed the real life. (30)

column2: ਹਰ ਦਮੇ ਕੁ ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰਦ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਊ
column5: Every moment that is spent in remembering Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਕਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ ਬੁਨਿਯਾਦਿ ਊ॥੩੧॥
column5: Builds permanent foundations with Him. (31)

column2: ਹਰ ਸਰੇ ਕੂ ਸਿਜਦਾਇ ਸੁਬਹਾ ਨਾ ਕਰਦ
column5: Whosoever prostrates before Waaheguru every morning

column2: ਹੱਕ ਮਰ ਊ ਰਾ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਈਮਾਨ ਕਰਦ॥੩੨॥
column5: Waaheguru makes him firm (believer) in contentment and faith. (32)

column2: ਸਰ ਬਰਾਇ ਸਿਜਦਾ ਪੈਦਾ ਕਰਦਾ ਅੰਦ
column5: The 'head' was created only to bow before the Almighty;

column2: ਦਰਦਿ ਹਰ ਸਰ ਰਾ ਮਦਾਵਾ ਕਰਦਾ ਅੰਦ॥੩੩॥
column5: And this is the cure for all headaches in (of) this world. (33)

column2: ਪਸ ਤੁਰਾ ਬਾਇਦ ਕੁਨੀ ਹਰਦਮ ਸਜੂਦ
column5: Therefore, we should always keep bowing our heads before the Beneficent;

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਵੈ ਯੱਕ ਜ਼ਮਾਂ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਨ ਬੂਦ॥੩੪॥
column5: In fact, someone cognizant of Akaalpurakh will not be derelict even for a moment in remembering Him. (34)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਚਿਰਾ ਆਕਿਲ ਬਵਦ
column5: How can anyone who has been oblivious in remembering Him be called wise and sane?

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਗਸ਼ਤ ਊ ਜ਼ਾਹਿਲ ਬਵਦ॥੩੫॥
column5: Anyone who has been negligent of Him should be considered an idiot and uncouth. (35)

column2: ਮਰਦਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਫ਼ਾਰਿਗ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਚੁਨੋ ਚਿਰਾ-ਸਤ
column5: A knowledgeable and enlightened person does not get bogged down in verbal rhetorics,

column2: ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਉਮਰਸ਼ ਹਮੀਂ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ॥੩੬॥
column5: The achievement of his whole life is just the memory of Akaalpurakh. (36)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਈਮਾਂ ਹਮਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਹਮਾਂ
column5: The only person who is honest and religious-minded is the one

column2: ਕੂ ਨ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਅਜ਼ ਵੈ ਯੱਕ ਜ਼ਮਾਂ॥੩੭॥
column5: Who does not become derelict even for one moment in memorializing the Omnipotent. (37)

column2: ਕੁਫ਼ਰ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਸ਼ੁਦਨ
column5: To be non-cognizant about Akaalpurakh and getting allured and enamored by the

column2: ਬਰ ਲਿਬਾਸਿ ਦੁਨਯਵੀ ਮਾਇਲ ਸ਼ੁਦਨ॥੩੮॥

column2: ਹੱਕ ਕਿ ਹਫ਼ਤਾਦੋ ਦੋ ਮਿੱਲਤ ਆਫਰੀਦ
column5: Akaalpurakh created seventy two communities,

column2: ਫ਼ਿਰਕਾਇ ਨਾਜੀ ਅਜ਼ੀਹਾਂ ਬਰ ਗੁਜ਼ੀਦ॥੮੫॥
column5: Out of which, He designated the Naajee community as the most elite. (85)

column2: ਫ਼ਿਰਕਾਇ ਨਾਜੀ ਬਿਦਾਂ ਬੇ-ਇਸ਼ਤਬਾਹ
column5: We should consider the Naajee (who is considered above and beyond the cycles of transmigration) community, without any doubt,

column2: ਹਸਤ ਹਫ਼ਤਾਦੋ ਦੋ ਮਿੱਲਤ ਰਾ ਪਨਾਹ॥੮੬॥
column5: As the shelter for the seventy two clans. (86)

column2: ਮਰਦਮਾਨਸ਼ ਹਰ ਯਕੇ ਪਾਕੀਜ਼ਾ ਤਰ
column5: Every member of this Najee community is sacred;

column2: ਖ਼ੂਬ-ਰੂ ਓ ਖ਼ੂਬ-ਖ਼ੂ ਓ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼-ਸੀਅਰ॥੮੭॥
column5: Beautiful and handsome, well mannered with a noble disposition. (87)

column2: ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਜੁਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਮਨਜ਼ੂਰ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: To these folks, nothing else but the remembrance of Akaalpurakh is acceptable;

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਦਸਤੂਰ ਨੀਸਤ॥੮੮॥
column5: And, they do not have any tradition or mannerism other than the recitation of the words of prayer. (88)

column2: ਮੀਚਕਦ ਅਜ਼ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਕੰਦੋ ਨਬਾਤ
column5: Utter sweetness oozes out of their words and conversation,

column2: ਬਾਰਦ ਅਜ਼ ਹਰ ਮੂਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਆਬਿ ਹਯਾਤ॥੮੯॥
column5: And, divine elixir is showered from every hair of theirs. (89)

column2: ਫ਼ਾਰਿਗ਼ ਅੰਦ ਅਜ਼ ਬੁਗ਼ਜ਼ੋ ਕੀਨਾ ਓ ਜ਼ਿ ਹਸਦ
column5: They are above and beyond any form of jealousy, hostility or enmity;

column2: ਬਰ ਨਮੀ-ਆਇਦ ਅਜ਼ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਫ਼ਿਅਲਿ ਬਦ॥੯੦॥
column5: They never commit any sinful acts. (90)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਇੱਜ਼ਤੋ ਹੁਰਮਤ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: They extend respect and honor to every one;

column2: ਮੁਫ਼ਲਸੇ ਰਾ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਦੌਲਤ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੯੧॥
column5: And, they help poor and needy to become rich and opulent. (91)

column2: ਮੁਰਦਾ ਰਾ ਆਬਿ-ਹੈਵਾਂ ਮੀਦਿਹੰਦ
column5: They bless the dead souls with divine nectar;

column2: ਹਰ ਦਿਲੇ ਪਜ਼ਮੁਰਦਾ ਰਾ ਜਾਂ ਮੀਦਿਹੰਦ॥੯੨॥
column5: They bestow new and rejuvenated life to withered and demoralized minds. (92)

column2: ਸਬਜ਼ ਮੀਸਾਜ਼ੰਦ ਚੋਬਿ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ਕ ਰਾ
column5: They can transform dry wood into green twigs;

column2: ਬੂਏ ਮੀਬਖਸ਼ੰਦ ਰੰਗਿ ਮੁਸ਼ਕ ਰਾ॥੯੩॥
column5: They also can convert a stinking odor into scented musk. (93)

column2: ਜੁਮਲਾ ਅਸ਼ਰਾਫ਼ ਅੰਦ ਦਰ ਜ਼ਾਤੋ ਸਿਫ਼ਾਤ
column5: All these well-intentioned persons possess noble personal qualities;

column2: ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਜ਼ਾਤ ਅੰਦ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਹਮ ਆਨਿ ਜ਼ਾਤ॥੯੪॥
column5: They are all seekers of Waaheguru's Entity; in fact, they are just like Him (are His image). (94)

column2: ਖ਼ੂਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਇਲਮੋ ਅਦਬ ਰਾ ਮੁਜ਼ਹਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: Learning and literature emerges (spontaneously) from their behavior;

column2: ਰੂਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਜ਼ਿ ਮਿਹਰਿ ਅਨਵਰ ਅਸਤ॥੯੫॥
column5: And, their faces radiate like the glowing divinely sun. (95)

column2: ਮਿੱਲਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਕੌਮਿ ਮਸਕੀਨਾਂ ਬਵਦ
column5: Their clan consists of a group of humble, meek and gentle persons;

column2: ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਸ਼ਾਇਕਿ ਈਨਾਂ ਬਵਦ॥੯੬॥
column5: And they have devotees in both the worlds; People in both the worlds believe in them. (96)

column2: ਕੌਮਿ ਮਿਸਕੀਂ ਕੌਮਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: This group of people is the community of gentle and humble souls, a community of God's men.

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਫ਼ਾਨੀ ਵ ਊ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਕਾਸਤ॥੯੭॥
column5: Every thing that we see is destructible, but Akaalpurakh is the only One that prevails perpetually and is Imperishable. (97)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਰਾ ਅਕਸੀਰ ਕਰਦ
column5: Their company and association transformed even the dust to an effective cure.

column2: ਲੁਤਫ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਰ ਹਰ ਦਿਲੇ ਤਾਸੀਰ ਕਰਦ॥੯੮॥
column5: Their blessings effectively impressed every heart. (98)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਬ-ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਨਸ਼ੀਨਦ ਯੱਕ ਦਮੇ
column5: Anyone who enjoys their company just once even for a moment,

column2: ਰੂਜ਼ਿ ਫ਼ਰਦਾ ਰਾ ਕੁਜਾ ਦਾਰਦ ਗ਼ਮੇਂ॥੯੯॥
column5: He, then, does not have to be apprehensive about the day of reckoning. (99)

column2: ਆਂ ਚਿ ਦਰ ਸਦ-ਸਾਲਾ ਉਮਰਸ਼ ਨਭਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: A person who could not attain much in spite of hundreds of years of life,

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਹਮਚੂ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦਸ਼ ਬਿਤਾਖ਼ਤ॥੧੦੦॥
column5: Shone like a sun when he joined the company of these people. (100)

column2: ਮਾ ਕਿ ਅਜ਼ ਇਹਸਾਨਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਸ਼ਰਮਿੰਦਾ-ਏਮ
column5: We are obligated and owe them a debt of gratitude,

column2: ਬੰਦਾਇ ਇਹਸਾਨਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਬੰਦਾ ਏਮ॥੧੦੧॥
column5: We are, in fact, persons/products of their favors and kindness. (101)

column2: ਸਦ ਹਜ਼ਾਰਾਂ ਹਮਚੂ ਮਨ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: Millions like me are willing to sacrifice themselves for these nobilities;

column2: ਹਬ ਚਿ ਗੋਇਮ ਕਮ ਬਵਦ ਦਰ ਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੦੨॥
column5: No matter how much I say in their honor and praise, it will be inadequate. (102)

column2: ਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਬੀਰੂੰ ਬਵਦ ਅਜ਼ ਗੁਫ਼ਤਗੂ
column5: Their honor and appreciation is beyond any words or expression;

column2: ਜਾਮਾਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਪਾਕ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੁਸਤੋ ਸ਼ੂ॥੧੦੩॥
column5: The style (attire) of their life is cleaner and chaste than any amount of washing or rinsing. (103)

column2: ਦਾਂ ਯਕੀਂ ਤਾ ਚੰਦ ਈਂ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਬਵਦ
column5: Believe me ! How long is this world going to last? Only for a short time;

column2: ਆਖ਼ਰਿਸ਼ ਕਾਰਿ ਤੂ ਬਾ ਮੌਲਾ ਬਵਦ॥੧੦੪॥
column5: Ultimately, we have to develop and maintain a relationship with the Almighty. (104)

column2: ਪਸ ਜ਼ ਅੱਵਲ ਕੁਨ ਹਦੀਸਿ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਰਾ
column5: Now you indulge yourself in the stories and discourses of (that) King, the Waaheguru.

column2: ਪੈਰਵੀ ਕੁਨ ਹਾਦੀਏ ਈਂ ਰਾਹ ਰਾ॥੧੦੫॥
column5: And, follow the Guide who shows you the direction (of life). (105)

column2: ਤਾ ਤੂ ਹਮ ਯਾਬੀ ਮੁਰਾਦਿ ਉਮਰ ਰਾ
column5: So that your life's hopes and ambitions are fulfilled;

column2: ਲਜ਼ਤੇ ਯਾਬੀ ਜ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਕਿਬਰੀਆ॥੧੦੬॥
column5: And, you can have the pleasure of the savor of the devotion for Akaalpurakh.(106)

column2: ਜਾਹਿਲ ਆਂ-ਜਾ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ-ਦਿਲ ਮੀਸ਼ਵਦ
column5: (With His grace) even s stupid person can become an intellectual and enlightened;

column2: ਗ਼ਰਕਿ ਦਰਅਿਾਓ ਬਸਾਹਿਲ ਮੀਸ਼ਵਦ॥੧੦੭॥
column5: And, a person drowning in deep waters of a river can reach the banks. (107)

column2: ਨਾ ਕਿਸ ਆਂ ਜਾ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਸ਼ਵਦ
column5: An insignificant person can become fully enlightened,

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਹਰ ਕਿ ਰਾ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੧੦੮॥
column5: When he engages himself in the remembrance of Waaheguru. (108)

column2: ਈਂ ਅਸਬ ਤਾਜਸਤ ਬਰ ਅਫ਼ਰਾਕਿ ਕਸ
column5: A person is being adorned, as if, with a crown of learning and honor on his head,

column2: ਆਂ ਕਿ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਨੀਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਯੱਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ॥੧੦੯॥
column5: Who does not become negligent even for one moment in remembering Akaalpurakh. (109)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਨੀਸਤ ਈਂ ਦੌਲਤ ਨਸੀਬ
column5: This treasure is not in the lot of everyone;

column2: ਦਰਦਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਨੀਸਤ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਤਬੀਬ॥੧੧੦॥
column5: The cure for their pain is no one else other than the Waaheguru, the doctor. (110)

column2: ਦਾਰੂਇ ਹਰ ਦਰਦ ਰਾ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਸਤ
column5: Remembrance of Akaalpurakh is the cure-all for every malady and pain;

column2: ਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ ਦਰ ਹਰ ਹਾਲ ਹੱਕ ਦਾਰਦ ਰਵਾ-ਸਤ॥੧੧੧॥
column5: In whatever condition or state He keeps us, should be acceptable. (111)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਹਮਾ ਰਾ ਆਰਜ਼ੂ
column5: It is the wish and desire of everyone to seek a perfect Guru;

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਕਸ ਨ ਯਾਬਦ ਰਹਿ ਬਦੂ॥੧੧੨॥
column5: Without such a mentor, no one can reach the Almighty. (112)

column2: ਰਾਹ-ਰਵਾਂ ਰਾ ਰਾਹ ਬਿਸੀਆਰ ਆਮਦਾ
column5: There are several paths for the travelers to traverse;

column2: ਕਾਰਵਾਂ ਰਾ ਰਾਹ ਦਰਕਾਰ ਆਮਦਾ॥੧੧੩॥
column5: But what they need is the path of the caravan. (113)

column2: ਦਮ ਬਦਮ ਦਰ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਹਾਜ਼ਰ ਅੰਦ
column5: They are always alert and prepared for the remembrance of Akaalpurakh;

column2: ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਮਨਜ਼ੂਰੋ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਰਾ ਨਾਜ਼ਿਰ ਅੰਦ॥੧੧੪॥
column5: They are acceptable to Him and they are His observers, on-lookers and spectators. (114)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਹਮਾਂ ਬਾਂਸ਼ਦ ਹਮਾਂ
column5: A perfect Satguru is the one and the only one,

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਕਲਾਮਸ਼ ਬੂਇ ਹੱਕ ਆਦਿ ਅਯਾਂ॥੧੧੫॥
column5: Whose conversation and Gurbaanee emits the Divine fragrance. (115)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਆਇਦ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਵਾਰ
column5: Anyone who comes in front of such persons (Perfect Gurus) in humility like a dust particle,

column2: ਜ਼ੂਦ ਗਰਦਦ ਹਮਚੂ ਮਿਹਰਿ ਨੂਰ ਬਾਰ॥੧੧੬॥
column5: He, soon, becomes capable to shower radiance like that of sun. (116)

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਈਨਸਤ ਬੇ ਚੁਨੋ ਚਿਰਾ
column5: That life is worth living that, without any delay or excuses,

column2: ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰਦ ਈਂ ਉਮਰ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ॥੧੧੭॥
column5: Is spent in the memory of the Providence in this lifetime. (117)

column2: ਖ਼ੁਦ-ਪ੍ਰਸਤੀ ਕਾਰਿ ਨਾਦਾਂ ਆਮਦਾ
column5: To indulge in self-propaganda is the work of stupid people;

column2: ਹੱਕ ਪ੍ਰਸਤੀ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ ਈਮਾਂ ਆਮਦਾ॥੧੧੮॥
column5: While to get engaged in meditation is the characteristic of the faithful. (118)

column2: ਹਰ ਦਮੇ ਗ਼ਫਲਤ ਬਵਦ ਮਰਗਿ ਅਜ਼ੀਮ
column5: Negligence of every moment of not remembering Him is like a huge death;

column2: ਹੱਕ ਨਿਗਾਹ ਦਾਰਦ ਜ਼ਿ ਸ਼ੈਤਾਨਿ ਰਜ਼ੀਮ॥੧੧੯॥
column5: May God, with His eye, save us from the Satan of Hell. (119)

column2: ਆਂ ਕਿ ਰੂਜ਼ੋ ਸ਼ਬ ਬ-ਯਾਦਸ਼ ਮੁਬਤਲਾ-ਸਤ
column5: Anyone who is (constantly) imbued in remembering Him day and night,

column2: ਈਂ ਮਤਾਅ ਅੰਦਰ ਦੁਕਾਨਿ ਔਲੀਆ-ਸਤ॥੧੨੦॥
column5: (Knows very well that) This wealth, the memory of Akaalpurakh, is available only at the store (congregation) of saintly persons. (120)

column2: ਕਿਹਤਰੀਨਿ ਬੰਦਾਇ ਦਰਗਾਹਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: Even the lowest person in their court

column2: ਬਿਹਤਰ ਅਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਮਿਹਤਰਾਨਿ ਈਂ ਜਹਾਂ॥੧੨੧॥
column5: Is superior than the so called most respectable stalwarts of this world. (121)

column2: ਬਸ ਬਜ਼ੁਰਗ਼ਾਂ ਕੂ ਫ਼ਿਦਾਇ ਰਾਹਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: Many wise and experienced persons are enamored and are prepared to make sacrifices on their paths,

column2: ਸੁਰਮਾਇ ਚਸ਼ਮਮ ਜ਼ਿ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਰਾਹਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੨੨॥
column5: And, the dust of their paths is like a collyrium for my eyes. (122)

column2: ਹਮਚੁਨੀਂ ਪਿੰਦਾਰ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਰਾ ਐ ਅਜ਼ੀਜ਼
column5: You, too, my dear youngster! Consider yourself just like this,

column2: ਤਾ ਸ਼ਵੀ ਐ ਜਾਨਿ ਮਨ ਮਰਦਿ ਤਮੀਜ਼॥੧੨੩॥
column5: So that, my dear! You, too, can transform yourself into a pious and saintly person. (123)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਾਂ ਰਾ ਬੰਦਾ ਬਿਸਆਰ ਆਮਦਾ
column5: These masters, the noble souls, have numerous followers and devotees;

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਬਾ-ਬੰਦਗੀ ਕਾਰ ਆਮਦਾ॥੧੨੪॥
column5: The main task assigned to each and every one of us is only to meditate. (124)

column2: ਮਸ ਤੁਰਾ ਬਾਇਦ ਕਿ ਖ਼ਿਦਮਤਗਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: Therefore, you should become their follower and a devotee;

column2: ਬਾਸ਼ੀ ਓ ਹਰਗਿਜ਼ ਨਭਬਾਸ਼ੀ ਬਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੨੫॥
column5: But you should never be liability for them. (125)

column2: ਗਰਚਿਹ ਯਾਰੀ-ਦਿਹ ਨ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਸਾਲਕੇ-ਸਤ
column5: Even though, there is no one else without them to connect us to the Almighty,

column2: ਲੇਕ ਕਰ ਗ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਨ ਚੁਨੀਂ ਐਬੇ ਬਸੇ-ਸਤ॥੧੨੬॥
column5: Still, for them to make such a claim would be a transgression. (126)

column2: ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਰਾ ਦੀਦਮ ਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦਿ ਜਹਾਂ
column5: I realized that even a small particle became the sun for the whole world,

column2: ਸ਼ੁਦ ਜ਼ਿ ਫ਼ੈਜ਼ਿ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸਾਹਿਬ-ਦਿਲਾਂ॥੧੨੭॥
column5: With the blessings of the association with the saintly persons. (127)

column2: ਕੀਸਤ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ-ਦਿਲ ਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਿਸਨਾਸਦਸ਼
column5: Who is that person with a great heart who can recognize the Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਲਕਾਇਸ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਮੀ-ਬਾਰਦਸ਼॥੧੨੮॥
column5: And whose face (constantly) radiates His splendor ? (128)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਦ ਤੁਰਾ
column5: The company of such noble souls blesses you with the Devotion for the Lord,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਕਿਤਾਬਿ ਹੱਕ ਸਬਕ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਦ ਤੁਰਾ॥੧੨੯॥
column5: And it is their company too that gives you spiritual lessons from the holy book. (129)

column2: ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਰਾ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦਿ ਅਨਵਰ ਮੀ-ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: They, the noble souls, can transform even a small particles into a sprightly sun;

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕ ਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਮੁਨੱਵਰ ਮੀ-ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੧੩੦॥
column5: And, it is them who can sparkle even the common dust into the Light of Truth. (130)

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਤੂ ਖ਼ਾਕੀ ਵ ਦਰ ਵੈ ਨੂਰਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: Even though your eye is made of dust, still it has the Divine radiance,

column2: ਅੰਦਰੂਨਸ਼ ਚਾਰ ਸੂ ਵ ਨਹੁ ਤਬਕ॥੧੩੧॥
column5: It also contains all the four directions, east, west, south and north, and the nine heavens. (131)

column2: ਖ਼ਿਦਮਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਬੰਦਗੀਇ ਹੱਕ ਬਵਦ
column5: Any service performed for them, the saintly persons, is the worship of Waaheguru;

column2: ਕਾਂ ਕਬੂਲਿ ਕਾਦਰਿ ਮੁਤਲਿਕ ਬਵਦ॥੧੩੨॥
column5: Because they are the ones who are acceptable to the Omnipotent. (132)

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀ ਕੁਨ ਜਾਂ ਕਿ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਬੂਲ
column5: You, too, should meditate so that you be acceptable before the Akaalpurakh.

column2: ਕਦਰਿ ਊ ਰਾ ਕੈ ਬਿਦਾਨਦ ਹਰ ਜਹੂਲ॥੧੩੩॥
column5: How can any stupid person appreciate His invaluable worth. (133)

column2: ਹਸਤ ਕਾਰਿ ਰੂਜ਼ੋ ਸ਼ਬ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਊ
column5: The only task we should be engaged day and night is to remember Him;

column2: ਯੱਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਖ਼ਾਲੀ ਨਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਅਜ਼ੂ॥੧੩੪॥
column5: Not even one moment should be spared without His meditation and prayers. (134)

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਜ਼ਿ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਅੱਲਾਹ
column5: Their eyes are glistened because of His Divine glimpse,

column2: ਦਰ ਲਿਬਾਸ ਅੰਦਰ-ਗਦਾ ਓ ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹ॥੧੩੫॥
column5: They may be in the guise of a mendicant, but they are the kings. (135)

column2: ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹੀ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਊ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ
column5: Only that kingdom is considered a real kingdom that lasts for ever,

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਜਾਤਿ ਪਾਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਕਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ॥੧੩੬॥
column5: And, like the pure and chaste Nature of God, should be eternal. (136)

column2: ਰਸਮਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਆਈਨਿ ਦਰਵੇਸ਼ੀ ਬਵਦ
column5: Their custom and tradition is mostly that of mendicants;

column2: ਅਸ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਓ ਬਾਹਮਾ ਖ਼ੇਸੀ ਬਵਦ॥੧੩੭॥
column5: They are the lineage and the scions of Waaheguru, and they have intimacy and familiarity with all. (137)

column2: ਹਰ ਗਦਾ ਰਾ ਇੱਜ਼ੇ ਜਾਹੇ ਮੀਦਿਹੰਦ
column5: The Akaalpurakh blesses every ascetic with honor and status;

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਬੇ-ਇਸ਼ਤਬਾਹੀ ਮੀਦਿਹੰਦ॥੧੩੮॥
column5: Without any doubt, He also bestows (everyone) with wealth and treasures. (138)

column2: ਨਾਕਸਾਂ ਰਾ ਆਰਿਫ਼ਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: They can transform trivial and paltry persons into perfectly knowledgeable ones;

column2: ਬੇ-ਦਿਲਾਂ ਰਾ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ-ਦਿਲ ਮੀਕੁਨੰਦ॥੧੩੯॥
column5: And, the demoralized ones into courageous persons and masters of their destiny. (139)

column2: ਖ਼ੁਦ-ਪ੍ਰਸਤੀ ਅਜ਼ ਮਿਆਂ ਬਰਦਾਸ਼ਤੰਦ
column5: They eject their vanities from their inner self;

column2: ਤੁਖ਼ਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਦਰ ਕਿਸ਼ਤਿ ਦਿਲਹਾ ਕਾਸ਼ਤੰਦ॥੧੪੦॥
column5: And, they sow seeds of Truth, the Lord, in the field-like hearts of the people. (140)

column2: ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ ਰਾ ਹੀਚ ਮੀ ਦਾਨੰਦ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: They always consider themselves insignificant and lowlier than others;

column2: ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਰੂਜ਼ੋ ਸ਼ਬ ਖ਼ਾਨੰਦ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੪੧॥
column5: And, they are absorbed in the meditation of Naam of Waaheguru day and night. (141)

column2: ਤਾ ਕੁਜਾ ਔਸਾਫ਼ਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: How much can I praise the God's men, the saints and the Mahaatamaas?

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਹਜ਼ਾਰਾਂ ਗਰ ਯਕੇ ਗੋਇਮ ਰਵਾ-ਸਤ॥੧੪੨॥
column5: It would be just wonderful if I could describe even one of their thousands of virtues. (142)

column2: ਹਮਚੁਨੀਂ ਮਰਦੁਮ ਬਜੂ ਕਆਂ ਕੀਸਤੰਦ
column5: You, too, should try to find such noble persons (What kind of persons?) who are alive for ever;

column2: ਦੀਗਰਾਂ ਮੁਰਦੰਦ ਈਹਾਂ ਜ਼ੀਸਤੰਦ॥੧੪੩॥
column5: The rest of them are apparently alive but are just like dead bodies. (143)

column2: ਜ਼ੀਸਤਨ ਰਾ ਮਾਅਨੀ ਦਾਨੀ ਕਿਹ ਚੀਸਤ
column5: Do you understand the meaning of 'being alive' ?

column2: ਐ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ਾ ਉਮਰੇ ਕਿ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਸ਼ ਬਜ਼ੀਸਤ॥੧੪੪॥
column5: Only that life is worth living that is spent in remembering the Akaalpurakh. (144)

column2: ਮਰਦਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਅਜ਼ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਨਿ ਊ-ਸਤ
column5: The enlightened persons are alive only because of the knowledge of the mysteries of God's attributes;

column2: ਨਿਅਮਤਿ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਜਹਾਂ ਦਰ ਖ਼ਾਨਾਇ ਊ-ਸਤ॥੧੪੫॥
column5: (They know) that He has and can shower blessings of both of the worlds in His house. (145)

column2: ਮਾਅਨੀਏ ਈਂ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: The main purpose of this life is to (constantly) remember Akaalpurakh;

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਤੁਫ਼ੈਲਸ਼ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਜਾਨਿ ਔਲੀਆ-ਸਤ॥੧੪੬॥
column5: The saints and prophets live only with this motive. (146)

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਊ ਬਰ ਹਰ ਜ਼ਬਾਨਿ ਗੋਯਾ ਸ਼ੁਦ
column5: A mention of them is on every living tongue;

column2: ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਰਾਹਿ ਹੱਕ ਜੂਯਾ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ॥੧੪੭॥
column5: And, both the worlds are seekers of His path. (147)

column2: ਜੁਮਲਾ ਮੀ-ਖ਼ਾਨੰਦ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਜ਼ੁਲਜਲਾਲ
column5: Everyone meditates the awe-inspiring splendid Waaheguru,

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਕੀਲੋ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਫ਼ਰਖ਼ੰਦਾ ਕਾਲ॥੧੪੮॥
column5: Only then such a meditation is auspicious and such a discourse propitious. (148)

column2: ਕੀਲੋ ਕਾਲੇ ਗਰ ਬਰਾਇ ਹੱਕ ਬਵਦ
column5: If you want to converse and describe the Truth,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬਰਾਇ ਕਾਦਰਿ ਮੁਤਲਿਕ ਬਵਦ॥੧੪੯॥
column5: That is possible only to discourse the Omnipotent. (149)

column2: ਯਾਫ਼ਤ ਈਂ ਸਰਮਾਯਾਇ ਉਮਰਿ ਨਜੀਬ
column5: Such an asset and the treasure of meditation for a spiritual life

column2: ਹਸਤ ਅੰਦਰ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਨਸੀਬ॥੧੫੦॥
column5: Was blessed through association and the company they kept with the saintly persons. (150)

column2: ਆਂ ਰਵਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਦਿਗਰ ਮਨਜ਼ੂਰ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: Any such treasure is not acceptable to them, and they do not like any thing other than the truth;

column2: ਗ਼ਰਿ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਰਾਸਤੀ ਦਸਤੂਰ ਨੀਸਤ॥੧੫੧॥
column5: It is not their tradition to speak any words but the words of truth. (151)

column2: ਸਾਧ ਸੰਗਤ ਨਾਮਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਦਰ ਹਿੰਦਵੀਸਤ
column5: In Hindi language, they are called the 'Saadh Sangat',

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਤਾਰੀਫ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਐ ਮੌਲਵੀਸਤ॥੧੫੨॥
column5: O Maulvee ! All this is in their praise; and all this defines them. (152)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਵਦ ਲੁਤਫ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: The attainment of their company happens only with His blessings;

column2: ਤਾ ਨਸੀਬਿ ਕਸ ਸ਼ਵਦ ਈਂ ਰੂ-ਨਮਾ॥੧੫੩॥
column5: And, only with His grace, such persons are revealed. (153)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਈਂ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: Anyone who is fortunate enough to have obtained this eternal wealth,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਏ ਉਮਰ ਰਾ ਉਮੀਦ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ॥੧੫੪॥
column5: One may assume then that he has become full of hope for the duration of his whole life. (154)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਫ਼ਾਨੀ ਵ ਆਂ ਬਾਕੀ ਬਿਦਾਂ
column5: All these, the wealth and the life, are perishable, but they are eternal;

column2: ਜਾਮਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ ਪਾਕ ਰਾ ਸਾਕੀ ਬਿਦਾਂ॥੧੫੫॥
column5: Consider them as bartenders who are serving glasses full of divine devotion. (155)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿ ਹਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਵਦ
column5: Whatever is seemingly apparent in this world is all because of their company;

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਤਫ਼ੈਲਸ਼ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਆਬਾਦਾ ਬਵਦ॥੧੫੬॥
column5: It is their grace that we see all the habitation and prosperity here. (156)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਆਬਾਦੀ ਅਜ਼ ਲੁਤਫ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: All these habitations (of living beings) are the result of the blessings of Waaheguru;

column2: ਗ਼ਫਲਤ ਅਜ਼ ਵੈ ਯੱਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਮਰਗੋ ਜਫ਼ਾ ਸਤ॥੧੫੭॥
column5: To neglect Him even for one moment is equivalent to pain and death. (157)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਹਾਸਲਿ ਈਂ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਸਤ
column5: To achieve an association with them, the noble persons, is the cornerstone of this life;

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਈਂ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਈਂ ਬੰਦਗੀ-ਸਤ॥੧੫੮॥
column5: That is life, that is indeed life which is spent in meditating His Naam. (158)

column2: ਗਰ ਤੂ ਮੀਖ਼ਾਹੀ ਕਿ ਮਰਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਸ਼ਵੀ
column5: If you wish to become Waaheguru's true devotee,

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਿ ਊ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਮੁਤਲਿਕ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੧੫੯॥
column5: Then, you should become knowledgeable and enlightened about the perfect Entity. (159)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਕੀਮੀਆ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਤੁਰਾ
column5: Their company is like a cure-all for you;

column2: ਤਾ ਚਿਹ ਮੀਖ਼ਾਹੀ ਰਵਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਤੁਰਾ॥੧੬੦॥
column5: Then, whatever you wish shall be appropriate. (160)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਕੂ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਜਾਂ ਆਮਦੰਦ
column5: All this breathing and living world that we see

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬਰਾਇ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਆਮਦੰਦ॥੧੬੧॥
column5: Is only because of the company of the noble souls. (161)

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਏ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਜ਼ਿਫ਼ੈਜ਼ਿ ਸਹੁਬਤ ਅਸਤ
column5: The existing lives of those living beings is the result of the company of saintly persons;

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਆਇਤਿ ਪੁਰ ਰਹਿਮਤ ਅਸਤ॥੧੬੨॥
column5: And, the company of such noble persons is the proof of the kindness and compassion of Akaalpurakh. (162)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਾਇਦਸ਼
column5: Everyone, in fact, needs their company;

column2: ਤਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਦਿਲ ਅਕਦਿ ਗੁਹਰ ਬਿਕੁਸ਼ਾਇਦਸ਼॥੧੬੩॥
column5: So that they could unravel the chain of pearls (noble aspects) from their hearts. (163)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਗੰਜੀਨਾਈ ਅ ਬੇ-ਖ਼ਬਰ
column5: O naive! You are the master of priceless treasure;

column2: ਲੇਕ ਜ਼ਾਂ ਗੰਜੇ ਤੁਰਾ ਨਭਬਵਦ ਖ਼ਬਰ॥੧੬੪॥
column5: But alas! You have no realization of that hidden treasure. (164)

column2: ਕੈ ਅਜ਼ਾਂ ਗੰਜੇ ਬ-ਯਾਬੀ ਇਤਲਾਅ
column5: How can you find out that invaluable treasure

column2: ਅੰਦਰੂਨਿ ਕੁਫ਼ਲ ਚੂੰ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਮਤਾਅ॥੧੬੫॥
column5: As to what kind of wealth is hidden inside the vault. (165)

column2: ਪਸ ਤੁਰਾ ਲਾਜ਼ਿਮ ਬਵਦ ਜੂਈ ਕੁਲੀਦ
column5: Therefore, it is essential for you to strive to find the key to the treasure,

column2: ਤਾ ਬ-ਬੀਨੀ ਗੰਜਿ ਖ਼ੁਫ਼ੀਆ ਰਾ ਪਦੀਦ॥੧੬੬॥
column5: So that you can have a clear realization of this covert, mysterious and valuable repository. (166)

column2: ਕੁਫ਼ਲ ਬਿਕੁਸ਼ਾ ਅਜ਼ ਕੁਲੀਦਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: You should use the Naam of Waaheguru as the key to open this hidden wealth;

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਕਿਤਾਬਿ ਗੰਜ ਮਖ਼ਫ਼ੀ ਖ਼ਾਂ ਸਬਕ॥੧੬੭॥
column5: And, learn the lessons from the Book of this hidden treasure, the Granth. (167)

column2: ਈਂ ਕੁਲੀਦਿ ਨਾਮ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਵਦ
column5: This key is found (only) with the saintly persons,

column2: ਮਰਹਮਿ ਦਿਲਹਾਇ ਰੇਸ਼ੇ ਜਾਂ ਬਵਦ॥੧੬੮॥
column5: And, this key serves as the ointment of lacerated hearts and lives. (168)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਈਂ ਕੁਲੀਦ ਆਇਦ ਬ-ਦਸਤ
column5: Anyone who can get hold of this key

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਗੰਜੀਨਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਹਰ ਕਿ ਹਸਤ॥੧੬੯॥
column5: He may be anyone, he can become the master of this treasure. (169)

column2: ਗੰਜ ਰਾ ਚੂੰ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾ ਜੋਯਾਇ ਗੰਜ
column5: When the seeker of the treasure does find his goal,

column2: ਗਸ਼ਤ ਫ਼ਾਰਿਗ ਅਜ਼ ਹਮਾ ਤਸ਼ਵੀਸ਼ੋ ਰੰਜ॥੧੭੦॥
column5: Then consider that he has been salvaged from all worries and anxieties. (170)

column2: ਆਂ ਹਮ ਅਜ਼ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਐ ਸ਼ਫ਼ੀਕ
column5: O my friend ! That person has joined the group of (true) devotees of God,

column2: ਆਂ ਕਿ ਰਾਹੇ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ ਦਰ ਕੂਇ ਰਫ਼ੀਕ॥੧੭੧॥
column5: Who has discovered the direction to the streets of the Beloved Friend. (171)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਰਾ ਚੂੰ ਮਾਹ ਕਰਦ
column5: Their association transformed an insignificant dust particle to a shining moon.

column2: ਹਰ ਗਦਾ ਰਾ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਕਰਦ॥੧੭੨॥
column5: Again, it was their company that transformed every beggar into a king. (172)

column2: ਰਹਿਮਤਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਾਦ ਬਰ ਔਜ਼ਾਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: May Akaalpurakh bless their disposition with His grace;

column2: ਬਰ ਪਿਦਰ ਬਰ ਮਾਦਰੇ ਇਬਨਾਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੭੩॥
column5: And, also on their parents and children. (173)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਦੀਦ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਦੀਦਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: Whosoever gets a chance to see them, consider that they have seen the Almighty God;

column2: ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਗੁਲ ਅਜ਼ ਬਾਗ਼ਿ ਮੁਹੱਬਤ ਚੀਦਾ ਅਸਤ॥੧੭੪॥
column5: And that he has been able to get a glimpse of a beautiful flower out of the garden of love. (174)

column2: ਗੁਲ ਜ਼ਿ ਬਾਗ਼ਿ ਮਾਅਰਫ਼ਤ ਬਰ-ਚੀਦਨ ਅਸਤ
column5: The association with such noble persons is like taking a beautiful flower out of the garden of divine knowledge;

column2: ਦੀਦਨਿ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਰਾ ਦੀਦਨ ਅਸਤ॥੧੭੫॥
column5: And, a sight of such saints is like getting a glimpse of Akaalpurakh. (175)

column2: ਮੁਸ਼ਕਿਲ ਆਮਦ ਦੀਦਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਬਿਆਂ
column5: It is difficult to describe the 'glimpse' of Waaheguru;

column2: ਮੀਦਿਹਦ ਈਂ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਰਾ ਕੁਦਰਤ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੭੬॥
column5: His powers are reflected in the entire Nature that He has created. (176)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਤੁਫ਼ੈਲਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਰਾ ਦੀਦਾ-ਅਮ
column5: With their kindness, I have seen a glimpse of Akaalpurakh;

column2: ਗੁਲ ਜ਼ ਬਾਗ਼ਿ ਮਾਅਰਫ਼ਤ ਬਰ ਚੀਦਾ-ਅਮ॥੧੭੭॥
column5: And, with their grace, I have selected a lively flower from the Divine Garden. (177)

column2: ਦੀਦਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਮਾਅਨੀਏ ਦਾਰਦ ਸ਼ਰੀਫ਼
column5: Even to think of getting a glimpse of Akaalpurakh is indeed a sacred intention;

column2: ਮਨ ਨਿ-ਅਮ ਈਂ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਆਂ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ ਲਤੀਫ॥੧੭੮॥
column5: Goya says, I am nothing! ll this, including the above thought, is due to His abstract and mysterious Entity. (178)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਊ ਦਾਨਿਸਤ ਈਂ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਤਮਾਮ
column5: Whosoever has understood this complete message (word),

column2: ਯਾਫ਼ਤ ਊ ਆਂ ਗੰਜਿ ਮਖ਼ਫ਼ੀ ਰਾ ਮਕਾਮ॥੧੭੯॥
column5: As if he has discovered the location of the hidden treasure. (179)

column2: ਮਾਅਨੀਏ ਹੱਕ ਸੂਰਤੇ ਦਾਰਦ ਨਿਕੂ-ਸਤ
column5: The reality of Waaheguru has an extremely attractive reflection;

column2: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਹੱਕ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਊ-ਸਤ॥੧੮੦॥
column5: The picture of Akaalpurakh is (can be seen) in His own men and women, the saintly persons. (180)

column2: ਖ਼ਲਵਤਿ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਵਦ ਦਰ ਅੰਜੁਮਨ
column5: They feel they are in seclusion even when they are in the company of groups of people, the congregations;

column2: ਵਸਫ਼ਿ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਨਿ ਮਰਦੋ ਜ਼ਨ॥੧੮੧॥
column5: Praises of their glory are on the tongues of everyone. (181)

column2: ਜ਼ੀਂ ਖ਼ਬਰ ਵਾਕਿਫ਼ ਕਸੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਊ
column5: Only that person can be cognizant of this mystery,

column2: ਦਾਰਦ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਮੁਹੱਬਤ ਗ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਗ਼ੂ॥੧੮੨॥
column5: Who talks and discusses about the devotion for Akaalpurakh with enthusiasm. (182)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ-ਅਸ਼ ਗਿਰੇਬਾਂ ਗੀਰ ਸ਼ੁਦ
column5: Anyone whose enthusiastic devotion for Waaheguru becomes a necklace (garland) for his neck,

column2: ਨਾਕਸੇ ਹਮ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਤਦਬੀਰ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥੧੮੩॥
column5: Even though he, being a lowly person, becomes wise and sagacious. (183)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਮੌਲਾਯਤ ਚੂੰ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਦਸਤਗੀਰ
column5: When the enthusiasm of devotion for God becomes your supporter,

column2: ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਗਰਦਦ ਰਸ਼ਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦ ਮੁਨੀਰ॥੧੮੪॥
column5: Then even a particle of dust desires to emulate (and becomes) a bright sun. (184)

column2: ਬਸਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਮੀਬਾਰਦ ਅਜ਼ ਗ਼ੁਫ਼ਤਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: When they talk, they shower (words of) the nectar of Truth,

column2: ਦੀਦਾਹਾ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਅਜ਼ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੮੫॥
column5: With their glimpse, the eyes become more bright and soothed. (185)

column2: ਰੂਜ਼ੋ ਸ਼ਬ ਬਾਸ਼ੰਦ ਦਰ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਸ਼ ਮੁਦਾਮ
column5: They keep meditating on the Naam of Waaheguru day and night;

column2: ਦਰ ਲਿਬਾਸਿ ਦੁਨਯਵੀ ਮਰਦਿ ਤਮਾਮ॥੧੮੬॥
column5: Even in the worldly guise, living in this world, they become perfect human beings. (186)

column2: ਬਾ ਹਮਾ ਅਜ਼ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਆਜ਼ਾਦੰਦ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: With everything around them, they are independent and are immune to the effects of these material distractions;

column2: ਦਰ ਹਮਾ ਹਾਲ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਸ਼ਾਦੰਦ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੧੮੭॥
column5: They are always satisfied and pleased under the Will of Akaalpurakh. (187)

column2: ਦਰ ਲਿਬਾਸਿ ਦੁਨਯਵੀਂ ਵ ਰਸਮਿ ਦੀਂ
column5: Even they are dressed in worldly clothes, their tradition and practice is religious.

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਏਸਾਂ ਸਾਨੀਏ ਦੀਗਰ ਮਬੀਂ॥੧੮੮॥
column5: There is no one like them in this world. (188)

column2: ਹਮ ਚੁਨਾਂ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਦਾਰੰਦ ਦਸਤ
column5: They are starkly steady, firm and adept in the memory of Waaheguru,

column2: ਹੱਕ ਸ਼ਨਾਸੋ ਹੱਕ ਪਸੰਦੋ ਹੱਕ ਪ੍ਰਸਤ॥੧੮੯॥
column5: They appreciate and recognize Him, are dedicated to the Truth and also worship the Truth. (189)

column2: ਦਰ ਲਿਬਾਸਿ ਦੁਨਯਵੀ ਸਰ ਤਾ ਕਦਮ
column5: Even though they are seen clad in worldly guise from head to toe,

column2: ਬੀਨੀ ਵਾ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਨਭਬੀਨੀ ਨੀਮ ਦਮ॥੧੯੦॥
column5: You will never find them to be negligent of remembering Waaheguru even for half a moment. (190)

column2: ਆਂ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਪਾਕ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਪਾਕ ਕਰਦ
column5: The chaste Akaalpurakh transforms them into pure and holy beings,

column2: ਗਰ ਚਿਹ ਜਿਸਮਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਜ਼ਿ-ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਕਰਦ॥੧੯੧॥
column5: Even though their body is made up of just a fistful of dust. (191)

column2: ਈਂ ਵਜੂਦਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਪਾਕ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਊ-ਸਤ
column5: This human body made of dust becomes sacred with His remembrance;

column2: ਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ ਏਸ਼ਾਂ ਮਜ਼ਹਰਿ ਬੁਨਿਆਦਿ ਊ-ਸਤ॥੧੯੨॥
column5: Because it is the manifestation of the foundation (personality) bestowed by Akaalpurakh. (192)

column2: ਰਸਮਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਆਈਨਿ ਦਿਲਦਾਰੀ ਬਵਦ
column5: It is their custom to remember the Almighty;

column2: ਦਰ ਹਮਾ ਹਾਲ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਯਾਰੀ ਬਵਦ॥੧੯੩॥
column5: And, it is their tradition to always generate love and devotion for Him. (193)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਕੈ ਨਸੀਬ ਈਂ ਦੌਲਤ ਅਸਤ
column5: How can every one be blessed with such a treasure?'

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਅੰਦਰ ਸੁਹਬਤ ਅਸਤ॥੧੯੪॥
column5: This non-perishable wealth is available only through their company. (194)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਅਜ਼ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਊਸਤ
column5: All these (material goods) are the result of the blessings of their company;

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਜਹਾਂ ਦਰ ਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਊਸਤ॥੧੯੫॥
column5: And, the wealth of both the worlds is in their praise and honor. (195)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਨਫ਼ੀਆ ਬਿਸੀਆਰ ਆਵੁਰਦ
column5: An association with them is extremely profitable;

column2: ਨਖ਼ਲਿ ਜਿਸਮਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਹੱਕ ਬਾਰ ਆਵੁਰਦ॥੧੯੬॥
column5: The date-palm of the body of dust brings in the fruit of Truth. (196)

column2: ਹਮਚੁਨੀਂ ਸੁਹਬਤ ਕੁਜਾ ਬਾਜ਼ ਆਇਦਤ
column5: When would you be able to run into such (an exalted) company?

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਬਰਾਏ ਮਰਦਮੀ ਮੀ-ਸ਼ਾਇਦਤ॥੧੯੭॥
column5: Such a blessed company will bestow upon you the humaneness. (197)

column2: ਮਰਦਮੀ ਯਾਅਨੀ ਬ-ਹੱਕ ਪੈਵਸਤਨ ਅਸਤ
column5: The purpose of human life is (ultimately) to merge with the Creator;

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਸ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਹਮਾ ਵਾ ਰਿਸਤਨ ਅਸਤ॥੧੯੮॥
column5: Absence of His description and discourse is tantamount to breaking away from every one else. (198)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਦਿਲਿ ਬੰਦਾ ਬਜ਼ਿਕਰਸ਼ ਰਾਹ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: When a human being gets into the tradition of remembering Waaheguru,

column2: ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਉਮਰੋ ਦਿਲ ਆਗਾਹ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ॥੧੯੯॥
column5: He becomes conversant with the attainment of both life and soul. (199)

column2: ਕਾਰਸ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਗਰਦੂਨਿ ਗਰਦਾਂ ਦਰ ਗੂਜ਼ਰਤ
column5: He will be redeemed and set free from the attachments of this revolving world when someone breaks away his connections from it;

column2: ਬਰ ਸਰਿ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਚੂ ਮਰਦਾਂ ਦਰ ਗੁਜ਼ਰਤ॥੨੦੦॥
column5: Then, he would become detached from the material distractions like a seeker of spiritual knowledge. (200)

column2: ਈਂ ਜਹਾਨੋ ਆਂ ਜਹਾਂ ਤਹਿਸੀਂ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: He was applauded in both the worlds,

column2: ਆਂ ਕਿ ਦਿਲ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਹੱਕ ਰੰਗੀਂ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੨੦੧॥
column5: When anyone imbued his heart and soul with the remembrance of Akaalpurakh. (201)

column2: ਦਰ ਵਜੂਦਸ਼ ਆਫਤਾਬੇ ਤਾਫ਼ਤਾ
column5: The body of such a person begins to radiate like the sun,

column2: ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਦਰ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾ॥੨੦੨॥
column5: When he, in the company of saintly persons, has attained the real Truth. (202)

column2: ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਅਜ਼ ਬਸਕਿ ਰੂਜ਼ੋ ਸ਼ਬ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ
column5: He remembered the Naam of Akaalpurakh day and night,

column2: ਦਸਤਿ ਊ ਰਾ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਬਰ-ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੨੦੩॥
column5: Then only the discourses and praises of the Lord became his support. (203)

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਯਾਰੀ ਦਾਦਾ ਸ਼ੁਦ
column5: Anyone who has received the support of Akaalpurakh due to his meditation,

column2: ਖ਼ਾਨਾਇ ਵੀਰਾਂ ਜ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਆਬਾਦਾ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥੨੦੪॥
column5: Will have his deserted house, with the achievement of the Truth, rehabilitated and revived again. (204)

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਦੌਲਤੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਅਜ਼ੀਮ
column5: Meditation of God is one great treasure;

column2: ਕੈ ਬਦਸਤ ਆਇਦ ਜ਼ਿ ਗੰਜੋ ਮਾਲੋ ਸੀਮ॥੨੦੫॥
column5: How can one obtain such a magnificent treasure from the worldly riches like wealth and silver? (205)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਖਾਸਤ ਹੱਕ ਊ ਰਾ ਬਖ਼ਾਸਤ
column5: Whosoever developed a desire for (meeting with) the Lord, the Lord liked him;

column2: ਸ਼ੌੋਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਬਿਹਤਰੀਨਿ ਕੀਮੀਆ-ਸਤ॥੨੦੬॥
column5: The love and devotion for Akaalpurakh is the best cure-all panacea. (206)

column2: ਗੋਹਰਿ ਮਕਸੂਦ ਤਨ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: The deed of the main purpose of this body is only to remember Waaheguru;

column2: ਲੇਕਨ ਊ ਅੰਦਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਨਿ ਔਲੀਆ ਸਤ॥੨੦੭॥
column5: However, He always abides and manifests on the tongues of the noble souls. (207)

column2: ਪਾਰਸਾਈ ਬਿਹ ਕਿ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਵਦ
column5: That saintliness is really worthwhile if it is to seek the Truth;

column2: ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹੀ ਂਚੀਸਤ ਕਾਂ ਨਾਹੱਕ ਬਵਦ॥੨੦੮॥
column5: What is the worth of that kingdom that is useless if it is not aiming towards God. (208)

column2: ਹਰ ਦੋ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਾਕ ਅੰਦ ਰਿੰਦੋ ਪਾਰਸਾ
column5: Both the drunk and the holy person are desirous of Him;

column2: ਤਾ ਕਿਰਾ ਖ਼ਾਹਦ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਕਿਬਰੀਆ॥੨੦੯॥
column5: Let us see! Whom does He, the transcendent Akaalpurakh likes? (209)

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਤਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਬਰਾਇ ਬੰਦਗੀਸਤ
column5: A human being deserves to be called a human being only if he directs himself towards meditation;

column2: ਗੈਰ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਮਾ ਸ਼ਰਮਿੰਦਗੀਸਤ॥੨੧੦॥
column5: Without a description/word of Waaheguru, it is all humiliation. (210)

column2: ਲੇਕ ਦਰ ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰ ਕਸੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਦਰੁਸਤ
column5: However, it is apparent that only that person is on the right path,

column2: ਆਂ ਕਿ ਆਰਦ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਦਸਤ॥੨੧੧॥
column5: Who has been able to attain (or attach himself to) a perfect Guru. (211)

column2: ਦੀਨੋ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਫ਼ਰਮਾਂ-ਦਾਰ ਊ
column5: Both the Faith and the world are in complete obedience to the Almighty;

column2: ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਸ਼ਾਇਕਿ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਊ॥੨੧੨॥
column5: Both the worlds are equally desirous to get just one glimpse of Him. (212)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਰਾ ਉਲਫ਼ਤ ਜ਼ਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਵਦ
column5: Anyone who has developed deep love for the Naam of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਆਰਫ਼ਿ ਮੁਤਲਿਕ ਬਵਦ॥੨੧੩॥
column5: He becomes a perfect seeker of the divine knowledge in real terms. (213)

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਊ ਮੀ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: Seekers of Waaheguru are (actively) involved in His meditation;

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਨੇਕੋ ਮੀ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੨੧੪॥
column5: Seekers of Waaheguru transform everyone into something very attractive. (214)

column2: ਹੱਕ ਹਮਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਬਾਸ਼ੀ ਬੰਦਾਇ
column5: The truth is that you should (always strive to) become a person of God,

column2: ਬੇ-ਅਦਬ ਦਾਇਮ ਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਸ਼ਰਮੰਿਦਾਇ॥੨੧੫॥
column5: A disrespectful (apostate/atheist) person is always chagrined and ashamed before Him. (215)

column2: ਉਮਰ ਆਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਊ ਦਰ ਯਾਦ ਰਫ਼ਤ
column5: Only that life is worth living that is spent in remembering Waaheguru,

column2: ਉਮਰ ਨਾ ਬੂਦ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਬਰਬਾਦ ਰਫ਼ਤ॥੨੧੬॥
column5: What use is that life that is spent needlessly and in vain. (216)

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਪੈਦਾ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਬਰਾਏ ਬੰਦਗੀ
column5: A person is born (only) to be engaged in meditation;

column2: ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਇਲਾਜੇ ਹਸਤ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ॥੨੧੭॥
column5: In fact, religious devotion (and prayers) is a good cure to put this life in the proper perspective. (217)

column2: ਐ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ਾ ਚਸ਼ਮੇ ਕਿ ਦੀਦਾ ਰੂਇ ਦੂਸਤ
column5: How fortunate is that eye that has got a glimpse of the face of the Beloved!

column2: ਮਰਦੁਮਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਸੂਇ ਊ ਸਤ॥੨੧੮॥
column5: Eyes of the people of both the worlds are fixed towards it. (218)

column2: ਈਂ ਜਹਾਨੋ ਆਂ ਜਹਾਂ ਅਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਪੁਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: This and the other world are satiated with the Truth;

column2: ਲੇਕ ਮਰਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਬ-ਆਲਮ ਕਮਤਰ ਅਸਤ॥੨੧੯॥
column5: But devoted men of God are rare in this world. (219)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਕੂ ਬ-ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਮਰੰਗ ਸ਼ੁਦ
column5: If some one became indistinguishable with Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਵਸਫ਼ਿ ਊ ਦਰ ਮੁਲਕਿ ਰੂਮੋ ਜ਼ੰਗ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥੨੨੦॥
column5: Then his glory spreads in countries like Rome and Africa. (220)

column2: ਮਾਅਨੀਏ ਯਕਰੰਗੀ ਆਮਦ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: To get assimilated in the Entity of God is, in fact, real love for Him;

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਆਰਾਮ ਅੰਦਰ ਜ਼ੌਕਿ ਹੱਕ॥੨੨੧॥
column5: And, it is only with a real devotion for Him that one gets eternal bliss. (221)

column2: ਊ ਬਰੰਗਿ ਸਾਹਿਬੀ ਬਾ ਇੱਜ਼ੋ ਜਾਹ
column5: Under the influence of Akaalpurakh, (Accepting the Will of Waaheguru) he enjoys eclat and honors;

column2: ਮਾ ਬਾਰੰਗ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਅੰਦਰ ਪਨਾਹ॥੨੨੨॥
column5: We, under the influence of meditation, have sought his (His) shelter and refuge. (222)

column2: ਊ ਬਰੰਗਿ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਫ਼ਰਮਾਂ ਰਵਾ
column5: Accepting Waaheguru's Will, he is the king of the world and his command prevails all over;

column2: ਮਾ ਬਰੰਗਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਨਿਜ਼ਦਸ਼ ਗਦਾ॥੨੨੩॥
column5: We, under the influence of meditation, are just beggars before him. (223)

column2: ਊ ਬਰੰਗਿ ਸਾਹਿਬੀ ਦਾਰਦ ਨਜ਼ਰ
column5: He, while imbued in accepting Master's Will, keeps a close watch on us;

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਖ਼ਬਰ॥੨੨੪॥
column5: And, one can get to know him only through meditation. (224)

column2: ਉਮਰ ਹਾ ਜੋਯਾਇ ਈਂ ਦੌਲਤ ਸ਼ੁਦੰਦ
column5: They kept looking for such a treasure for ages;

column2: ਸਾਲਹਾ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਾਕਿ ਈਂ ਸੁਹਬਤ ਸ਼ੁਦੰਦ॥੨੨੫॥
column5: They were anxiously desirous of such a company for years. (225)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਜ਼ਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਨਸੀਬ
column5: Anyone who was fortunate enough to attain even an atomic particle of such a wealth,

column2: ਆਂ ਬਖ਼ੂਬੀ ਗਸ਼ਤ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦਿ ਨਜੀਬ॥੨੨੬॥
column5: He became an extraordinary sun easily and spontaneously. (226)

column2: ਗੈਰ ਊ ਯਾਅਨੀ ਜ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਗਫ਼ਲਤ ਬਵਦ
column5: The underlying meaning of living without the memory of Waaheguru is complete ignorance and naivety.

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਊ ਸਰਮਾਯਾਇ ਦੌਲਤ ਬਵਦ॥੨੨੭॥
column5: The valuable asset of the remembrance of Akaalpurakh becomes the treasure of some fortunate beings. (227)

column2: ਦੀਦਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਤਾ ਮੁਯੱਸਰ ਮੀ-ਸ਼ਵਦ
column5: One can get a glimpse of the Omnipotent only

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਂ ਤਅਸੁਰ ਮੀ-ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੨੨੮॥
column5: When one's association with noble saints become fruitful. (228)

column2: ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਦਰ ਦਿਲ ਅਗਰ ਮਾਵਾ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: If someone can abide even one word of Truth in his heart,

column2: ਦਰ ਬੁਨਿ ਹਰ ਮੂਇ ਊ ਹੱਕ ਜਾ ਕੁਨਦ॥੨੨੯॥
column5: Then, Truth but nothing but the Truth gets assimilated to the roots of each of his hair. (229)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਰਾ ਸੂਇ ਹੱਕ ਮੀ-ਆਦਰਸ਼
column5: Anyone who can direct himself towards the divine path of Waaheguru,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਰੁਖ਼ਿ ਊ ਨੂਰਿ-ਹੱਕ ਮੀ-ਬਾਰਦਸ਼॥੨੩੦॥
column5: God's glory and splendor radiates from his face. (230)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਫ਼ੈਜ ਅਜ਼ ਤੁਫ਼ੈਲਿ ਸੁਹਬਤ ਅਸਤ
column5: All this benevolence and benignity is because of them and their blessings,

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਦੌਲਤ ਅਸਤ॥੨੩੧॥
column5: The company of saintly persons (of God) is one priceless asset. (231)

column2: ਹੀਚ ਕਸ ਅਜ਼ ਹਾਲਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਆਗਾਹ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: No one really understands or appreciates the state of mind of these noble royalties;

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਓ ਮਿਹ ਰਾ ਦਰਾਂਜਾ ਰਾਹ ਨੀਸਤ॥੨੩੨॥
column5: Every Tom, Dick or Harry does not have the wherewithal to reach there (them). (232)

column2: ਦਰ ਨਜ਼ਰ ਆਇੰਦ ਚੂੰ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ ਅੱਲਾਹ
column5: In appearance, they look just like an embodiment of the specie of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਅਪਨਾਹ॥੨੩੩॥
column5: In reality, they are the refuge for everyone in both the worlds. (233)

column2: ਦਰ ਕਸਬ ਬਾਸ਼ੰਦ ਆਜ਼ਾਦ ਅਜ਼ ਕਸਬ
column5: While engaged in their professions or trades, they are still unattached and unduly engrossed in them;

column2: ਉਮਰ ਗੁਜ਼ਰਾਨੰਦ ਅੰਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਰੱਬ॥੨੩੪॥
column5: They spend their lives in remembering the Provident (day and night). (234)

column2: ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਰਾ ਚੂੰ ਮੂਰ ਬਿਸ਼ਨਾਸੰਦ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: They, the noble souls, consider themselves (out of humility) just like an ant,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਬਿਹਤਰ ਅਜ਼ ਪੀਲਿ ਦਮਾਂ॥੨੩੫॥
column5: Even though they, in fact, might be more powerful than a ferocious and dangerous elephant. (235)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿ ਮੀ-ਬੀਨੀ ਹਮਾ ਹੈਰਾਨਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: Whatever you are seeing in this world is simply astonished and confounded about them;

column2: ਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਿਹਤਰ ਬਵਦ ਅਜ਼ ਇਮਤਿਹਾਂ॥੨੩੬॥
column5: Their splendor and aura is far superior than even that of the examinations. (236)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਰਮ
column5: The company of the true devotees of Waaheguru is a great boon;

column2: ਦੌਲਤੇ ਕਆਂ ਰਾ ਨਭਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਹੀਚ ਗ਼ਮ॥੨੩੭॥
column5: Such a wealth and virtuosity does not suffer from any anxiety or grief. (237)

column2: ਖ਼ੁਦ ਬਜ਼ੁਰਗੋ ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਨਿਸ਼ਸਤ
column5: They, themselves, are elevated, mature and blessed; anyone who happens to be bestowed with their company;

column2: ਊ ਬਜ਼ੁਰਗੀ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ ਤਾਂ ਹਰ ਜਾ ਕਿ ਹਸਤ॥੨੩੮॥
column5: He, too, becomes elevated, mature and blessed, and receives laurels everywhere. (238)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਕੂ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਰਾ ਬਿਸ਼ਨਾਖ਼ਤਾ
column5: Anyone who has recognized his own reality;

column2: ਦਰ ਤਰੀਕਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਪਰਦਾਖ਼ਤਾ॥੨੩੯॥
column5: Consider that he has adopted the mode or the method of meditation. (239)

column2: ਈਂ ਜ਼ਮੀਨੋ ਆਸਮਾਂ ਪੁਰ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: This earth and the skies are saturated with (the creations of ) God,

column2: ਆਲਮੇ ਹਰ ਸੂ ਦਵਾਂ ਕਆਂ ਸ਼ਹਿ ਕੁਜਾ-ਸਤ॥੨੪੦॥
column5: But this world keeps wandering and straying in all directions to find out as to where He is. (240)

column2: ਦੀਦਾ ਬਰ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਹੱਕ ਗਰ ਮੁਬਤਲਾ-ਸਤ
column5: If you can aim your eyes fixedly on a glimpse of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਮੀ ਬੀਨੀ ਬਚਸ਼ਮਤ ਹੱਕ-ਨੁਮਾ-ਸਤ॥੨੪੧॥
column5: Then, whatever you see will be a vision of the Almighty Waaheguru. (241)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਦੀਦ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਦੀਦਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: Anyone who has seen that noble soul, consider that he has got a glimpse of the Omnipotent;

column2: ਊ ਤਰੀਕਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਫ਼ਹਿਮੀਦਾ ਅਸਤ॥੨੪੨॥
column5: And, that person has perceived and realized the path of meditation. (242)

column2: ਤਰਜ਼ਿ ਯੱਕ-ਰੰਗੀ ਅਜਬ ਰੰਗ ਆਰਦਸ਼
