column5: The focus on devotion for God brings in with it an unusual complexion of disposition,

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਬਦਨ ਨੂਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਮੀ-ਬਾਰਦਸ਼॥੨੪੩॥
column5: The splendor and radiance of Akaalpurakh then oozes out from each and every aspect of such a dedicated devotion. (243)

column2: ਊ ਬਰੰਗਿ ਸਾਹਿਬੀ ਈਂ ਹਸਤੋ ਬੂਦ
column5: He is the Master of all this illusion (of materialism), this is His own form;

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀ ਦਾਇਮ ਬ-ਆਦਾਬ ਸਜੂਦ॥੨੪੪॥
column5: And, His meditation, with respect and prostrations, always seems apt. (244)

column2: ਊ ਬਰੰਗਿ ਸਾਹਿਬੀ ਅਰਸ਼ਾਦਿ ਊ
column5: He is the shape and form of the Master and only His command prevails;

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀ ਤਾ ਸਰ ਕਦਮ ਬੁਬਯਾਦਿ ਊ॥੨੪੫॥
column5: The meditation from head to toe also emerges out of (because of) Him. (245)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬੇ ਬਾ ਸਾਹਿਬਾਂ ਜ਼ੇਬਦ ਮੁਦਾਮ
column5: A Master looks graceful and befitting only among the Masters,

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਦਰ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਿਆਮ॥੨੪੬॥
column5: A person should, therefore, continue to stay in meditation all the times. (246)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਾਂ ਰਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਸ਼ੁਆਰ
column5: The character of Masters is to be master-like,

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਦਰ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਫ਼ਸਲਿ ਬਹਾਰ॥੨੪੭॥
column5: And, a man has the spring season around him only when he meditates. (247)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਾਂ ਰਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੀ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ
column5: The master-ship character, His praises, of the Master is eternal,

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਹਮ ਦਰ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਕਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ॥੨੪੮॥
column5: And, the meditation of a human being is permanent. (248)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬਰਾਇ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਤੂ ਸਰ-ਗਸ਼ਤਾਈ
column5: For this, you have turned your head away from Him, you have strayed;

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਪਏ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਜ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਰ-ਗਸ਼ਤਾਈ॥੨੪੯॥
column5: That you have turned your face away from Him just for the sake of this material world. (249)

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਗੀਤੀ ਨ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਪਾਇਦਾਰ
column5: The worldly riches are not going to last for ever,

column2: ਯੱਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਰਾ ਬਸੂਇ ਹੱਕ ਬਿਆਰ॥੨੫੦॥
column5: (Therefore) You should turn yourself towards Waaheguru even for just one moment. (250)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਦਿਲਿ ਤੂ ਮਾਇਲਿ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: When your heart and soul becomes inclined towards remembering Waaheguru,

column2: ਆਂ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਪਾਕ ਕੈ ਅਜ਼ ਤੂ ਜੁਦਾ-ਸਤ॥੨੫੧॥
column5: Then, how and when would that pious and chaste Waaheguru be separated from you? (251)

column2: ਗਰ ਤੂ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ-ਬਾਸ਼ੀ ਅਜ਼ ਫ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਬੁਲੰਦ
column5: If you remain negligent about paying close attention to the remembrance of lofty Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਤੂ ਕੁਜਾ ਓ ਊ ਕੁਜਾ ਐ ਹੋਸ਼ਮੰਦ॥੨੫੨॥
column5: Then, you mentally alert person! How can there be a meeting between you and Him (You are here and He is somewhere else)? (252)

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਊ ਦਰਦਿ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਰਾ ਦਵਾ-ਸਤ
column5: Memory of Waaheguru is the cure-all for all pains and agonies of both the worlds;

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਊ ਹਰ ਗੁਮ-ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਰਾ ਰਾਹਨੁਮਾ ਸਤ॥੨੫੩॥
column5: His memory also directs all lost and strayed ones to the correct path. (253)

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਊ ਈਂ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਰਾ ਲਾਜ਼ਮ ਬਵਦ
column5: His remembrance is absolutely imperative for everyone,

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਅਜ਼ੋ ਮੁਲਜ਼ਮ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੨੫੪॥
column5: And, anyone who becomes negligent and forgets Him is indeed guilty. (254)

column2: ਯਾ ਇਲਾਹੀ ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਤੌਫ਼ੀਕ ਦਿਹ
column5: O Akaalpurakh ! Kindly bless me with such a courage and strength,

column2: ਤਾ ਬ-ਯਾਦਤ ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰਦ ਈਂ ਉਮਰ ਬਿਹ॥੨੫੫॥
column5: So that this life of mine is spent in a worthwhile manner in remembering You. (255)

column2: ਉਮਰ ਆਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: That life is worth living that is spent in remembering Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰਦ ਦੀਗਰ ਨਭਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਮੁਦਆ॥੨੫੬॥
column5: Any part of it spent without His memory, is simply a waste and useless. (256)

column2: ਮੁਦਆ ਬਿਹਤਰ ਜੁਜ਼ ਯਾਦ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: There is no purpose (of life) better than the remembrance of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਯਾਦਸ਼ ਈਂ ਦਿਲਿ ਮਾ ਸ਼ਾਦ ਨੀਸਤ॥੨੫੭॥
column5: And, our hearts and minds can never be pleased without remembering Him. (257)

column2: ਸ਼ਾਦੀਇ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਵਦ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: The nostalgia about Waaheguru bestows upon us an eternal elation;

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਦੌਲਤ ਕਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਰਾਹਨੁਮਾ॥੨੫੮॥
column5: How fortunate we are that it shows us the direction (in our lives)!(258)

column2: ਗਰ ਚਿ ਹੱਕ ਦਰ ਜੁਮਲਾਇ ਦਿਲਹਾ ਬਵਦ
column5: Even though Akaalpurakh abides in the hearts of everyone,

column2: ਲੇਕ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਈਮਾਂ ਬਵਦ॥੨੫੯॥
column5: Still, only an enlightened persons can be called 'a man of faith and religion'. (259)

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਕਾਬਲਿ ਦੀਦਾਰ ਹਸਤ
column5: Only the eye of an enlightened person deserves a glimpse of the Almighty;

column2: ਮਰਦਿ ਆਰਿਫ ਵਾਕਿਫ਼ਿ ਅਸਰਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੨੬੦॥
column5: And, it is only the heart of a knowledgeable person who is familiar with His mysteries. (260)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਦੂਸਤ ਦਾਰ
column5: You should develop friendship with the noble souls and should keep their company;

column2: ਤਾ ਤੂ ਹਮ ਗਰਦੀ ਜ਼ਿ ਯਮਨਸ਼ ਰੁਸਤਗਾਰ॥੨੬੧॥
column5: So that, with the Providential blessings, you may be redeemed from the cycles of transmigration. (261)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਹਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਈਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਵਦ
column5: Whatever is visible in this world is all due to company of saintly persons;

column2: ਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ ਜਿਸਮੋ ਜਾ ਸਰਾਪਾ ਜਾਂ ਬਵਦ॥੨੬੨॥
column5: Because our bodies and souls are, in fact, the soul of the Provident. (262)

column2: ਮੁਰਦੁਮਾਨਿ ਦੀਦਾ ਰੋਸ਼ਨ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਅਜ਼ੋ
column5: The pupils of my eyes are fully lighted only because of their company;

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਜਿਸਮਮ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਗੁਲਸ਼ਨ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਅਜ਼ੋ॥੨੬੩॥
column5: And, the dirt of my body, for the same reason, is transformed into a lush garden. (263)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਸਹੁਬਤ ਕਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਅਕਸੀਰ ਕਰਦ
column5: Blessed is that association that has transformed a dirt into a cure-all;

column2: ਨਾਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਤਦਬੀਰ ਕਰਦ॥੨੬੪॥
column5: And, converted every lowly uniformed person into knowledgeable and intelligent. (264)

column2: ਗੋਹਰੋ ਲਾਲੋ ਜਵਾਹਰ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: I present myself in the form of pearls, rubies and gems to my Beloved,

column2: ਹਰ ਦਮੇ ਕੂ ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰਦ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਆਂ॥੨੬੫॥
column5: When I spend every moment of my life in His memory. (265)

column2: ਈਂ ਜਵਾਹਰ-ਹਾ ਹਮਾ ਫ਼ਾਨੀ ਬਵਦ
column5: All these worldly diamonds and pearls are perishable;

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਰ ਬੰਦਾ ਅਰਜ਼ਾਨੀ ਬਵਦ॥੨੬੬॥
column5: The remembrance of Waaheguru, however, is extremely valuable for a human being. (266)

column2: ਰਸਮਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਦਾਨੀ ਕਿ ਚੀਸਤ
column5: Do you know what is the custom and tradition of the devotees of Omnipotent?

column2: ਫ਼ਾਰਿਗ਼ ਅੰਦ ਅਜ਼ ਕੈਦਹਾਇ ਮਰਜੋ ਜ਼ੀਸਤ॥੨੬੭॥
column5: They are redeemed and are free from the imprisonment of the cycles of births and deaths for ever. (267)

column2: ਯੱਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਬੇ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਨਭਗੁਜ਼ਾਸ਼ਤੰਦ
column5: They do not spend even one moment without remembering Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਆਲਮ ਬਰ ਨਹੁ ਤਬਕ ਅਫਰਾਸ਼ਤੰਦ॥੨੬੮॥
column5: They unfurl their beautiful flag (of meditation) over all the nine skies. (268)

column2: ਖ਼ੈਰਖ਼ਾਹਿ ਜੁਲਗੀ ਪੈਦਾਇਸ਼ ਅੰਦ
column5: They wish and pray for the well-being of the entire created world,

column2: ਜ਼ੇਬ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਿ ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਆਰਾਇਸ਼ ਅੰਦ॥੨੬੯॥
column5: It is them who bless all these creations and natural beauties with grace and elegance. (269)

column2: ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਜ਼ੇਵਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: The Naam of Waaheguru is an ornament for His noble and saintly devotees,

column2: ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਅਜ਼ ਨੂਰਿ ਹੱਕ ਪੁਰ ਗੌਹਰ ਅਸਤ॥੨੭੦॥
column5: And, the eye of these nobilities is always full of pearls and gems because of the refulgent glow of the Almighty. (270)

column2: ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਤਾਅਲੀਮਿ ਉਮਰਿ ਜਾਵਿਦਾਂ
column5: Their words are a lessons for a permanent life,

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਦਾਰੰਦ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ॥੨੭੧॥
column5: And, the memory of Akaalpurakh abides on their lips/tongue for ever. (271)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਮੀਗੋਇੰਦ ਅਰਸ਼ਾਦਸਤੋ ਬਸ
column5: Their utterances have the status of the words of Divine,

column2: ਬਰ ਨਮੀ ਆਰੰਦ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਨਫ਼ਸ॥੨੭੨॥
column5: And, not even one breath of theirs is spent without remembering Him. (272)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਾਕਿ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: All these saintly persons are really seekers of a Divine glimpse,

column2: ਬੋਸਤਾਨਿ ਦਹਿਰ ਗੁਲਜ਼ਾਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ॥੨੭੩॥
column5: And, this delightful worldly spread is, in fact, a heavenly flower bed. (273)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਬਾ-ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ
column5: Whosoever developed a friendship with the devotees of Waaheguru,

column2: ਸਾਇਆਇ ਊ ਬਿਹਤਰ ਅਜ਼ ਬਾਲਿ ਹੁਮਾ॥੨੭੪॥
column5: Take it that his shadow (on them) will be many times more blessed than the shadow of the feathers of the Humaa bird (It is said that a shadow of Humaa bird can bestow the kingdom of the world). (274)

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਯਾਅਨੀ ਜ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਬਿਗ਼ੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤਨ ਅਸਤ
column5: We should take it that getting absorbed in Waaheguru's meditation is to give up self-ego,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ਿਆਲਿ ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਹੱਕ ਵਾਰੁਸਤਨ ਅਸਤ॥੨੭੫॥
column5: And, not thinking about Him would get us stuck in every other worldly attraction. (275)

column2: ਰਸਤਗੀ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ ਵਾਰਸਤਗੀ-ਸਤ
column5: To redeem ourselves from our egos is the real emancipation,

column2: ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ ਦਿਲ-ਬਸਤਗੀ-ਸਤ॥੨੭੬॥
column5: And, to tie up our minds with Waaheguru's devotion is also real deliverance. (276)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸ ਕੂ ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਦਿਲ ਬਸਤਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: Whosoever has linked and attached his mind to the Almighty,

column2: ਊ ਜ਼ ਚਰਖ਼ਿ ਨਹੁ ਤਬਕ ਬਰ-ਜਸਤਾ ਅਸਤ॥੨੭੭॥
column5: Take it that he has easily leaped over a sky equipped even with nine locks. (277)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਦਿਲ-ਬਸਤਗਾਨਿ ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: The company of such God-attached devotees,

column2: ਕੈ ਮੁਯੱਸਰ ਆਇਦਤ ਈਂ ਕੀਮੀਆ॥੨੭੮॥
column5: Take it that it is the cure-all; however, how can we be fortunate enough to obtain it? (278)

column2: ਦੀਨੋ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਹੈਰਾਂ ਮਾਂਦਾ ਅੰਦ
column5: Both the faith and the religion are amazed,

column2: ਬਸ ਜ਼ ਹੈਰਾਨੀ ਪ੍ਰੀਸ਼ਾਂ ਮਾਂਦਾ ਅੰਦ॥੨੭੯॥
column5: And in this amazement beyond limits they are perplexed. (279)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿਹ ਰਾ ਈਂ ਆਰਜ਼ੂਏ ਪਾਕ ਹਸਤ
column5: Whosoever imbibes such a chaste and divine desire,

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਊ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਅਦਰਾਕ ਹਸਤ॥੨੮੦॥
column5: His Guru (teacher) is the master of innate and intrinsic knowledge. (280)

column2: ਵਾਸਿਲਾਨਿ-ਹੱਕ ਤੁਰਾ ਵਾਸਿਲ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: God connected noble saints can make your connection with Him,

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਰਾ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੨੮੧॥
column5: They can also help you to obtain the eternal treasure, the Naam. (281)

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਅਸਤ
column5: This is an immortal achievement for an enlightened person,

column2: ਈਂ ਸਖ਼ੁਨ ਮਸ਼ਹੂਰ ਬਸ ਮੁਤਆਰਿਫ਼ ਅਸਤ॥੨੮੨॥
column5: This adage is commonly well known, and everyone is well acquainted with it. (282)

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਨੋ ਕਾਮਿਲਾਨੋ ਵਾਸਿਲਾਂ
column5: The enlightened, perfect and submerged into the love of God devotees;

column2: ਨਾਮਿ ਊ ਦਾਰਦ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ॥੨੮੩॥
column5: They always have His Naam on their tongues and lips in meditation. (283)

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਵਦ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: To continually meditate on His Naam is their worship;

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਹੱਕ-ਨੁਮਾ॥੨੮੪॥
column5: And, the eternal treasure blessed by Akaalpurakh directs one towards His path. (284)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਨੁਮਾਇਦ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਰੂ
column5: When the divine eternal treasure shows Its face,

column2: ਤੂ ਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਬਾਸ਼ੀ ਵਾ ਹੱਕ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਜ਼ ਤੂ॥੨੮੫॥
column5: You would then belong to Waaheguru and He would belong to you. (285)

column2: ਬਰ ਦਿਲਤ ਗਰ ਪਰਤਵੇ ਹੱਕ ਬਰਫ਼ਗੰਦ
column5: If the shadow of Akaalpurakh falls on someone's heart and soul,

column2: ਖ਼ਾਰਿ ਹਿਜਰਤ ਰਾ ਜ਼ ਪਾਇ ਦਿਲ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੨੮੬॥
column5: Then take it that the painful thorn of separation has been extracted out of the foot (depth) of our mind. (286)

column2: ਖ਼ਾਰਿ ਹਿਜਰ ਅਜ਼ ਪਾਇ ਦਿਲ ਚੂੰ ਦੂਰ ਸ਼ੁਦ
column5: When the thorn of separation has been removed from the feet of the heart,

column2: ਖ਼ਾਨਾਇ ਦਿਲ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਮਾਮੂਰ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥੨੮੭॥
column5: Take it then that the Akaalpurakh has made the temple of our heart as His abode. (287)

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਕਤਰਾ ਕੂ ਬਦਰਿਆ ਦਰ ਫ਼ਤਾਦ
column5: Like that drop of water that fell into a river or ocean, giving up its own identity (showing humility),

column2: ਐਨ ਦਰਿਆ ਗਸ਼ਤੋ ਵਸਲਸ਼ ਦਸਤਦਾਦ॥੨੮੮॥
column5: It itself became the river and the ocean; (thus falling on the feet of Aakaalpurakh), and convergence with Him took place. (288)

column2: ਕਤਰਾ ਚੂੰ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਬਦਰਿਆ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ
column5: Once the drop merges into the ocean,

column2: ਬਾਅਦ ਅਜ਼ਾਂ ਤਫ਼ਰੀਕ ਨਤਵਾਂ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਜ਼ ਜਾ॥੨੮੯॥
column5: After that, it cannot be separated from the ocean. (289)

column2: ਕਤਰਾ ਚੂੰ ਜਾਨਿਬਿ ਦਰਿਆ ਸ਼ਤਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: When the drop started to rush in the direction of the ocean,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਰਹਿ ਤਫ਼ਰੀਕ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਰਾ ਕਤਰਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ॥੨੯੦॥
column5: It, then, realized the significance of being just a drop of water. (290)

column2: ਕਤਰਾ ਰਾ ਈਂ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਚੂੰ ਦਸਤਦਾਦ
column5: When the drop was bestowed with this eternal meeting,

column2: ਕਤਰਾ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਅੰਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਬਾ-ਮੁਰਾਦ॥੨੯੧॥
column5: The reality dawned on it, and its long-cherished desire was fulfilled. (291)

column2: ਗੁਫ਼ਤ ਮਨ ਯੱਕ ਕਤਰਾ ਆਬੀ ਬੂਦਾ ਅਮ
column5: The droplet said, Even though I am a small drop of water,

column2: ਪੈਹਨਿ ਦਰਿਆ ਰਾ ਚੁਨਾਂ ਪੈਮੂਦਾ ਅਮ॥੨੯੨॥
column5: I have been able to measure the expanse of this huge ocean. (292)

column2: ਗਰ ਮਰਾ ਦਰ ਬਾਜ਼ ਰਾਹਿ ਲੁਤਫ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼
column5: If the ocean, out of its extreme kindness, would agree to take me in,

column2: ਵਾਸਿਲ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਕਰਦ ਅਜ਼ ਅੰਦਾਜ਼ਾ ਬੇਸ਼॥੨੯੩॥
column5: And, it agreed to merge me into itself even beyond its capacity; (293)

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਮੌਜ ਅਜ਼ ਪੈਹਨਿ ਦਰਿਆ ਰੂ ਨਮੂਦ
column5: And, it rose like a tidal wave out of the span of the ocean,

column2: ਮੌਜ ਗਸ਼ਤ ਵਾ ਕਰਦ ਦਰਿਆ ਰਾ ਸਜੂਦ॥੨੯੪॥
column5: It became another wave, and then bowed in reverence to the ocean. (294)

column2: ਹਮ ਚੁਨਾਂ ਹਰ ਬੰਦਾ ਕੁ ਵਾਸਿਲ ਅਸਤ
column5: In the same manner, every such person who had a confluence with the Almighty,

column2: ਦਰ ਤਰੀਕਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਬਸ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਅਸਤ॥੨੯੫॥
column5: Became complete and perfect on the path of meditation. (295)

column2: ਮੌਜੌ ਦਰਿਆ ਗਰ ਚਿ ਦਰ ਮਾਅਨੀ ਯਕੇਸਤ
column5: In reality, a wave and the ocean are one and the same,

column2: ਲੇਕ ਅੰਦਰ ਈਨੋ ਆਂ ਫ਼ਰਕੇ ਬਸੇਸਤ॥੨੯੬॥
column5: But still there is a vast difference between them. (296)

column2: ਮਨ ਯਕੇ ਮੌਜਮ ਤੂ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਬੇਕਰਾਂ
column5: I am just a simple wave, while you are an enormously large ocean,

column2: ਫ਼ਰਕ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ਮੀਨੋ ਆਸਮਾਂ॥੨੯੭॥
column5: Thus, there is a big difference between you and me like that between the earth and the sky. (297)

column2: ਮਨ ਨੀਅੱਮ ਈਂ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਅਜ਼ ਅਲਤਾਫ਼ਿ ਤੂ
column5: I am nothing; all this (that I am) is only because of your blessings,

column2: ਮਨ ਯੱਕ ਮੌਜਮ ਜ਼ ਤਬਆਇ ਸਾਫ਼ਿ ਤੂ॥੨੯੮॥
column5: I am, too, one wave in Your vast manifest world. (298)

column2: ਬਾ ਬਜ਼ੁਰਗਾਂ ਸੁਹਬਤੇ ਮੀ ਬਾਇਦਤ
column5: You would need the association with the noble persons,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਹੁਮਾ ਅੱਵਲ ਹਮੀਂ ਮੀ ਬਾਇਦਤ॥੨੯੯॥
column5: This will be the first and the foremost thing that you will need. (299)

column2: ਕਾਦਰਿ ਮੁਤਲਿਕ ਬਕੁਦਰਤ ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: That perfect and complete Creator is visible through His own creations,

column2: ਦਰਮਿਆਨਿ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਕਾਦਰ ਅਸਤ॥੩੦੦॥
column5: The Creator, in fact, abides amidst His own Nature and manifestations. (300)

column2: ਕਾਦਰੋ ਕੁਦਰਤ ਬਹਮ ਆਮੇਖ਼ਤੰਦ
column5: The Creator and His creations are one and the same,

column2: ਆਂ ਮਤਾਇ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਰੇਖ਼ਤੰਦ॥੩੦੧॥
column5: They, the noble persons, renounce all material distractions except the Provident. (301)

column2: ਪਸ ਤੁਰਾ ਹਮ ਬਾਇਦ ਐ ਯਾਰਿ ਅਜ਼ੀਜ਼
column5: O my dear friend! Then you should also make a judgement and conclude,

column2: ਹੱਕ ਕੁਦਾਮ ਵਾ ਤੂ ਕੁਦਾਮੀ ਕੁਨ ਤਮੀਜ਼॥੩੦੨॥
column5: As to who is God, and who you are, and how to distinguish between the two. (302)

column2: ਗਰ ਤੂ ਵਾਸਿਲ ਗਸ਼ਤਾਈ ਦਰ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ ਊ
column5: If, in your pursuits, you happen to have a meeting with the Akaalpurakh.

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਦੀਗਰ ਮਗੂ॥੩੦੩॥
column5: Then you should not utter any other word except the word of worship and meditation. (303)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਅਜ਼ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਈਂ ਬੰਦਗੀਸਤ
column5: All this tangible and non-tangible boons are because of the meditation,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਬੇ-ਬੰਦਗੀ ਸ਼ਰਮਿੰਦਗਸਿਤ॥੩੦੪॥
column5: Without meditation, this life of ours is only mortification and humiliation. (304)

column2: ਹੱਕ ਤਾਅਲਾ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਫ਼ਰਮੂਦਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: The Almighty God has also said,

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਕੂ ਬੰਦਾ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਆਸੂਦਾ ਅਸਤ॥੩੦੫॥
column5: Anyone who transformed himself into a man of God is redeemed. (305)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਅਨਅਲਹੱਕ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ ਇਜ਼ਹਾਰ ਕਰਦ
column5: Anyone who proclaimed through his own mouth that he was God,

column2: ਸ਼ਰਆ ਆਂ ਮਨਸੂਰ ਰਾ ਬਰ-ਦਾਰ ਕਰਦ॥੩੦੬॥
column5: The Islamic religious law crucified him just like Mansoor. (306)

column2: ਮਸਤੀਏ ਹੱਕ ਮਾਅਨੀਇ ਹੁਸ਼ਿਆਰੀ ਅਸਤ
column5: Inebriated with God is, in fact, to be always in a state of alertness,

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਖ਼ਾਬ ਹਮ ਬੇਦਾਰੀ ਅਸਤ॥੩੦੭॥
column5: Even dreaming while asleep for the knowledgeable is indeed like staying awake. (307)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਬੇ-ਅਦਬ ਯਾਬਦ ਸਜ਼ਾ
column5: In reality, a disrespectful faces the consequences (reaps the fruits) of his own actions,

column2: ਚੂੰ ਆਦਬ ਅਮਦ ਹਮਾ ਰਾ ਰਹਾਨੁਮਾ॥੩੦੮॥
column5: Because, it is the 'respect' and 'civility' that is capable of showing all the direction of the right path. (308)

column2: ਗਰ ਤੂ ਸਰ ਤਾ ਪਾ ਹਮਾ ਹੱਕ ਗਸ਼ਤਾਈ
column5: If you have transformed yourself from head to toe into Akaalpurakh's form,

column2: ਵਾਸਿਲਿ ਬੇ-ਚੂਨਿ ਮੁਤਲਿਕ ਗਸ਼ਤਾਈ॥੩੦੯॥
column5: And, If you have merged into that unparalleled and matchless Waaheguru, (309)

column2: ਬਾਜ਼ ਰਾਹਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਦਰ ਪੇਸ਼ ਗੀਰ
column5: Then you should adopt the path of meditation,

column2: ਬੰਦਾਇ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ ਵਾ ਰਾਹਿ ਖੇਸ਼ ਗੀਰ॥੩੧੦॥
column5: And, become His (favorite) person by holding on to the divine spiritual passage of meditation. (310)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਮਾ ਹਾਲਤ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਾਜ਼ਰ ਬਬੀਂ
column5: One should assume His presence under all circumstances,

column2: ਹਾਜ਼ਿਰੋ ਨਾਜ਼ਿਰ ਹਮਾਂ ਨਾਜ਼ਿਰ ਬਬੀਂ॥੩੧੧॥
column5: Considering Him Omnipresent and Immanent, and also take it that He is capable of seeing everything everywhere. (311)

column2: ਦਰ ਰਾਹਿ ਹੱਕ ਜੁਜ਼ ਅਦਬ ਤਾਅਲੀਮ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: There is no education except that of respect and civility in the path of God,

column2: ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਊ ਰਾ ਬਜੁਜ਼ ਤਸਲੀਮ ਨੀਸਤ॥੩੧੨॥
column5: It is not prudent for His seeker-devotee to accept anything but His Order. (312)

column2: ਤਾਲਿਬਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾ ਬਾ ਅਦਬ
column5: The seekers of the Divine Spirit are always respectful,

column2: ਬਾ ਅਦਬ ਬਾਸ਼ੰਦ ਅੰਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਰੱਬ॥੩੧੩॥
column5: They are also satiated with reverence while remembering Him. (313)

column2: ਬੇਅਦਬ ਰਾ ਕੈ ਜ਼ਿ ਰਾਹਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਖ਼ਬਰ
column5: What does an apostate know about the tradition of those noble persons?

column2: ਬੇਅਦਬ ਅਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾਂ ਬੇ-ਅਸਰ॥੩੧੪॥
column5: The efforts of an atheist to get a glimpse of Akaalpurakh will always be ineffective. (314)

column2: ਬੇਅਦਬ ਹਰਗਿਜ਼ ਬ-ਹੱਕ ਰਾਹਿ ਨਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: A disrespectful can never find the path leading towards the Divine Spirit;

column2: ਰਾਹਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਰੀਚ ਗੁਮਰਾਹੇ ਨਯਾਫ਼ਤ॥੩੧੫॥
column5: No strayed person has ever been able to find the path of God and much less to reach Him. (315)

column2: ਹਾਦੀਏ ਰਾਹਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਆਮਦ ਅਦਬ
column5: It is reverence that is the guide to the path of Waaheguru;

column2: ਬੇ-ਅਦਬ ਖ਼ਾਲੀ-ਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਅਲਤਾਫ਼ਿ ਰੱਬ॥੩੧੬॥
column5: And, an atheist remains blank from receiving His blessings. (316)

column2: ਬੇ-ਅਦਬ ਰਾਹਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਕੈ ਦਾਨਦਸ਼
column5: How can an atheist find the way to the Almighty,

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿਰਾ ਕਹਿਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਮੀਰਾਨਦਸ਼॥੩੧੭॥
column5: Who has been condemned due to the anger of Waaheguru?(317)

column2: ਦਰ ਪਨਾਹਿ ਸਾਇਆਇ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: If you care to seek the shelter (and agree to function in their shadow) of the noble souls of God,

column2: ਗਰ ਰਵੀ ਆਂ ਜਾ ਅਦਬ ਯਾਬੀ ਸਬਕ॥੩੧੮॥
column5: You will receive teachings and instructions about respectability there. (318)

column2: ਬੇ ਅਦਬ ਈਂਜਾ ਅਦਬ ਆਮੋਜ਼ ਸ਼ੁਦ
column5: Coming to this place (of noble persons), even the apostates become capable to teach lessons of reverence,

column2: ਈਂ ਚਰਾਗ਼ਿ ਗੁਲ ਜਹਾਂ ਅਫ਼ਰੋਜ਼ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥੩੧੯॥
column5: Here, even extinguished lamps begin to spread light throughout the world. (319)

column2: ਐ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਹਰ ਬੇ ਅਦਬ ਰਾ ਦਿਹ ਅਦਬ
column5: O Akaalpurakh! Kindly bestow reverence even on the irreverent,

column2: ਤਾ ਗੁਜ਼ਾਰਦ ਉਮਰ ਰਾ ਦਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਰੱਬ॥੩੨੦॥
column5: So that they can spend their lives in Your remembrance. (320)

column2: ਗਰ ਬਯਾਬੀ ਲੱਜ਼ਤੇ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਊ
column5: If you can relish the savor (sweet taste) of Waaheguru's remembrance,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਬਾਸ਼ੀ ਦਾਇਮਾ ਐ ਨੇਕ-ਖੂ॥੩੨੧॥
column5: Then O good man! You can become immortal. (321)

column2: ਈਂ ਵਜੂਦਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਰਾ ਕਾਇਮ ਬਦਾਂ
column5: Consider this body of dirt as permanent for this reason

column2: ਕਾਇਮ ਆਮਦ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਊ ਦਰ ਹਿਰਜ਼ਿ ਜਾਂ॥੩੨੨॥
column5: Because the devotion for Him have established permanently in the fortress of your heart. (322)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਏ ਜਾਂ ਬਵਦ
column5: The love and elation for Akaalpurakh are the life-line of the soul,

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਊ ਸਰਮਾਆਇ ਈਮਾਂ ਬਵਦ॥੩੨੩॥
column5: There is a wealth of faith and religion in His memory. (323)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਊ ਦਰ ਹਰ ਦਿਲੇ ਕੈ ਜਾ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: How can the euphoria and elation for Waaheguru abide in every heart,

column2: ਦਰ ਵਜੂਦਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਕੈ ਮਾਵਾ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੨੪॥
column5: And, how can He take refuge in the body made up of dirt. (324)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਮਰ ਤੁਰਾ ਚੂੰ ਦਸਤਦਾਦ
column5: When your fondness for Akaalpurakh supported you,

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਦਾਇਮ ਬਦਸਤਤ ਦਰ-ਫ਼ਤਾਦ॥੩੨੫॥
column5: Then, take it for granted that you will obtain control and have the divine eternal wealth. (325)

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਰਾਹਸ਼ ਸੁਰਮਾਇ ਸਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: The dust of His path is like a collyrium for our eyes and head,

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਬਿਹ ਜ਼ ਤਾਜ਼ੋ ਅਫ਼ਸਰ ਅਸਤ॥੩੨੬॥
column5: This dust is much more valuable than the crown and the thrones for the enlightened. (326)

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਨਭਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਪਾਇਦਾਰ
column5: This worldly wealth is not ever lasting,

column2: ਦਰ ਤਰੀਕਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਗੁਜ਼ਾਰ॥੩੨੭॥
column5: You should accept this according to the verdict of the true devotees of God. (327)

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਊ ਲਾਜ਼ਿਮ ਬਵਦ ਦਾਇਮ ਤੁਰਾ
column5: Meditation of Waaheguru is always absolutely essential for you,

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਊ ਕਾਇਮ ਕੁਨਦ ਕਾਇਮ ਤੁਰਾ॥੩੨੮॥
column5: And, the discourse about Him makes you steady and immovable forever. (328)

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਂ ਦਾਰੰਦ ਬਾ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼
column5: The devotees of Akaalpurakh are intimately connected to the divine knowledge,

column2: ਹਾਸਲਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਂ ਦਰੂਨਿ ਜਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼॥੩੨੯॥
column5: And, the achievement of divine knowledge is completely absorbed inside their souls. (329)

column2: ਮਸਨਦਿ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਇਲਾਹੀ ਬੇ-ਜ਼ਵਾਲ
column5: The throne of devotion for Akaalpurakh is permanent and indestructible,

column2: ਵਰਨਾ ਬੀਨੀ ਪੁਰ ਜ਼ਵਾਲੇ ਹਰ ਕਮਾਲ॥੩੩੦॥
column5: Although every peak has a trough. (330)

column2: ਬੇ-ਜ਼ਵਾਲ ਆਮਦ ਕਮਾਲਿ ਜ਼ੌਕਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: The marvel of the zest for God's love is eternal and non-destructible,

column2: ਤਾ ਕਿ ਯਾਬਦ ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਹੱਕ॥੩੩੧॥
column5: Wish were it that we can get just one particle of His devotion. (331)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਕੂ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ ਊ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਸ਼ੁਦ
column5: Whosoever is lucky enough to obtain such a particle, he becomes immortal,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਉਮੀਦ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥੩੩੨॥
column5: In reality, his desire (to meet with Akaalpurakh) is fulfilled. (332)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਉਮੀਦਸ਼ ਸੂਰਤਿ ਹਾਸਲ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ
column5: When he reached the stage of fulfillment,

column2: ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕ ਜਾ ਦਰ ਦਿਲ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੩੩੩॥
column5: That particle of the strong desire for His devotion gets seeded into his heart. (333)

column2: ਆਬਿ ਹੈਵਾਂ ਮੀਚਕਦ ਅਜ਼ ਮੂਇ ਊ
column5: The divine nectar oozes out of his every hair,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਮੀਗਰਦਦ ਜਹਾਂ ਅਜ਼ ਬੂਇ ਊ॥੩੩੪॥
column5: And the whole world, with his aroma, becomes alive and rises up. (334)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਇਮਸਾਨ ਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤਾ
column5: Fortunate is that person who has attained the Provident;

column2: ਰੂ ਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਗੈਰਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਰ-ਤਾਫ਼ਤਾ॥੩੩੫॥
column5: And, who has turned himself away (detached) from every worldly items except the memory of God. (335)

column2: ਦਰ ਲਿਬਾਸਿ ਦੁਨਿਯਵੀ ਫ਼ਾਰਿਗ਼ ਅਜ਼ਾਂ
column5: Even while living in worldly guise, he is detached from every materialistic thing,

column2: ਹਮਚੂ ਜ਼ਾਤਿਸ਼ ਆਸ਼ਿਕਾਂ ਰਾ ਊ ਨਿਹਾਂ॥੩੩੬॥
column5: Like the Entity of God, he maintains a hidden profile. (336)

column2: ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰਿਸ਼ ਦਰ ਕੈਦਿ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਹਸਤ
column5: Outwardly he might be appearing to be in the grip of a fistful of dust,

column2: ਬਾਤਨਿ ਊ ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਪਾਕ ਹਸਤ॥੩੩੭॥
column5: Inwardly, he is always engaged in talking about the chaste Akaalpurakh and abides with Him. (337)

column2: ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰ ਅੰਦਰ ਮਾਇਲਿ ਫਰਜ਼ੰਦੋ ਜ਼ਨ
column5: Outwardly, he might appear to be immersed in love for his child and wife,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ॥੩੩੮॥
column5: In reality, he always abides (in thought and action) with his God. (338)

column2: ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰ ਅੰਦਰ ਮਾਇਲਿ ਹਿਰਸੋ ਹਵਾ
column5: Outwardly, he might seem to be inclined towards 'desires and greed',

column2: ਬਾਤਨਿ ਊ ਪਾਕ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ॥੩੩੯॥
column5: But inwardly, he remains chaste and sacred in the memory of Waaheguru. (339)

column2: ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰ ਅੰਦਰ ਮਾਇਲਿ ਅਸਪੋ ਸ਼ੁਤਰ
column5: Outwardly, he might appear to be paying attention to horses and camels,

column2: ਬਾਤਨਸ਼ ਫ਼ਾਰਿਗ਼ ਜ਼ ਕੈਦਿ ਸ਼ੋਰੋ ਸ਼ਰ॥੩੪੦॥
column5: But inwardly, he remains detached from the worldly hub-hub and noises. (340)

column2: ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰ ਅੰਦਰ ਮਾਇਲਿ ਸੀਮੋ ਜ਼ਰ ਅਸਤ
column5: He might appear to be involved in gold and silver outwardly,

column2: ਬਾਤਨ ਅੰਦਰ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਬਹਿਰੋ ਬਰ ਅਸਤ॥੩੪੧॥
column5: But he is, in fact, master of land and water inwardly. (341)

column2: ਰਫ਼ਤਾ ਰਫ਼ਤਾ ਬਾਤਨਸ਼ ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: His intrinsic worth gets revealed slowly and gradually,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਤਿਬਲਾਇ ਅੰਬਰ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ॥੩੪੨॥
column5: In fact, he becomes a casket of fragrance. (342)

column2: ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰੋ ਬਾਤਨ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਯਕਸਾਨਿ ਊ
column5: His internal and external selves become one and the same,

column2: ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਬੰਦਾਇ ਫ਼ਰਮਾਨਿ ਊ॥੩੪੩॥
column5: Both the worlds become followers of his command. (343)

column2: ਹਮ ਬਦਿਲ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਵਾ ਰਾ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ
column5: His heart and tongue are completely immersed in Akaalpurkh's remembrance at all times and for ever,

column2: ਈਂ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਨਸ਼ ਦਿਲ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਦਿਲ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ॥੩੪੪॥
column5: His tongue becomes his heart, and his heart his tongue. (344)

column2: ਵਾਸਿਲਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਚੁਨੀਂ ਫ਼ਰਮੂਦਾ ਅੰਦ
column5: Those saintly souls who have converged with God have said clearly,

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਹਾ ਦਰ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਆਸੂਦਾ ਅੰਦ॥੩੪੫॥
column5: That persons of God remain comfortable and happy while they are in meditation. (345)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਮੁਸੱਲਮ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਰਾ
column5: The mastery and splendor of our True King, the Waaheguru, is well known,

column2: ਕੁਰਨਸ਼ਿ ਮਨ ਸਾਲਿਕਿ ਈਂ ਰਾਹ ਰਾ॥੩੪੬॥
column5: I bow before the pedestrian who is walking on this path. (346)

column2: ਸਾਲਾਕਿ ਈਂ ਰਾਹ ਬਮੰਜ਼ਲ ਰਾਹ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: The traveler on this path arrived at his destination,

column2: ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਉਮਰਿ ਦਿਲ ਆਗਾਹ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ॥੩੪੭॥
column5: And, his heart became familiar with the real purpose and attainment of life. (347)

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਹਾ ਰਾ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਦਰਕਾਰ ਹਸਤ
column5: Persons of God really need only His meditation,

column2: ਜਾਮਿ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਸਰਸ਼ਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੩੪੮॥
column5: And, the cup of the pleasure and elation of meditation continues to overflow for ever. (348)

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬੀ ਜ਼ੇਬਦ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਪਾਕ ਰਾ
column5: The Mastery (of all creations in this world) is befitting and looks elegant only for the true and the chaste Master, the Akaalpurakh;

column2: ਆਂ ਕਿ ਜ਼ੀਨਤ ਦਾਦ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਰਾ॥੩੪੯॥
column5: And, it is Him alone who has blessed this fistful of dust the gaiety and prosperity. (349)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕਸ਼ ਮੁਮਤਾਜ਼ ਕਰਦ
column5: The fondness to remember the Waaheguru blessed him with prominence,

column2: ਸਰ ਫ਼ਰਾਜ਼ੋ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਹਰ ਰਾਜ਼ ਕਰਦ॥੩੫੦॥
column5: And, this tendency also blessed him with honor and eminence and made him familiar with His mysteries. (350)

column2: ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਰੌਂਕ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ
column5: This fistful of dust, with the remembrance of Akaalpurakh, became bright and shiny,

column2: ਬਸਕਿ ਦਰ ਦਿਲ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਗ੍ਰਿਫ਼ਤ॥੩੫੧॥
column5: And, the fondness to remember Him began to surge like a storm in his heart. (351)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਕਾਦਰ ਕਿ ਅਜ਼ ਯੱਕ ਕਤਰਾ ਆਬ
column5: May we express our deep devotion for the Omnipotent who from just one drop of water

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕ ਰਾਂ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਕੁਨਦ ਚੂੰ ਆਫ਼ਤਾਬ॥੩੫੨॥
column5: Gave this lowly fistful of dust the brightness and shine of the sun. (352)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਖ਼ਾਕੇ ਕਿ ਨੂਰ-ਅਫ਼ਰੋਜ਼ ਸ਼ੁਦ
column5: May we sacrifice ourselves for that dust that became enlightened and radiant,

column2: ਈਂ ਸਆਦਤ ਹਾ ਨਸੀਬ ਅੰਦੋਜ਼ ਸ਼ੁਦ॥੩੫੩॥
column5: And, which was fortunate enough to deserve such boons and blessings. (353)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਕੁਦਰਤ ਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਾਰ ਆਵੁਰਦ
column5: Wonderful is the Nature that brings in the fruits of truth,

column2: ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਖ਼ਾਕੀ ਰਾ ਬਗੁਫ਼ਤਾਰ ਆਵੁਰਦ॥੩੫੪॥
column5: And, which blesses the humble fistful of dust the power to speak. (354)

column2: ਹਾਸਲਿ ਈਂ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: It is the meditation of Waaheguru that is the achievement of this life;

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਚਸ਼ਮੇ ਕਿ ਬਰ ਹੱਕ ਮੁਬਤਲਾ-ਸਤ॥੩੫੫॥
column5: May we sacrifice ourselves for the eye that becomes overawed and gets obsessed with the Truth (God). (355)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਦਿਲ ਕਿ ਅੰਦਰਸ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕਸ਼ ਬਵਦ
column5: How blessed is the heart that has an innocent eagerness for the love of God!

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਜ਼ੌਕਸ਼ ਬਵਦ॥੩੫੬॥
column5: In fact, he becomes a fervent and fascinated devotee for His love. (356)

column2: ਆਂ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਸਰ ਕੂ ਬਰਾਹਿਸ਼ ਦਰ ਸਜੂਦ
column5: Blessed is the head that bows to the real path of Truth, the God;

column2: ਹਮ ਚੂੰ ਚੌਗਾਂ ਗੁਏ ਸ਼ੌਕਸ਼ ਦਰ ਰਬੂਦ॥੩੫੭॥
column5: And, who like the crooked stick with a grip, ran away with the ball of elation. (357)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਦਸਤੇ ਕਿ ਵਸਫ਼ਸ਼ ਊ ਨਵਿਸ਼ਤ
column5: Wonderful are those hands that have written His praises and lauds;

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਪਾਏ ਕੂ ਦਰ ਕੂਇਸ਼ ਗੁਜ਼ਸ਼ਤ॥੩੫੮॥
column5: Blessed are those feet that have passed through His street. (358)

column2: ਆਂ ਜ਼ਬਾਨੇ ਬਿਹ ਕਿ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਊ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: Noble is the tongue that meditates on His Naam;

column2: ਖ਼ਾਤਰੇ ਆਂ ਬਿਹ ਕਿ ਫ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਊ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੫੯॥
column5: And, virtuous is the mind that concentrates its thoughts on Waaheguru. (359)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਮਾ ਉਜ਼ਵੇ ਕਿ ਊ ਅੰਦਰ ਤਨਸਤ
column5: Akaalpurakh abides in every limb of our bodies,

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਊ ਅੰਦਰ ਸਰਿ ਮਰਦੋ ਜ਼ਨ ਅਸਤ॥੩੬੦॥
column5: And, the zeal and ardor for His love is assimilated in the heads of all men and women. (360)

column2: ਆਰਜ਼ੂਏ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਸ਼ੂਇ ਊ ਬਵਦ
column5: All desires and wishes are concentrated in His direction,

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਊ ਆਗੁਸ਼ਤਾ ਦਰ ਹਰ ਮੂ ਬਵਦ॥੩੬੧॥
column5: And, fondness for Him is absorbed in every hair of our bodies. (361)

column2: ਗਰ ਤੂ ਖ਼ਾਹੀ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਂ ਸ਼ਵੀ
column5: If you want that you become master of divine thought,
Then, you should sacrifice your life for your Beloved Waaheguru, so that you will acquire the same shape and form that He has. (362)

column2: ਹਰਚਿ ਦਾਰੀ ਕੁਨ ਹਮਾ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਿ ਊ
column5: You should sacrifice all that you have for your true Beloved,

column2: ਰੇਜ਼ਾ-ਚੀਨੀ ਕੁਨ ਦਮੇ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ਾਨਿ ਊ॥੩੬੩॥
column5: And, pick up scraps of food from His dining table just for a moment. (363)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਨਸ਼ ਅਗਰ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਵਦ
column5: If you become completely desirous of His true knowledge and enlightenment,

column2: ਗੌਹਰਿ ਮਕਸੂਦ ਤੂ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੩੬੪॥
column5: Then, you will, inevitably, achieve your purpose. (364)

column2: ਤੂ ਹਮ ਅਜ਼ ਈਂ ਉਮਰ ਯਾਬੀ ਬਹਿਰਾਇ
column5: You will receive your life's fruits,

column2: ਮਿਹਰਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਨਸ਼ ਚੂ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਦ ਜ਼ੱਰਾਇ॥੩੬੫॥
column5: When the sun of divine knowledge would bless you with just one ray of its radiance. (365)

column2: ਨਾਮਿ ਤੂ ਅੰਦਰ ਜਹਾਂ ਗਰਦਦ ਬੁਲੰਦ
column5: Your name would become famous and illuminated;

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਨਤ ਕੁਨਦ ਬਸ ਅਰਜ਼ਮੰਦ॥੩੬੬॥
column5: And, your ardor for divine knowledge would make you extremely popular in this world. (366)

column2: ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਨਿਸ਼ ਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਦਸਤਬਾਦ
column5: Whosoever developed special affection and fondness for divine love,

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਕੁਲੀਦਸ਼ ਕੁਫ਼ਲਿ ਦਿਲਹਾ ਰਾ ਕੁਸ਼ਾਦ॥੩੬੭॥
column5: With his key, all locks of the hearts got opened up (realities became known). (367)

column2: ਕੁਫ਼ਲ ਰਾ ਬਿਕੁਸ਼ਾ ਵਾ ਮਾਲਿ ਬੇਕਰਾਂ
column5: You should, too, open up the lock of your heart, and from the hidden

column2: ਦਰ ਕਫ਼ਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਆਰ ਅਜ਼ ਗੰਜਿ ਨਿਹਾਂ॥੩੬੮॥
column5: treasure, should derive unlimited pleasure and elation. (368)

column2: ਕੰਦਰਾਂ ਲਾਲੋ ਗੁਹਰ ਬਿਸੀਆਰ ਹਸਤ
column5: In the nooks of your heart, numerous gems and diamonds are lying hidden;

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਮਤਾਇ ਲੁਲੂਏ ਸ਼ਹਿਵਾਰ ਹਸਤ॥੩੬੯॥
column5: And, your treasure and wealth contain many royal pearls. (369)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਮੀਖ਼ਾਹੀ ਜ਼ਿ ਗੰਜਿ ਬੇਸ਼ੁਮਾਰ
column5: Then whatever you would desire to obtain from this infinite treasure,

column2: ਆਇਦਤ ਦਰ ਦਸਤ ਐ ਆਾਲੀ ਤਬਾਰ॥੩੭੦॥
column5: O person of high status! You would be able to get. (370)

column2: ਪਸ ਬਖ਼ਾਨੀ ਸਾਹਿਬਾਨਿ ਸ਼ੌਕ ਰਾ
column5: Therefore you should call upon the faithful devotees of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਤਾ ਜ਼ਿ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਕੁਨੀਂ ਈਂ ਜ਼ੌਕ ਰਾ॥੩੭੧॥
column5: So that you would be able to inculcate such an ardor and zeal for Him. (371)

column2: ਜ਼ੌਕਿ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਗਰ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਤੁਰਾ
column5: If you can acquire the strong desire for the love of Waaheguru,

column2: ਫ਼ੈਜ਼ਿ ਈਂ ਸੁਹਬਤ ਅਸਰ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਦ ਤੁਰਾ॥੩੭੨॥
column5: Then, the blessing of their company is bound to influence you and your personality. (372)

column2: ਗਰ ਚਿਹ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਦਿਲਹਾ ਨਭਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਜੁਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: Even though, there is nothing else but the Almighty abides in the hearts of every one,

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਮੰਜ਼ਲੇ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਊਲਾ॥੩੭੩॥
column5: Still, the true and sincere enlightened persons have a high status and elevated destination. (373)

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਵਾਕਿਫ਼ਿ ਈਂ ਹਾਲ ਨੀਸਤ
column5: No one other than the knowledgeable is aware of the condition(s) of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਸ਼ ਕਾਲ ਨੀਸਤ॥੩੭੪॥
column5: The enlightened do not speak any words other than the discourses and meditation of the Naam of Waaheguru. (374)

column2: ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹਾਂ ਸਲਤਨਤ ਬਿ-ਗੁਜ਼ਾਸ਼ਤਦ
column5: The kings abdicated their thrones, luxurious living and royal powers,

column2: ਚੂ ਗਦਾਯਾਂ ਕੂ ਬਕੂ ਬਿਸ਼ਤਾਫ਼ਤੰਦ॥੩੭੫॥
column5: And they kept roaming around from street to street like beggars. (375)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬਰਾਇ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਯਾਦਿ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: For all of them, it is essential to continue to get engaged in the true memory of the Omnipotent;

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਮਕਾਫ਼ਾਤਿ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਵਾ ਰਹੰਦ॥੩੭੬॥
column5: And, thus, get redemption from the cycles of births and deaths in both the worlds. (376)

column2: ਵਾਕਫ਼ਿ ਈਂ ਰਾਹ ਅਗਰ ਦਸਤ ਆਮਦੇ
column5: If ever one can come across some one who is familiar with this path and tradition,

column2: ਮਕਸਦਸ਼ ਦਰ ਸਲਤਨਤ ਦਸਤ ਆਮਦੇ॥੩੭੭॥
column5: Then, all aims and objectives of government administration would be fulfilled. (377)

column2: ਜੁਮਲਾ ਲਸ਼ਕਰ ਤਾਲਿਬਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਸ਼ੁਦੇ
column5: If all forces of the army were to becomes seekers of Divine Power,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਆਾਰਫ਼ਿ ਮੁਤਲਿਕ ਸ਼ੁਦੇ॥੩੭੮॥
column5: Then, in fact, they all can become enlightened persons indeed. (378)

column2: ਸਾਲਿਕਿ ਈਂ ਰਾਹ ਅਗਰ ਦਰਯਾਫ਼ਤੇ
column5: If we can run into a fellow traveler of this path, and ask him about its true tradition;

column2: ਦਿਲ ਅਜ਼ੀਂ ਸ਼ਾਹੀ ਕੁਜਾ ਬਰਤਾਫ਼ਤੇ॥੩੭੯॥
column5: Then, how can his mind turn away from this royal kingdom? (379)

column2: ਤੁਖ਼ਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਦਰ ਮਜ਼ਰਾਇ ਦਿਲ ਕਾਸ਼ਤੇ
column5: If the seed of Truth can be cultivated in the fields of mind,

column2: ਤਾ ਗ਼ੁਬਾਰਿ ਦਿਲ ਜ਼ਿ ਦਿਲ ਬਰਦਾਸ਼ਤੇ॥੩੮੦॥
column5: Then, all suspicions and illusions of our mind will be eliminated. (380)

column2: ਬਰ ਸਰਿ ਤਖ਼ਤਿ ਨਗੀਨ ਕਾਇਮ ਬੁਦੇ
column5: They can sit on a diamond studded throne for good

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਗਰ ਬਦਿਲ ਕਾਇਮ ਸ਼ੁਦੇ॥੩੮੧॥
column5: If they can inculcate the meditation of Akaalpurakh in their minds, (381)

column2: ਬੂਇ ਹੱਕ ਮੀਆਇਦ ਅਜ਼ ਹਰ ਮੁਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: Fragrance of Truth is emitting from their each and every hair,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਮੀ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਹਰ ਕਸੇ ਅਜ਼ ਬੂਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ॥੩੮੨॥
column5: In fact, every one is becoming alive and getting invigorated with the aroma of the company of such people. (382)

column2: ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਬੀਰੂੰ ਨ ਬੂਦ ਅਜ਼ ਜਿਸਮਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ
column5: Waaheguru's Naam would not have been outside their bodies,

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਅਗਰ ਦਾਦੇ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਂ॥੩੮੩॥
column5: If the perfect Guru had indicated to them with the information about His whereabouts and location. (In stead of looking outside, they could have attained His convergence from within their own hearts.)(383)

column2: ਆਬਿ ਹੈਵਾਂ ਅੰਦਰੂਨਿ ਖ਼ਾਨਾ ਹਸਤ
column5: The elixir of life is, in fact, inside the heart's so called abode,

column2: ਲੇਕ ਬੇ-ਹਾਦੀ ਜਹਾਂ ਬੇਗ਼ਾਨਾਂ ਹਸਤ॥੩੮੪॥
column5: But the world would not know about this fact without a perfect Guru. (384)

column2: ਚੂੰ ਜ਼ ਸ਼ਹਿਰਗ਼ ਹਸਤ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਨਜ਼ਦੀਕ ਤਰ
column5: When the True Master is nearer than even your main artery,

column2: ਚੂੰ ਬਸਹਿਰਾ ਮੀਰਵੀ ਐ ਬੇ-ਖ਼ਬਰ॥੩੮੫॥
column5: O ignorant and amateur person! Then why are you roaming around jungles and wilderness. (385)

column2: ਵਾਕਫ਼ਿ ਈਂ ਰਾਹ ਚੂ ਗਰਦਦ ਰਾਹਨੁਮਾ
column5: When someone familair and well conversant with this path becomes your guide,

column2: ਖ਼ਲਵਤੇ ਦਰ ਅੰਜੁਮਨ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਤੁਰਾ॥੩੮੬॥
column5: You will be able to achieve solitude within the company of noble persons. (386)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਅੰਦਰ ਖ਼ਿਲਾਫ਼ਤ ਦਾਸ਼ਤੰਦ
column5: Whatever worldly possessions they have,

column2: ਜਮਲਾ ਰਾ ਯੱਕ ਬਾਰੋਗੀ ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਾਸ਼ਤੰਦ॥੩੮੭॥
column5: They are willing to relinquish them in one instalment instantly. (387)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬਰਾਇ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: So that they could achieve the Ultimate Entity,

column2: ਪੈਰਵੀਇ ਆਰਿਫ਼ਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੩੮੮॥
column5: For this reason, they follow completely the perfectly enlightened persons. (388)

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਤੁਰਾ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਕੁਨੰਦ
column5: Perfect saints can transform you also into perfect saints;

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਮੀਖ਼ਾਹੀ ਤੁਰਾ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਕੁਨੰਦ॥੩੮੯॥
column5: And, they can fulfill all your desires and wishes. (389)

column2: ਰਾਸਤੀ ਈਨਸਤ ਰਾਹਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਗੀਰ
column5: The truth in it is that you should adopt the path leading towards the Lord,

column2: ਤਾ ਤੂ ਹਮ ਗਰਦੀ ਚੂ ਖ਼ੁਰਸ਼ੀਦਿ ਮੁਨੀਰ॥੩੯੦॥
column5: So that you, too, can shine like the brilliance of the sun. (390)

column2: ਹੱਕ ਦਰੂਨਿ ਦਿਲ ਕਿ ਦਿਲਦਾਰੀ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: The true Akaalpurakh, abiding in your heart, extends His love to you;

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਤੁਰਾ ਯਾਰੀ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੯੧॥
column5: And, the perfect and complete Guru like a true friend assists you in this process. (391)

column2: ਵਾਕਫ਼ਿ ਈਂ ਰਾਹ ਅਗਰ ਆਰੀ ਬਦਸਤ
column5: If you can run into someone who is familiar with this (divine) path,

column2: ਅੰਦਰੂੰ ਯਾਬੀ ਮਤਾਇ ਹਰ ਚਿ ਹਸਤ॥੩੯੨॥
column5: Then, you will discover all types of material and non-material wealth and treasures inside you. (392)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਕਸੇ ਰਾ ਦਸਤ ਦਾਦ
column5: Whosoever has come across a true Guru,

column2: ਤਾਜਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਬਫ਼ਰਕਿ ਊ ਨਿਹਾਦ॥੩੯੩॥
column5: The true Guru will put on the crown of true divine knowledge on his head. (393)

column2: ਮਹਿਰਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: The true and perfect Guru can make one conversant with the mysteries and love of Waaheguru,

column2: ਦੌਲਤਿ ਜਾਵੀਦ ਰਾ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਕੁਨਦ॥੩੯੪॥
column5: And, helps in achieving the eternal divine wealth. (394)

column2: ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਬੰਦਾਇ ਫ਼ਰਮਾਨਿ ਊ
column5: Persons from both the worlds obey his (the Guru's) command spontaneously,

column2: ਈਂ ਜਹਾਨੋ ਆਂ ਜਹਾਂ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਿ ਊ॥੩੯੫॥
column5: And, both the worlds are willing to lay their lives for him. (395)

column2: ਮਾਅਨੀਇੇ ਇਹਸਾਨ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਨਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ-ਸਤ
column5: The real gratitude for Akaalpurakh is the (achievement of) true divine knowledge,

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਰੂ ਨੁਮਾ ਸਤ॥੩੯੬॥
column5: And, the immortal wealth emerges showing its face to the enlightened persons. (396)

column2: ਤਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ ਬਿਸ਼ਨਾਖ਼ਤਸ਼
column5: When, by abiding the All-powerful in his heart, recognized His Entity,

column2: ਨਕਦਿ ਉਮਰਿ ਜਾਵਿਦਾਂ ਦਰਯਾਫ਼ਤਸ਼॥੩੯੭॥
column5: Take it that he achieved the treasure of eternal life. (397)

column2: ਊ ਦਰੂਨਿ ਦਿਲ ਤੂ ਬੀਰੂੰ ਮੀ-ਰਵੀ
column5: He, the Almighty Lord, abides inside your heart, but you keep running around outside,

column2: ਊ ਬਖ਼ਾਨਾ ਤੂ ਬਹੱਜ ਚੂੰ ਮੀ-ਰਵੀ॥੩੯੮॥
column5: He is right inside your home, but you keep going (outside) for Hadj in search of Him. (398)

column2: ਊ ਸਤ ਅਜ਼ ਹਰ ਮੂਇ ਤੂ ਚੂੰ ਆਸ਼ਕਾਰ
column5: When He manifests Himself from each and every hair of your body,

column2: ਤੂ ਕੁਜਾ ਬੀਰੂੰ ਰਵੀ ਬਹਿਰਿ ਸ਼ਿਕਾਰ॥੩੯੯॥
column5: Where do you stray outside to track Him out (to hunt for Him). (399)

column2: ਅੰਦਰੂਨਿ ਖ਼ਾਨਾ-ਅਤ ਨੂਰਿ ਅੱਲਾਹ
column5: The splendor of Akaalpurakh radiates in your home-like-heart in such a way,

column2: ਤਾਫ਼ਤ ਚੂੰ ਬਰ ਆਸਮਾਂ ਰਖ਼ਸ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਮਾਹ॥੪੦੦॥
column5: Just like the bright moon shines (during moonlit nights) in the sky. (400)

column2: ਅੰਦਰੂਨਿ ਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਤਰ ਬੀਨਾ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: It is the Provident who enables you to see through your tearful eyes,

column2: ਬਰ ਜ਼ਬਾਨਤ ਹੁਕਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਗੋਯਾ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ॥੪੦੧॥
column5: And, it is His command that speaks from your tongue. (401)

column2: ਈਂ ਵਜੂਦਤ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਅਜ਼ ਨੂਰਿ ਹੱਕ ਅਸਤ
column5: This body of yours is radiant because of the splendor of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਅਜ਼ ਨੂਰਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਮੁਤਲਿਕ ਅਸਤ॥੪੦੨॥
column5: This entire world is shining with His refulgence. (402)

column2: ਲੇਕ ਵਾਕਿਫ਼ ਨੀਸਤੀ ਅਜ਼ ਹਾਲਿ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼
column5: But you are not aware of your inside situation and condition,

column2: ਰੂਜ਼ੋ ਸ਼ਬ ਹੈਰਾਨੀ ਅਜ਼ ਅਫ਼ਆਲਿ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼॥੪੦੩॥
column5: You are distraught day and night because of your own actions and deeds. (403)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਤੁਰਾ ਮਹਿਰਮ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: The perfect true Guru makes you Waaheguru's confidant,

column2: ਦਰਦਿ ਰੇਸ਼ਿ ਹਿਜ਼ਰ ਰਾ ਮਰਹਮ ਕੁਨਦ॥੪੦੪॥
column5: He provides ointment and dressing for the pain of the wounds of separation. (404)

column2: ਤਾ ਤੂ ਹਮ ਅਜ਼ ਵਾਸਿਲਾਨਿ ਊ ਸ਼ਵੀ
column5: So that you can also become one of Waaheguru's close companions,

column2: ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਦਿਲ ਗਰਦੀ ਵਾ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ਬੂ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੪੦੫॥
column5: And, you could become the master of your heart with a noble character. (405)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬਰਾਇ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਸਰ-ਗਰਦਾਂ ਸ਼ਵੀ
column5: You have ever been perplexed and confused about the Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਉਮਰ ਹਾ ਅੰਦਰ ਤਲਬ ਹੈਰਾਂ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੪੦੬॥
column5: Because, you have been troubled for ages searching for Him. (406)

column2: ਤੂ ਚਿਹ ਬਾਸ਼ੀ ਆਲਮੇ ਹੈਰਾਨਿ ਊ
column5: What to speak of you alone! The whole world is perplexed indeed for Him,

column2: ਅਰਸ਼ੋ ਕੁਰਸੀ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਸਰ-ਗਰਦਾਨਿ ਊ॥੪੦੭॥
column5: This sky and the fourth firmament are all distressed about Him. (407)

column2: ਚਰਖ਼ ਮੀ ਗਰਦਦ ਬਗਿਰਦਿ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਊ
column5: This sky revolves around Him for the reason

column2: ਦਾਰਦ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਫ਼ਰਖ਼ੰਦਾ ਖ਼ੂ॥੪੦੮॥
column5: That it, too, can adopt noble virtues because of its fondness for Him. (408)

column2: ਜੁਮਲਾ ਹੈਰਾਨੰਦ ਸਰ-ਗਰਦਾਨਿ ਊ
column5: The people of the whole world are amazed and confused about Waaheguru,

column2: ਚੂੰ ਗਦਾ ਜੋਇਦ ਊ ਰਾ ਕੂ ਬ-ਕੂ॥੪੦੯॥
column5: Just like the beggars are looking for Him from street to street. (409)

column2: ਬਾਦਸ਼ਾਹਿ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਦਰ ਦਿਲ ਅਸਤ
column5: The king of both the worlds is abiding inside the heart,

column2: ਲੈਕਨ ਈਂ ਆਗ਼ਿਸ਼ਤਾਇ ਆਬੋ ਗਿੱਲ ਅਸਤ॥੪੧੦॥
column5: But this body of our is mired in water and mud. (410)

column2: ਦਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਤੂ ਨਕਸ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਚੂੰ ਨਕਸ਼ ਬਸਤ
column5: When the True Image of Waaheguru definitely made a stern image and abode in your heart.

column2: ਜੁਮਲਾ ਨਫ਼ਸਿ ਸ਼ੌਕ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਐ ਹੱਕ-ਪ੍ਰਸਤ॥੪੧੧॥
column5: Then O devotee of True Akaalpurakh! Your entire family, out of elation and euphoria, will be transformed itself into His image. (411)

column2: ਨਕਸ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਯਾਅਨੀ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: The form of Akaalpurakh is really the symbol of His Naam,

column2: ਆਬਿ ਹੈਵਾਂ ਰਾ ਬਨੋਸ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਜਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ॥੪੧੨॥
column5: Therefore, you should drink the nectar from the cup of Truth. (412)

column2: ਆਂ ਕਿ ਊ ਰਾ ਜੁਸਤਮ ਅਜ਼ ਹਰ ਖ਼ਾਨਾਇ
column5: The Lord that I have been looking for from home to home,

column2: ਯਾਫ਼ਤਮ ਨਾਗਾਹ ਦਰ ਕਾਸ਼ਾਨਾਇ॥੪੧੩॥
column5: Suddenly, I discovered Him inside my own home (body). (413)

column2: ਈਂ ਤੁਫ਼ੈਲਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਵਦ
column5: This blessing is from the true and perfect Guru,

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਮੀ ਖ਼ਾਹੀ ਅਜ਼ੋ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੪੧੪॥
column5: Whatever I wanted or needed, I could get that from Him. (414)

column2: ਈਂ ਮੁਰਾਦਿ ਦਿਲ ਕਸੇ ਬੇ ਆਂ ਨ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: No one else can fulfill his heart's desire,

column2: ਹਰ ਗਦਾਇ ਦੌਲਤਿ ਸੁਲਤਾਂ ਨ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ॥੪੧੫॥
column5: And, every beggar is not able to acquire royal riches. (415)

column2: ਨਾਮ ਬੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਮਿਆ ਵਰ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ
column5: Do not bring any name other than that of the Guru on your tongue,

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਦਿਹਦ ਅਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਂ॥੪੧੬॥
column5: In fact, a perfect Guru alone can give us the correct whereabouts of Akaalpurakh. (416)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਹਰ ਚੀਜ਼ ਮੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਬਸੇ
column5: There may be numerous teachers and instructors for every item (in this world),

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਕੁਜਾ ਯਾਬਦ ਕਸੇ॥੪੧੭॥
column5: However, when can one meet a perfect Guru? (417)

column2: ਆਂ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਪਾਕ ਦਿਲ ਰਾ ਕਾਮ ਦਾਦ
column5: The chaste Waaheguru fulfilled the keen desire of my heart,

column2: ਈਂ ਦਿਲਿ ਬਿਸ਼ਕਸਤਾ ਰਾ ਆਰਾਮ ਦਾਦ॥੪੧੮॥
column5: And provided succor to the heart-broken. (418)

column2: ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਹੱਕ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਵਦ
column5: To meet a perfect Guru is the real attainment of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ ਊ ਆਰਾਮਿ ਜਾਨੋ ਦਿਲ ਬਵਦ॥੪੧੯॥
column5: Because it is him (Him) who can bestow tranquility to the mind and the soul. (419)

column2: ਅੱਵਲਨ ਐ ਦਿਲ ਫ਼ਨਾਇ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਸ਼ੌ
column5: O my heart! Firstly, you must get rid of your vanity and ego,

column2: ਤਾ ਬਯਾਬੀ ਰਾਹਿ ਹੱਕ ਦਰ ਕੂਇ ਓ॥੪੨੦॥
column5: So that you could get the correct direction from His street to the path of Truth. (420)

column2: ਵਾਕਿਫ਼ ਅਰ ਅਜ਼ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਸ਼ਵੀ
column5: If you can get to know the perfect and complete true Guru,

column2: ਬੇ ਤਕੱਲਫ਼ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਈਂ ਦਿਲ ਸ਼ਵੀ॥੪੨੧॥
column5: Then, you can be the master of this heart without any (ritual) problems. (421)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਊ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਫ਼ਨਾਇ ਊ ਨ ਕਰਦ
column5: Whosoever has not been able to eradicate his self ego,

column2: ਹੱਕ ਮਰ ਊ ਰਾ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਂ ਨ ਕਰਦ॥੪੨੨॥
column5: The Akaalpurakh does not divulge His mysteries to him. (422)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਹਸਤ ਆਂ ਅੰਦਰੂਨਿ ਖ਼ਾਨਾ ਅਸਤ
column5: Whatever is there, is inside the house, the human body,

column2: ਸੈਰ ਕੁਨ ਦਰ ਕਿਸ਼ਤਿ ਦਿਲ ਈਂ ਦਾਨਾ ਹਸਤ॥੪੨੩॥
column5: You should walk around your heart's field of crops; the grain of enlightenment is there only inside it. (423)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਚੂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਰਾਹਨੁਮਾ
column5: When the complete and perfect true Guru becomes your guide and mentor,

column2: ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ ਗਰਦੀ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ॥੪੨੪॥
column5: You would then become very well informed and conversant about your Waaheguru. (424)

column2: ਗਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਤੂ ਜਾਨਬਿ ਹੱਕ ਆਰਦਤ
column5: If your heart can be motivated and inspired towards the Omnipotent,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬੁਨਿ ਹਰ ਮੂਇ ਹੱਕ ਮੀ ਬਾਰਦਤ॥੪੨੫॥
column5: Then, there would be a pouring showers of His Naam in every hair of your body. (425)

column2: ਹਮ ਦਰੀਂ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਬ-ਯਾਬੀ ਕਾਮ ਰਾ
column5: Then, all your desires in this world would be fulfilled,

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕ ਬਰ ਸਰ ਕੁਨ ਗ਼ਮਿ ਅੱਯਾਮ ਰਾ॥੪੨੬॥
column5: And, you will bury all the worries and apprehensions of the time. (426)

column2: ਬੀਰੂੰ ਅਜ਼ ਜਿਸਮਿ ਤੂ ਨਭਬਵਦ ਹੀਚ ਚੀਜ਼
column5: Nothing exists in this world outside your body,

column2: ਯੱਕ ਦਮੇ ਹਮ ਖ਼ੇਸ਼ਤਨ ਤਾ ਕੁਨ ਤਮੀਜ਼॥੪੨੭॥
column5: You should introspect just for a moment to realize your own self. (427)

column2: ਤਾ ਬ-ਯਾਬੀ ਈਂ ਸਆਦਤ ਰਾ ਮਦਾਮ
column5: You will be bestowed with the Waaheguru's true boon for ever,

column2: ਗਰ ਬਿਦਾਨੀ ਹੱਕ ਕੁਦਾਮੋ ਮਨ ਕੁਦਾਮ॥੪੨੮॥
column5: If you can appreciate (the stark distinction) as to who you are and who God is? (428)

column2: ਮਨ ਚਿਹ ਜ਼ੱਰਾ ਮੁਸ਼ਤੇ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਗ਼ਰੀਬ
column5: Who am I? I am just one particle of one fistful of dust of top-layer,

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਦੌਲਤ ਜ਼ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਸ਼ੁਦ ਨਸੀਬ॥੪੨੯॥
column5: All this blessing, due to my good fortune, was bestowed upon me by my true Guru. (429)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਪਾਕ ਰਾ
column5: Great is the true Guru who has blessed me with Akaalpurakh's sacred Naam,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਕਰਮ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ੀਦ ਮੁਸ਼ਤਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਰਾ॥੪੩੦॥
column5: With his enormous kindness and compassion to this fistful of dust. (430)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਚੂ ਮਾ ਤੀਰਾ ਦਿਲਾਂ
column5: Great is the true Guru who has, blind minds like mine,

column2: ਕਰਦ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਦਰ ਜ਼ਮੀਨੋਂ ਆਸਮਾਂ॥੪੩੧॥
column5: Made them refulgent on both the earth and the sky. (431)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਦਿਲ ਰਾ ਸ਼ੌਕ ਦਾਦ
column5: Great is the true Guru who has blessed my heart with a keen desire and fondness,

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਬੰਦਿ ਦਿਲ ਕੁਸ਼ਾਦ॥੪੩੨॥
column5: Blessed is the true Guru who has broken all the limitations and shackles of my heart. (432)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਬਾ ਹੱਕ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ
column5: Great is the true Guru, Guru Gobind Singh, who introduced me to the Lord,

column2: ਕਰਦ ਫ਼ਾਰਿਗ਼ ਅਜ਼ ਗ਼ਮਿ ਰੰਜੋ ਬਲਾ॥੪੩੩॥
column5: And, got me redeemed from worldly worries and griefs. (433)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਉਮਰਿ ਜਾਵਿਦਾਂ
column5: Great is the true Guru who has blessed persons like me the eternal life only

column2: ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ਦ ਅਜ਼ ਨਾਮਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾਇ ਬੇ-ਨਿਸ਼ਾਂ॥੪੩੪॥
column5: because of the Naam of the Untraceable Akaalpurakh. (434)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਕਿ ਊ ਅਜ਼ ਕਤਰਾ ਆਬ
column5: Great is the perfect and true Guru, who has

column2: ਕਰਦ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਹਮਚੂ ਮਾਹੋ ਆਫ਼ਤਾਬ॥੪੩੫॥
column5: Illuminated just a drop of water like the brightness of moon and the sun. (435)

column2: ਐ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਜ਼ਹੇ ਇਹਸਾਨਿ ਊ
column5: Blessed is that true Guru and blessed are his numerous boons and bestowals,

column2: ਸਦ ਹਜ਼ਾਰਾਂ ਹਮਚੂ ਮਨ ਕੁਰਬਾਨਿ ਊ॥੪੩੬॥
column5: For whom millions of people like me are willing to sacrifice themselves. (436)

column2: ਦਰ ਜ਼ਮੀਨੋ ਆਸਮਾਂ ਨਾਮਸ਼ ਬਵਦ
column5: His Naam pervades and is prevalent over the earth and the sky,

column2: ਹਰ ਮੁਰੀਦੇ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਕਾਮਸ਼ ਬਵਦ॥੪੩੭॥
column5: It is Him who fulfills all the strong desires of His disciples. (437)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਜ਼ਿ ਗੁਫ਼ਤੋ ਗੂਇ ਊ
column5: Whosoever is elated and satisfied on hearing His conversation,

column2: ਹੱਕ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਊ ਰਾ ਰੂ-ਬਰੂ॥੪੩੮॥
column5: Take it that he will be face to face with the Almighty for ever. (438)

column2: ਹੱਕ ਹਮੇਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਊ ਰਾ ਦਰ ਹਜ਼ੂਰ
column5: The Akaalpurakh is always present before him,

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਮਰ ਊ ਰਾ ਦਰ ਸਦੂਰ॥੪੩੯॥
column5: And, the meditation and remembrance of Waaheguru always abides in his heart. (439)

column2: ਗਰ ਹਜ਼ੂਰੀ ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਬਾਇਦ ਬ-ਤੌ
column5: If you have the yearning to be face to face to the Omnipotent,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਜ਼ੂਰਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਿਰੌ॥੪੪੦॥
column5: Then, you should try to be face to face to the perfect and complete Guru. (440)

column2: ਸੂਰਤਿ ਹੱਕ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਵਦ
column5: A perfect Guru is, in fact, the image of the Omnipresent,

column2: ਦੀਦਨਸ਼ ਆਰਾਮਿ ਜਾਨੋ ਦਿਲ ਬਵਦ॥੪੪੧॥
column5: A glimpse of such a perfect Guru provides succor and serenity to the heart and soul. (441)

column2: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਹੱਕ ਮਾਅਨੀ ਅਜ਼ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਬਵਦ
column5: The perfect and true Guru is, indeed, an image of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਬਰ-ਗਰਦਦ ਅਜ਼ਾਂ ਮੁਰਤਦ ਬਵਦ॥੪੪੨॥
column5: Anyone who has turned himself away from him, was discarded and thrown away like a trash. (442)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਹੱਕ ਨਭਗੁਫ਼ਤ
column5: The perfect and the true Guru does not utter anything but the truth,

column2: ਦੁੱਰਿ ਈਂ ਮਾਅਨੀ ਬਗੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਆਂ ਨ ਗੁਫ਼ਤ॥੪੪੩॥
column5: No one else but him has been able to pierce the pearl of this spiritual idea. (443)

column2: ਤਾ ਕੁਜਾ ਸ਼ੁਕਰੇ ਜ਼ ਇਹਸਾਨਸ਼ ਕੁਨਮ
column5: How far and how much can I thank Him for His bestowals ?

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਆਇਦ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ ਈਂ ਮੁਗ਼ਤਨਮ॥੪੪੪॥
column5: Whatever comes on my lips and tongue, I would consider that as a boon. (444)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਗ਼ਿਲਾਜ਼ਤਿ ਦਿਲ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਚੂੰ ਪਾਕ ਕਰਦ
column5: When Akaalpurakh cleansed the heart from filth, profanity and muck

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬ-ਈਂ ਇਦਰਾਕ ਕਰਦ॥੪੪੫॥
column5: The complete and perfect Guru bestowed the good sense to it. (445)

column2: ਵਰਨਾ ਈਂ ਰਾਹਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਕੈ ਜਾਨਦੇ
column5: Otherwise, how could we find out the true path of God ?

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਕਿਤਾਬਿ ਹੱਕ ਸਬਕ ਕੈ ਖ਼ਾਨਦੇ॥੪੪੬॥
column5: And, when and how could we learn a lesson from the book of Truth? (446)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਚੂੰ ਅਜ਼ ਤੁਫ਼ੈਲਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਅਸਤ
column5: If all this is the bestowal of the true Guru out of his compassion and kindness,

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿਹ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦ ਰਾ ਨਾ-ਦਾਨਦ ਮੁਰਤਦ ਅਸਤ॥੪੪੭॥
column5: Then, those who do not know or appreciate the Guru, are, indeed, apostates. (447)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਇਲਾਜਿ ਦਿਲ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: The perfect and true Guru removes the maladies of the heart,

column2: ਕਾਮਿ ਦਿਲ ਅੰਦਰ ਦਿਲਤ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਕੁਨਦ॥੪੪੮॥
column5: In fact, all your cravings are fulfilled within your heart itself (448)

column2: ਨਬਜ਼ਿ ਦਿਲ ਚੂੰ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਸ਼ਨਾਖ਼ਤ
column5: When the perfect Guru correctly diagnosed the pulse of the heart,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਇ ਉਮਰ ਰਾ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਸ਼ਨਾਖ਼ਤ॥੪੪੯॥
column5: Then the life attained the purpose of its existence. (449)

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਇ ਉਮਰ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਮੀ ਸ਼ਵਦ
column5: Because of the perfect and true Guru, the human being gets an eternal life,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਤੁਫ਼ੈਲਸ਼ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਦਿਲ ਮੀ ਸ਼ਵਦ॥੪੫੦॥
column5: With his grace and kindness, one attains the mastery and control of the heart. (450)

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਬਰਾਇ ਆਂ ਕਿ ਈਂ ਪੈਦਾ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ
column5: This human being came into this world only to attain the Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਦਰ ਫ਼ਿਰਾਕਸ਼ ਵਾਲਾ ਓ ਸ਼ੈਦਾ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ॥੪੫੧॥
column5: And, keeps wandering in His separation as a lunatic. (451)

column2: ਈਂ ਮਤਾਅ ਅੰਦਰ ਦੁਕਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਵਦ
column5: This true deal is available only at the shop of Truth,

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਬਵਦ॥੪੫੨॥
column5: The complete and perfect Guru is the symbolic image of Akaalpurakh Himself. (452)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਦਿਹਦ ਪਾਕੀ ਤੁਰਾ
column5: The perfect Guru, reference here is to Guru Gobind Singh Ji, bestows chastity and holiness upon you;

column2: ਮੀ ਕਸ਼ਦ ਅਜ਼ ਚਾਹਿ ਗ਼ਮਨਾਕੀ ਤੁਰਾ॥੪੫੩॥
column5: And, drags you out of the well (depths) of grief and sorrow. (453)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਇਲਾਜਿ ਦਿਲ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: The perfect and true Guru removes the maladies of the heart,

column2: ਈਂ ਮੁਰਾਦਿ ਦਿਲ ਬਦਿਲ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਕੁਨਦ॥੪੫੪॥
column5: With which, all the heart's desires are achieved (fulfilled) within the heart itself. (454)

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ ਅਜਬ ਦੌਲਤ ਬਵਦ
column5: The company of noble souls is, by itself, an extraordinary wealth,

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਮੌਕੂਫ਼ ਬਰ ਸੁਹਬਤ ਬਵਦ॥੪੫੫॥
column5: All this (these) is attained only through the support of the noble persons' company. (455)

column2: ਐ ਅਜ਼ੀਜ਼ਿ ਮਨ ਸ਼ਿਨੌ ਅਜ਼ ਮਨ ਸਖ਼ੁਨ
column5: O my dear ! Please listen to what I have to say,

column2: ਤਾ ਬਯਾਬੀ ਰਾਹ ਅੰਦਰ ਜਾਨੋ ਤਨ॥੪੫੬॥
column5: So that you can realize the secret and mystery of life and the body. (456)

column2: ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਦੂਸਤਦਾਰ
column5: You should become friendly to the seekers of the Waaheguru's devotees,

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਸ਼ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ ਹਰਫ਼ੇ ਮਯਾਰ॥੪੫੭॥
column5: And should not bring any word other than the meditation of the Naam of Akaalpurakh on your tongue and lips. (457)

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕ ਸ਼ੌ ਮਰਦਾਨਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਬਾਸ਼
column5: You should become and act like dust, i.e., be humble, and become the dust of the passage of the holy men,

column2: ਨੇ ਪਏ ਦੁਨੀਆਇ ਦੂੰ ਗ਼ਮਨਾਕ ਬਾਸ਼॥੪੫੮॥
column5: And, do not worry about this frivolous and undignified world. (458)

column2: ਗ਼ਰ ਤੂ ਖ਼ਾਨੀ ਨੁਸਖ਼ਾ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ
column5: If you can read the book of glory of romance,

column2: ਮੀਸ਼ਵੀ ਸਰ ਦਫ਼ਤਰਿ ਦੀਵਾਨਿ ਇਸ਼ਕ॥੪੫੯॥
column5: Then, you could become the address and headline of the book of love. (459)

column2: ਇਸ਼ਕਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਮਰ ਤੁਰਾ ਮੌਲਾ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: Love for Waaheguru transforms you into the very image of Waaheguru Himself,

column2: ਦਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਮਿਹਤਰੋ ਔਲਾ ਕੁਨਦ॥੪੬੦॥
column5: And, makes you lofty and famous in both the worlds. (460)

column2: ਯਾ ਇਲਾਹੀ ਈਂ ਦਿਲਮ ਰਾ ਸ਼ੌਕ ਦਿਹ
column5: O my Akaalpurakh! Kindly bless this heart of mine with Your devotion and love,

column2: ਲਜ਼ਤੇ ਅਜ਼ ਸ਼ੌਕਿ ਖ਼ਾਸੋ ਜ਼ੌਕ ਦਿਹ॥੪੬੧॥
column5: And, also bestow upon me the savor of the euphoria of Your love. (461)

column2: ਤਾ ਬ-ਯਾਦਤ ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰਦ ਰੂਜ਼ੋ ਸ਼ਬਮ
column5: So that, I can spend my days and nights remembering You,

column2: ਦਿਹ ਰਹਾਈ ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਬੰਦਿ ਗ਼ਮ॥੪੬੨॥
column5: And, You bless me with redemption from the shackles of worries and griefs of this world. (462)

column2: ਦੌਲਤੇ ਆਂ ਦਿਹ ਕਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਪਾਇਦਾਰ
column5: Kindly bless me with such a treasure that should be permanent and everlasting,

column2: ਸੁਹਬਤੇ ਆਂ ਦਿਹ ਕਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਗ਼ਮਗ਼ੁਸਾਰ॥੪੬੩॥
column5: Also bless me with the company (of such persons) that can dispel all my worries and griefs. (463)

column2: ਨੀਅਤੇ ਆਂ ਦਿਹ ਕਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਹੱਕ ਗੁਜ਼ਾਰ
column5: Kindly bless me with such intentions and purposes that should worship the Truth,

column2: ਹਿੰਮਤੇ ਆਂ ਦਿਹ ਕਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਜਾਂ ਨਿਸਾਰ॥੪੬੪॥
column5: Kindly bless me with such a courage and fortitude that I should be prepared to sacrifice my life to venture to go on the road to God. (464)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਦਾਰਦ ਦਰ ਰਹਿਤ ਕੁਰਬਾਂ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: Whatever there is, he should be prepared to sacrifice on Your account,

column2: ਜਾਨੋ ਦਿਲ ਕੁਰਬਾਂ ਰਹਿ ਸੁਬਹਾਂ ਕੁਨਦ॥੪੬੫॥
column5: Should also be prepared to sacrifice both the life and the soul on the path of Akaalpurakh. (465)

column2: ਦੀਦਾ-ਅਮ ਰਾ ਲੱਜ਼ਤਿ ਦੀਦਾਰ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼
column5: Bless my eyes with the sweet taste of Your glimpse,

column2: ਸੀਨਾ-ਅਮ ਰਾ ਮਖ਼ਜ਼ਨਿ ਅਸਰਾਰ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼॥੪੬੬॥
column5: And, bless my heart with the treasures of Your mysteries and secrets. (466)

column2: ਈਂ ਦਿਲਿ ਬਿਰਯਾਨਿ ਮਾ ਰਾ ਸ਼ੌਕ ਦਿਹ
column5: Kindly bless our charred hearts with the fervor (of Your love)

column2: ਦਰ ਗ਼ੁਲਏਮ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਰਾ ਤੌਕ ਦਿਹ॥੪੬੭॥
column5: And, bless us with a strap (dog-collar) of meditation in our necks. (467)

column2: ਹਿਜਰਿ ਮਾ ਰਾ ਆਰਜ਼ੂਇ ਵਸਲ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼
column5: Please bless our separation (from you) with a strong yearning to meet with You,

column2: ਈਂ ਖ਼ਿਜ਼ਾਨਿ ਜਿਸਮਿ ਮਾ ਰਾ ਫ਼ਜਲ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼॥੪੬੮॥
column5: And, bestow Your beneficence on the autumn-like-state of our bodies. (468)

column2: ਹਰ ਸਰਿ ਮੂਏਮ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਂ ਕੁਨ ਅਜ਼ ਕਰਮ
column5: Kindly transform, with your Benefaction, each hair on my body into a tongue,

column2: ਤਾ ਬਗੋਏਮ ਵਸਫ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਰਾ ਦਮ ਬਦਮ॥੪੬੯॥
column5: So that I could keep uttering and singing Your kudos in my every breath after breath. (469)

column2: ਵਸਫ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਬੀਰੰ ਬਵਦ ਅਜ਼ ਗੁਫ਼ਤਗੂ
column5: The eclat and glories of Akaalpurakh are beyond any words or conversation,

column2: ਈਂ ਹਦੀਸਿ ਸ਼ਾਹ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕੂ ਬ ਕੈ॥੪੭੦॥
column5: This discourse and tale of the true King can be heard in every street after street. (470)

column2: ਮਾਅਨੀਇ ਈਂ ਕੂ ਬ-ਕੂ ਦਾਨੀ ਕਿ ਚੀਸਤ
column5: Do you know as to what is the essence of this street?

column2: ਹਮਦ ਗੋ ਦੀਗਰ ਮਗੋ ਈਨਸਤ ਜ਼ੀਸਤ॥੪੭੧॥
column5: You should utter only His approbations and nothing else. This is life. (471)

column2: ਜ਼ੀਸਤਨ ਦਰ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਊਲਾ ਬਵਦ
column5: It is superb to live with His constant meditation,

column2: ਗਰ ਚਿਹ ਸਰ ਤਾ ਪਾ ਹਮਾ ਮੂਲਾ ਬਵਦ॥੪੭੨॥
column5: Even though we may be the masters of the body from head to toes. (472)

column2: ਗਰ ਦਿਹਦ ਤੌਫ਼ੀਕ ਫ਼ਜਲਿ ਜ਼ੁਲਜਲਾਲ
column5: If the all Truth Akaalpurakh blesses someone with courage and capability,

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਅਜ਼ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਮਾਲ॥੪੭੩॥
column5: Then that person can earn laurels because of meditation. (473)

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਮਾਲਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ
column5: Meditation is the marvel and cornerstone of being a human being,

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ॥੪੭੪॥
column5: And, meditation is the real sign of being alive. (474)

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਇ ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਈਂ ਬੰਦਗੀਸਤ
column5: The (purpose of) life of a human being is indeed the meditation of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀਇ ਹੱਕ ਕਿ ਐਨ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀਸਤ॥੪੭੫॥
column5: The remembrance of Waaheguru is the real (purpose of) life. (475)

column2: ਗਰ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਮੀ-ਬਾਇਦਤ
column5: If you are looking for some signs and symbols of life for yourself,

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀਇ ਹੱਕ ਤੁਰਾ ਮੀ-ਸ਼ਾਇਦਤ॥੪੭੬॥
column5: Then, it is absolutely appropriate for you to keep on meditating(on the Naam of Akaalpurakh). (476)

column2: ਤਾ ਤਵਾਨੀ ਬੰਦਾ ਸ਼ੌ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਮਬਾਸ਼
column5: As far as possible, you should become an humble person like a servant, and not an arrogant master,

column2: ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ ਜੁਜ਼ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਨਬਵਦ ਤਲਾਸ਼॥੪੭੭॥
column5: A person should not be looking for anything in this world except meditation of the Almighty. (477)

column2: ਈਂ ਵਜੂਦਿ ਖ਼ਾਕ ਪਾਕ ਅਜ਼ ਬੰਦਗੀਸਤ
column5: This body of dust becomes sacred only due to the remembrance of the Provident,

column2: ਗੁਫ਼ਤਗੂਹਾਇ ਦਿਗਰ ਸ਼ਰਮਿੰਦਗੀਸਤ॥੪੭੮॥
column5: To get involved in any conversation other than the meditation will be nothing but an utter shame. (478)

column2: ਬੰਦਗੀ ਕੁਨ ਜ਼ਾਂ ਕਿ ਊ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਕਬੂਲ
column5: You should meditate so that you become acceptable in His court,

column2: ਬਿਗੁਜ਼ਰ ਅਜ਼ ਖ਼ੁਦ-ਬੀਨੀ ਓ ਤਰਜ਼ਿ ਜ਼ਹੂਲ॥੪੭੯॥
column5: And, give up the pattern of self ego and the way of life of that of an apostate. (479)

column2: ਦਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਸਾਹਿਬਿ-ਦਿਲਾਂ ਆਇਦ ਪਸੰਦ
column5: The meditation is extremely pleasing to the heart of the Master of all hearts,

column2: ਰੁਤਬਾ-ਅਤ ਗਰਦਦ ਅਜ਼ਾਂ ਹਰਦਮ ਬੁਲੰਦ॥੪੮੦॥
column5: Your status in this world remains high at all times only because of meditation. (480)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਕਿ ਊ ਅਰਸ਼ਾਦ ਕਰਦ
column5: The perfect and true Guru said like this,

column2: ਈਂ ਦਿਲਤ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਆਬਾਦ ਕਰਦ॥੪੮੧॥
column5: He has inhabited you desolate heart with the remembrance of Waaheguru. (481)

column2: ਈਂ ਹਮਾ ਅਰਸ਼ਾਦ ਦਰ ਦਿਲ ਨਕਸ਼-ਬੰਦ
column5: You should etch this command of the perfectly true Guru in your heart,

column2: ਤਾ ਸ਼ਵੀ ਦਰ ਹਰ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਸਰ ਬੁਲੰਦ॥੪੮੨॥
column5: So that you can have your head high in both the worlds. (482)

column2: ਈਂ ਵਜੂਦਿ ਮਿਸ ਤੁਰਾ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ ਤਿਲਾ
column5: This command of the perfect and true Guru transforms your body of copper into that of gold,

column2: ਈਂ ਤਿਲਾ ਮਾਅਲੂਮ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ॥੪੮੩॥
column5: And, this gold is realized only through the memory of Akaalpurakh. (483)

column2: ਆਂ ਤਿਲਾ ਫ਼ਾਨੀ ਵਾ ਸਦ ਮੌਜ਼ਿ ਬਲਾ
column5: This materialistic gold is destructible and is the root cause and whirlpool of numerous problems and conflicts,

column2: ਈਂ ਤਿਲਾ ਬਾਕੀ ਚੂ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ ਕਿਬਰੀਆ॥੪੮੪॥
column5: The gold of meditation, however, like the Entity of Omnipresent and True Waaheguru is permanent. (484)

column2: ਦੌਲਤ ਅੰਦਰ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਪਾਇ ਮੁਕਬਲਾਂ
column5: The (true) wealth is in the dust of the feet of the noble and accepted souls,

column2: ਦੌਲਤੇ ਕਾਂ ਰਾ ਨਮੀ ਆਯਦ ਜ਼ਿਆਂ॥੪੮੫॥
column5: It is such a true wealth that it is above and beyond any damage or loss. (485)

column2: ਆਕਬਤ ਦੀਦੀ ਖ਼ਿਜ਼ਾਂ ਆਵੁਰਦ ਬਹਾਰ
column5: You must have noticed that every spring brings in the autumn,

column2: ਵਰਨਾ ਦਰ ਦੁਨਿਆ ਹਮਾ ਫ਼ਸਲਿ ਬਹਾਰ॥੪੮੬॥
column5: Although the spring keeps coming into this world again and again. (486)

column2: ਈਂ ਬਹਾਰਿ ਤਾਜ਼ਾ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਤਾ ਅਬਦ
column5: However, this meditational form of spring remains fresh and new till the doomsday,

column2: ਯਾ ਇਲਾਹੀ ਦੂਰ ਦਾਰ ਅਜ਼ ਂਚਸ਼ਮਿ ਬਦ॥੪੮੭॥
column5: O Akaalpurakh! Kindly keep the influence of the evil eye far away from this spring . (487)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਪਾਇ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਸੁਰਮਾ ਯਾਫ਼ਤ
column5: Anyone who happens to obtain the collyrium of the dust of the feet of the holy persons,

column2: ਬਰ ਰੁਖ਼ਸ਼ ਤਹਿਕੀਕ ਨੂਰਿ ਮਿਹਰ ਤਾਫ਼ਤ॥੪੮੮॥
column5: Rest assured that his face will shine like the brilliance and radiance of the divine sun. (488)

column2: ਆਰਿਫ਼ਿ ਅੱਲਾਹ ਦਰ ਦੁਨੀਆਂ ਬਵਦ
column5: Even though spiritually enlightened person lives in this world,

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਤਾਲਿਬਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਬਵਦ॥੪੮੯॥
column5: He, in fact, is always a seeker-devotee of the Waaheguru. (489)

column2: ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਮੌਲਾ ਦਮ ਬ-ਦਮ ਦਰ ਜਾਨਿ ਊ
column5: He meditates and describes His virtues every breath of his life,

column2: ਆਇਤਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਦਰ ਸ਼ਾਨਿ ਊ॥੪੯੦॥
column5: And, he recites verses of His Naam every moment in His honor. (490)

column2: ਹਰ ਨਫ਼ਸ ਦਾਰੰਦ ਦਿਲ ਰਾ ਸੂਇ ਹੱਕ
column5: They keep directing their hearts and concentrating towards thoughts about Him,

column2: ਸ਼ੁਦ ਮੁਅੱਤਰ ਮਗ਼ਜ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਅਜ਼ ਬੂਇ ਹੱਕ॥੪੯੧॥
column5: They make their intellect fragrant with the aroma of the memory of Akaalpurakhb in every breath. (491)

column2: ਹਰ ਦਮੇ ਕੂ ਬਾ ਖ਼ੁਦਾ ਵਾਸਿਲ ਬਵਦ
column5: He always concentrates and is united with the Almighty at all times,

column2: ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਈਂ ਉਮਰ ਰਾ ਹਾਸਿਲ ਬਵਦ॥੪੯੨॥
column5: And, he has been able to achieve the real fruits of this life. (492)

column2: ਹਾਸਿਲਿ ਈਂ ਉਮਰ ਪੇਸ਼ਿ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਿਦ ਅਸਤ
column5: The real fruits of this life lies with the Guru,

column2: ਨਾਮਿ ਹੱਕ ਚੂੰ ਬਰ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਨਸ਼ ਵਾਰਿਦ ਅਸਤ॥੪੯੩॥
column5: And, silent repetition and meditation of His Naam is always on his tongue and the lips. (493)

column2: ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਵਦ ਦੀਦਾਰਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: The true Guru is the apparent glimpse of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਜ਼ੁਬਾਨਿਸ਼ ਬਿਸ਼ਨਵੀ ਅਸਰਾਰਿ ਹੱਕ॥੪੯੪॥
column5: Therefore, you should listen to His mysteries from his tongue. (494)

column2: ਸੂਰਤਿ ਹੱਕ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦਿ ਕਾਮਿਲ ਬਵਦ
column5: A true Guru is indeed a perfect personification of the image of God,

column2: ਨਕਸ਼ਿ ਊ ਦਾਇਮ ਦਰੂਨਿ ਦਿਲ ਬਵਦ॥੪੯੫॥
column5: And, the image of Akaalpurakh always abides in his heart. (495)

column2: ਨਕਸ਼ਿ ਊ ਦਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਕਸ ਜਾ ਕੁਨਦ
column5: When His image resides permanently in someone's heart,

column2: ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਅੰਦਰ ਦਿਲਸ਼ ਮਾਵਾ ਕੁਨਦ॥੪੯੬॥
column5: Then, just one word of Akaalpurakh settles down in the depths of his heart. (496)

column2: ਖ਼ਾਸਤਮ ਤਰਤੀਬਿ ਈਂ ਦੁੱਰ ਦਾਨਾ ਰਾ
column5: I have threaded these grains of pearls into a necklace,

column2: ਕਿ ਆਸ਼ਨਾ ਸਾਜ਼ਦ ਦਿਲਿ ਬੇਗਾਨਾ ਰਾ॥੪੯੭॥
column5: So that this arrangement may make the ignorant hearts aware of the secrets of Waaheguru. (497)

column2: ਆਬਿ ਹੈਵਾਂ ਪੁਰ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਚੂ ਜਾਮਿ ਊ
column5: (This compilation) Like a cup is filled to the brim with the divine elixir,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗੀ ਨਾਮਾ ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਜ਼ਾਂ ਨਾਮਿ ਊ॥੪੯੮॥
column5: That is why, it has been given the name 'Zindagee Naamaa'. (498)

column2: ਕਜ਼ ਤਕੱਲੁਮ ਬੂਇ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਂ ਆਇਦਸ਼
column5: From his speeches emerges the fragrance of divine knowledge,

column2: ਵਜ਼ ਦਿਲਿ ਆਲਮ ਗਿਰਾਹ ਬਿਕੁਸ਼ਾਇਦਸ਼॥੪੯੯॥
column5: With it, the knot (mysteries and suspicions) of the heart of the world is untangled. (499)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਖ਼ਾਨਦ ਅਜ਼ ਰਹਿ ਲੁਤਫ਼ੋ ਕਰਮ
column5: Whosoever recites this with the grace and compassion of Waaheguru,

column2: ਗਰਦਦਸ਼ ਦਰ ਰਾਹਿ ਇਰਫ਼ਾਂ ਮੁਹਤਰਿਮ॥੫੦੦॥
column5: He receives laurels among the enlightened persons. (500)

column2: ਹਸਤ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਿ ਆਰਿਫ਼ਾਨਿ ਪਾਕ ਰਾ
column5: This volume contains description and delineation of sacred and divine men;

column2: ਆਂ ਕਿ ਊ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਕੁਨਦ ਇਦਰਾਕ ਰਾ॥੫੦੧॥
column5: This description lightens up the intelligence and the wisdom. (501)

column2: ਨੀਸਤ ਦਰ ਵੈ ਮੁੰਦਰਜ ਐ ਬਾ-ਖ਼ਬਰ
column5: O informed person! In this volume,

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਦਿਗਰ॥੫੦੨॥
column5: there is no other word or expression except the words or expressions of remembrance and meditation of Akaalpuralkh. (502)

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਸਰਮਾਯਾ-ਇ ਰੌਸ਼ਨ ਦਿਲੀਸਤ
column5: Remembrance of Waaheguru is the treasure of the enlightened minds,

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਮਾ ਬੇ-ਹਾਸਲੀਅਤ॥੫੦੩॥
column5: Everything else other than the meditation of Waaheguru is (absolutely) useless. (503)

column2: ਹਰਫ਼ਿ ਦੀਗਰ ਨੀਸਤ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ
column5: Do not read or even look at any word or expression except the ones about meditation of the Omnipotent,

column2: ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਾਂ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਾਂ ਯਾਦਿ ਹੱਕ॥੫੦੪॥
column5: Remembrance of God, yes remembrance of God, and only remembrance of God. (504)

column2: ਯਾ ਇਲਾਹੀ ਹਰ ਦਿਲਿ ਪਜ਼ਮੁਰਦਾ ਰਾ
column5: O Akaalpurakh! Kindly make every withered and disheartened mind green and confident again,

column2: ਸਬਜ਼ ਕੁਨ ਹਰ ਖ਼ਾਤਿਰਿ ਅਫ਼ਸੁਰਦਾ ਰਾ॥੫੦੫॥
column5: And, refresh and rejuvenate every wilted and languishing mind. (505)

column2: ਯਾ ਇਲਾਹੀ ਯਾਵਰੀ ਕੁਨ ਬੰਦਾ ਰਾ
column5: O Waaheguru! Kindly help this person, yours truly,

column5: People of this world are all his creations as angels,

column5: And, his glimpse is clearly manifested on the lips of everyone. (19)

column5: Their honor and esteem, status and rank and name and imprints stay in this world forever;

column5: And, the chaste Creator bestows upon them a higher rank than others. (21)

column2: ਗੁਫ਼ਤ ਮਨ ਬੰਦਾ ਓ ਗ਼ੁਲਾਮਿ ਤੂ ਅਮ
column5: Then he said, I am Your servitor, and I am Your slave,

column2: ਖ਼ਾਕਿ ਅਕਦਾਮਿ ਖ਼ਾਸੋ ਆਮਿ ਤੂ ਅਮ॥੨੩॥

column2: ਬਾਜ਼ ਚੂੰ ਹਮਚੁਨੀਂ ਖ਼ਿਤਾਬ ਆਮਦ
column5: Thus when he addressed Him like this (in stark humility)

column2: ਮਤਵਾਤਰ ਚੁਨੀਂ ਜਵਾਬ ਆਮਦ॥੨੪॥

column5: Whatever I, the Waheeguru, desire, I do; and I do only the justice. (25)

column2: ਬ-ਹਮਾ ਸ਼ੋ ਜ਼ਿ ਵਸਫ਼ਿ ਮਨ ਗੋਯਾ॥੨੬॥
column5: And, make every one chaste and sacred through My (Akaalpurakh's) kudos. (26)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਮਾ ਜਾ ਪਨਾਹੋ ਯਾਰਿ ਤੂ ਅਮ

column2: ਯਾਵਰੀ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ੋ ਖ਼ਾਸਤਗਾਰਿ ਤੂ ਅਮ॥੨੭॥

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਤੂ ਬਰਤਰੀਂ ਦਾਨਦ

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਦਿਲੋ ਜਾਂ ਬ-ਵਸਫ਼ਿ ਮਨ ਖ਼ਾਨਦ॥੨੮॥
column5: He would, in fact, be approbating Me with his heart and soul. (28)

column2: ਮਨ ਊ ਰਾ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਨੁਮਾਇਮ ਬਾਜ਼

column2: ਐਹਦਿ ਮਨ ਸਖ਼ਤਗੀਰ ਬਰ ਕੁਨ ਸਾਜ਼॥੨੯॥
column5: And, thus make my difficult resolves and situations eased up. (29)

column2: ਗਿਰਦਿ ਆਲਮ ਬਰਆ ਵਾ ਹਾਦੀ ਸ਼ੌ
column5: You should come to this world and act like a guide and captain,

column2: ਕਿ ਜਹਾਂ ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਮਨ ਨਿਯਰਜ਼ਦ ਜੌ॥੩੦॥
column5: Because this world is not worth even a grain of barley without Me, the Akaalpurakh. (30)

column2: ਦਰ ਹਕੀਕਤ ਮਨਮ ਚੂ ਰਾਹ-ਨੁਮਾ
column5: In reality, when I am your guide and steer,

column2: ਤੂ ਜਹਾਂ ਰਾ ਬਪਾਇ ਖ਼ੁਦ ਪੈਮਾ॥੩੧॥
column5: Then, you should traverse the journey of this world with your own feet. (31)

column2: ਹਰ ਕਿ ਰਾ ਖ਼ਾਹਮ ਵ ਸ਼ਵਮ ਹਾਦੀ
column5: Whomsoever I like and I show him the direction in this world,

column2: ਅਜ਼ ਤੂ ਦਰ ਦਿਲ ਦਰ-ਆਰਮਸ਼ ਸਾਦੀ॥੩੨॥
column5: Then, for his sake, I bring elation and happiness in his heart. (32)

column2: ਵਾਂ ਕਿ ਗੁਮਰਾਹ ਸਾਜ਼ਮਸ਼ ਜ਼ਿ ਗਜ਼ਬ
column5: Whomsoever I will misdirect and put him on a wrong track out of My indignation for him,

column2: ਨਭਰਸਦ ਅਜ਼ ਹਦਾਇਤਿ ਤੂ ਬ-ਰੱਬ॥੩੩॥
column5: He will not be able to reach Me, the Akaalpurakh, in spite of your advice and counsel. (33)

column2: ਸ਼ੁਦਾ ਗੁਨਰਾਹ ਆਲਮੇ ਬੇਮਨ
column5: This world is being misdirected and strayed without me,

column2: ਸਾਹਿਰਾਂ ਗਸ਼ਤਾ ਅੰਦ ਜਾਦੂਇ ਮਨ॥੩੪॥
column5: My sorcery has become the sorcerer himself. (34)

column2: ਮੁਰਦਗਾਂ ਰਾ ਕੁਨੰਦ ਜ਼ਿੰਦਾ ਹਮੀ
column5: My charms and spells bring the dead back alive,

column2: ਜ਼ਿੰਦਗਾਂ ਰਾ ਬਜਾਂ ਕੁਸ਼ੰਦਾ ਹਮੀ॥੩੫॥
column5: And, those who are living (in sin) kill them. (35)

column2: ਆਤਿਸ਼ੇ ਰਾ ਕੁਨੰਦ ਆਬ ਵਸ਼
column5: My charms transform the 'fire' into ordinary water,

column2: ਬਰ ਸਰੇ ਆਬ ਜ਼ਨੰਦ ਆਤਿਸ਼॥੩੬॥
column5: And, with the ordinary water, they extinguish and cool off the fires. (36)

column2: ਹਰ ਚਿਹ ਖ਼ਾਹੰਦ ਮੀ-ਕੁਨੰਦ ਹਮਾਂ
column5: My charms do whatever they like;

column2: ਜੁਮਲਾ ਜਾਦੂ ਫ਼ਨ ਅੰਦ ਬਰ ਸਾਮਾਂ॥੩੭॥
column5: And, they mystify with their spell all material and non-material things. (37)

column2: ਰਾਹਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਨੁਮਾ ਬ-ਸੂਇ ਮਨ
column5: Please divert their path in my direction,

column2: ਕਿ ਪਜ਼ੀਰੰਦ ਗੁਫ਼ਤਗੂਇ ਮਨ॥੩੮॥
column5: So that they can adopt and acquisition my words and message. (38)

column2: ਗ਼ੈਰ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਮ ਬ-ਜਾਦੁਏ ਨਭਰਵੰਦ
column5: They do not go for any spells except My meditation,

column2: ਜੁਜ਼ ਦਰਿ ਮਨ ਬਜਾਨਬੇ ਨਭਰਵੰਦ॥੩੯॥
column5: And, they do not move in any direction other than towards My door. (39)

column2: ਕਿ ਜ਼ਿ ਦੋਜ਼ਖ਼ ਸ਼ਵੰਦ ਰੁਸਤਾ ਹਮੇ
column5: Because they have been spared of the Hades,

column2: ਵਰਨਾ ਉਫ਼ਤੰਦ ਦਸਤ-ਬਸਤਾ ਹਮੇ॥੪੦॥
column5: Otherwise, they would fall with their hands tied down. (40)

column2: ਕਾਫ਼ ਤਾ ਕਾਫ਼ ਆਲਮੇ ਜੁਮਲਾ
column5: This whole world, from one end to the other,

column2: ਦਾਅਵਤ ਆਮੋਜ਼ੋ ਜ਼ਾਲਮੇ ਜੁਮਲਾ॥੪੧॥
column5: Is relaying the message that this world is cruel and corrupt. (41)

column2: ਰੰਜੇ ਫ਼ਰਹਤ ਜ਼ਿ ਮਨ ਨ ਮੀ-ਦਾਨੰਦ
column5: They do not realize any grief or happiness because of me,

column2: ਹਮਾ ਅਜ਼ ਗ਼ੈਰਿ ਮਨ ਪਰੇਸ਼ਾਨੰਦ॥੪੨॥
column5: And, without me, they all are confused and perplexed. (42)

column2: ਔਜੁਮਨ ਮੀ-ਕੁਨੰਦ ਵ ਅਜ਼ ਅੰਜਮ
column5: They congregate and from the stars

column2: ਬਰ ਸ਼ੁਮਾਰੰਦ ਰੂਜ਼ਿ ਸ਼ਾਦੀ ਓ ਗ਼ਮ॥੪੩॥
column5: They count the number of days of sorrow and happiness. (43)

column2: ਬਰ ਨਿਗਾਰੰਦ ਨਹਿਸੋ ਸਾਆਦ ਹਮੇ
column5: Then they pen down their good and not-so-good fortunes in their horoscopes,

column2: ਬਾਜ਼ ਗੋਇੰਦ ਕਬਲੋ ਬਾਅਦ ਹਮੇ॥੪੪॥
column5: And say, sometimes before and other times afterwards, as: (44)

column2: ਨੀਸਤ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਰਾ ਬ-ਜ਼ਿਕਰ ਇਸਤਿਕਲਾਲ
column5: They are not firm and consistent in their meditation chores,

column2: ਕਾਲ ਦਾਨੰਦ ਜੁਮਲਗਾਂ ਬੇ-ਹਾਲ॥੪੫॥
column5: And, they talk and project themselves like confused and perplexed persons. (45)

column2: ਰੂ ਨੁਮਾ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਰਾ ਸੂਇ ਫ਼ਿਕਰਮ
column5: Divert their attention and face towards My meditation

column2: ਕਿ ਨਦਾਰੰਦ ਦੋਸਤ ਜੁਜ਼ ਜ਼ਿਕਰਮ॥੪੬॥
column5: So that they will not consider anything other than discourses about Me as their friend. (46)

column2: ਤਾ ਹਮਾ ਕਾਰਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਨਿਕੋ ਸਾਜ਼ਮ
column5: So that I could set their worldly tasks on the right path,

column2: ਖ਼ਾਤਰਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਜ਼ਿ ਨੂਰ ਬਿਤਰਾਜ਼ਮ॥੪੭॥
column5: And, I could improve and refine their inclinations and tendencies with the divine glow. (47)

column2: ਮਨ ਤੁਰਾ ਆਫਰੀਦਮ ਅਜ਼ ਪਏ ਆਂ
column5: I have created you for this purpose

column2: ਕਿ ਸ਼ਵੀ ਰਹਿਨੁਮਾ ਬ-ਜੁਮਲਾ ਜਹਾਂ॥੪੮॥
column5: So that you should be the leader to steer the whole world towrads the right path. (48)

column2: ਹੁੱਬਿ ਗ਼ੈਰ ਅਜ਼ ਜ਼ਮੀਰਿ ਸ਼ਾਂ ਬਜ਼ਦਾਇ
column5: You should dispel the love for dualism from their hearts and minds,

column2: ਹਮਗਨਾਂ ਰਾ ਤੂ ਰਾਹਿ ਰਾਸਤ ਨੁਮਾਇ॥੪੯॥
column5: And, you should direct them towards the true path. (49)

column2: ਸ਼ਾਹ ਗੁਫ਼ਤਾ ਚਿ ਲਾਇਕ ਆਨਮ
column5: The Guru (Nanak) said, How can I be so capable of this stupendous task

column2: ਕਿ ਦਿਲਿ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਬਾਜ਼ ਗਰਦਾਨਮ॥੫੦॥
column5: That I should be able to divert the minds of everyone towards the true path. (50)

column2: ਮਨ ਕੁਜਾ ਵ ਚੁਨੀਂ ਕਮਾਲ ਕੁਜਾ
column5: The Guru said, I am no where near such a miracle,

column2: ਮਨ ਕਿਹ ਵ ਫ਼ੱਰੇ ਜ਼ੁਲਜਲਾਲ ਕੁਜਾ॥੫੧॥
column5: I am lowly without any virtues compared to the grandiose and exquisiteness of the form of Akaalpurakh. (51)

column2: ਲੇਕ ਹੁਕਮਤ ਕਸ਼ਮ ਬਜਾਨਿ ਬਦਿਲ
column5: However, Your command is entirely acceptable to my heart and soul,

column2: ਨਸ਼ਵਮ ਯੱਕ ਜ਼ਮਾਂ ਅਜ਼ੋ ਗ਼ਾਫ਼ਿਲ॥੫੨॥
column5: And, I shall not be negligent of Your order even for a moment. (52)

column2: ਹਾਦੀ ਓ ਰਹਿਨੁਮਾਇ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਤੂਈ
column5: Only you are the guide to lead the people to the right path, and you are the mentor for all;

column2: ਰਹਿਬਰੋ ਦਿਲ-ਗਿਰਾਮੀਇ ਜੁਮਲਾ ਤੂਈ॥੫੩॥
column5: You are the one who can lead the way and who can mold the minds of all people to your way of thinking. (53)

column2: ਸਲਤ਼ਨਤੇ ਦੋਇਮ
column5: Second Guru, Guru Angad Dev Ji

column2: ਸਲਤ਼ਨਤੇ ਦੋਇਮਸ਼, ਅੱਵਲ ਮੁਰੀਦੇ ਸਾਜਿਦ; ਵ ਆਖ਼ਿਰ, ਮੁਰਸ਼ਿਦੇ ਮਸਜੂਦਸ਼॥
column5: The second Guru, Guru Angad Dev Ji, became the first supplicating disciple of Guru Nanak Sahib. Then he transformed himself into a mentor worth supplicating to.

column2: ਵ ਫ਼ਰੋਗ਼ੇ, ਆਂ ਮਸ਼ਅ਼ਲੇ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ੋ ਈਕ਼ਾਂ; ਰੋਜ਼ ਅਫ਼ਜ਼ੂੰ, ਅਜ਼ ਵਹੂਦਸ਼॥
column5: The light emitted from the flame of his strong faith in truth and belief, due to his disposition and personality, was far greater than that of the day.

column2: ਦਰ-ਮਅ਼ਨੀ ਯਕੇ, ਵ ਦਰ ਸੂਰਤ ਦੋ; ਮਸ਼ਅ਼ਲੇ ਜਾਂ ਅਫ਼ਰੋਜ਼॥
column5: Both he and his mentor, Guru Nanak, had, in fact, one soul but outwardly were two torches to glisten the minds and the hearts of the people.

column2: ਵ ਦਰ ਹ਼ਕ਼ੀਕ਼ਤ ਅਹ਼ਦ, ਵ ਬ-ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰ ਦੋ; ਸ਼ੁਅ਼ਲਾਏ ਮਾ-ਸਵਾ-ਅਲਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਸੋਜ਼॥
column5: Intrinsically, they were one but overtly were two sparks that could singe everything but the truth.

column2: ਮਤ਼ਅ਼ਏ ਖ਼ਾਸਾਨੇ, ਬਾਰਗਾਹੇ ਅਹ਼ਦੀਯਤ
column5: The second Guru was the wealth and treasure and the leader of the special persons of the court of the Akaalpurakh.

column2: ਵ ਮਸਜੂਦੇ ਮੁਕ਼ਬਿਲਾਨੇ, ਹ਼ਜ਼ਰਤੇ ਸਮਦੀਯਤ॥
column5: He became the anchor for the people who were acceptable in the divine court.

column2: ਮੌਸੂਫ਼ੇ ਏਜ਼ਦੇ-ਮੁਤਅ਼ਾਲ
column5: He was a selected member of the heavenly court of the majestic

column2: ਵ ਬਰਗੁਜ਼ੀਦ-ਹਏ, ਦਰਗਾਹੇ ਜ਼ੁਲ-ਜਲਾਲ॥
column5: and awe-inspiring Waaheguru and had received high praises from Him.

column2: ਅੱਵਲੀਨ ਅਲਿਫ਼ੇ ਨਾਮੇ ਪਾਕਸ਼, ਅਹਾਤਾ-ਗੀਰੇ ਫ਼ੈਜ਼ੋ ਫ਼ੁਜ਼ਲਾ; ਬਰ-ਅੰਦਕ ਵ ਕਸੀਰ, ਵ ਹਰ ਅਮੀਰ ਵ ਫ਼ਕ਼ੀਰ॥
column5: The first letter of his name, 'Aliph', is the one that encompasses the virtues and blessings of high and low, rich and poor, and the king and the mendicant.

column2: ਵ ਸ਼ਮੀਮੇ ਨੂਨੇ, ਹ਼ਕ਼ੀਕ਼ਤ ਮਸ਼ਹੂਨਸ਼; ਨਵਾਜ਼ਿੰਦਹਏ, ਹਰ ਨਕ਼ੀਰੋ ਕ਼ਿਤ਼ਮੀਰ, ਵ ਅਦਨਾ ਵ ਕਬੀਰ॥
column5: The aroma of the truth-filled letter 'Noon' in his name bestows and cares for the high rulers and the low like menials.

column2: ਕਾਫ਼ੇ ਫ਼ਾਰਸੀਅਸ਼, ਕਾਮਰਾਨੇ ਈਵਾਨੇ ਅਜ਼ਲ
column5: The next letter in his name 'Gaaf' represents the traveler of the path to the eternal congregation

column2: ਵ ਗੀਹਾਨੇ ਖ਼ਦੀਵੇ, ਲਮ ਯਜ਼ਲ॥
column5: and for the world to stay in the highest spirits.

column2: ਦਾਲੇ ਆਖ਼ਿਰਸ਼, ਦਵਾਏ ਦਰਦੋ ਅਲਮ
column5: The last letter in his name, 'Daal' is the cure for all diseases

column2: ਵ ਬਰਤਰ, ਅਜ਼ ਬੇਸ਼ੋ ਕਮ॥੫੪॥
column5: and pains and is above and beyond progression and recession. (54)

column2: ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ ਜੀਉ ਸਤ
column5: Waaheguru is the Truth,

column2: ਗੁਰੂ ਅੰਗਦ ਆਂ ਮੁਰਸ਼ਦੁਲ-ਆਲਮੀਂ
column5: Guru Angad is the prophet for both the worlds,

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਫ਼ਜਲਿ ਅਹਦ ਰਹਿਮਤੁਲ ਮਜ਼ਨਬੀਨ॥੫੫॥
column5: With the grace of Akaalpurakh, he is the blessing for the sinners. (55)

column2: ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਚਿ ਬਾਸ਼ਦ ਹਜ਼ਂਰਾਂ ਜਹਾਂ
column5: What to talk of just two worlds! With his bestowals,

column2: ਤੁਫੈਲਿ ਕਰਮਹਾਇ ਓ ਕਾਮਾਰਾਂ॥੫੬॥
column5: Thousands worlds are successful to get redemption. (56)

column2: ਵਜੂਦਸ਼ ਹਮਾ ਫ਼ਜਲੋ ਫ਼ੈਜ਼ਿ ਕਰੀਮ
column5: His body is the treasure of the graces of the forgiving Waaheguru,

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਹੱਕ ਆਮਦੋ ਹਮ ਬਹੱਕ ਮੁਸਤਕੀਮ॥੫੭॥
column5: He manifested from Him and at the end, he got absorbed in Him as well. (57)

column2: ਹਮਾ ਆਸ਼ਕਾਰੋ ਨਿਹਾਂ ਜ਼ਾਹਿਰਸ਼
column5: He is always manifest whether he is visible or hidden,

column2: ਬਤੂਨੋ ਇਯਾਂ ਜੁਮਲਗੀ ਬਾਹਿਰਸ਼॥੫੮॥
column5: He is present everywhere here and there, inside and outside. (58)

column2: ਚੂ ਵੱਸਾਫ਼ਿ ਊ ਜ਼ਾਤਿ ਹੱਕ ਆਮਦਾ
column5: His admirer is, in fact, an admirer of Akaalpurakh,

column2: ਵਜੂਦਸ਼ ਜ਼ਿ ਕੁਦਸੀ ਵਰਕ ਆਮਦਾ॥੫੯॥
column5: And, his disposition is a page from the tome of the gods. (59)

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਵਸਫ਼ਸ਼ ਜ਼ਬਾਨਿ ਦੋ ਆਲਮ ਕਸੀਰ
column5: He cannot be admired enough by the tongues of both the worlds,

column2: ਬਵਦ ਤੰਗ ਪੇਸ਼ਸ਼ ਫ਼ਜ਼ਾਇ ਜ਼ਮੀਰ॥੬੦॥
column5: And, for him, the vast courtyard of the soul is not large enough. (60)

column2: ਹਮਾਂ ਬਿਹ ਕਿ ਖ਼ਾਹੇਮ ਅਜ਼ ਫ਼ਜਲਿ ਊ
column5: Therefore, it would be prudent for us that we should, from his eclat and beneficence

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਅਲਤਾਫ਼ੋ ਅਕਰਾਮ ਹੱਕ ਅਦਲਿ ਊ॥੬੧॥
column5: And His kindness and generosity, obtain His command. (61)

column2: ਸਰਿ ਮਾ ਬਪਾਇਸ਼ ਬਵਦ ਬਰ ਦਵਾਮ
column5: Our heads should, therefore, always bow at His lotus feet,

column2: ਨਿਸ਼ਾਰਸ਼ ਦਿਲੋ ਜਾਨਿ ਮਨ ਮੁਸਤਦਾਮ॥੬੨॥
column5: And, our heart and soul should always be willing to sacrifice themselves for Him. (62)

column2: ਸਲਤ਼ਨਤੇ ਸੋਇਮ
column5: Third Guru Guru Amar Das Ji

column2: ਸਲਤ਼ਨਤੇ ਸੋਇਮਸ਼, ਸੁਲਤ਼ਾਨੇ ਮੁਮਾਲਿਕੇ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ੇ-ਪਰਵਰੀ
column5: The Third Guru, Guru Amar Das Ji, was the noursiher-upholder of truth, emperor of regions

column2: ਵ ਮੁਹ਼ੀਤ਼ੇ ਅ਼ਾਲਮੇ, ਕਰਾਮਤ ਗੁਸ-ਤਰੀ॥
column5: and is an expansive ocean of bestowals and largess.

column2: ਮਲਕੁਲ-ਮੌਤੇ, ਗ਼ਾਲਿਬ ਮਗ਼ਲੂਬਸ਼
column5: The strong and powerful angel of death was subservient to him,

column2: ਵ ਸੁਲਤ਼ਾਨੇ ਮਲਕੂਤ, ਮੁਹ਼ਾਸਿਬ ਮਹ਼ਸੂਬਸ਼॥
column5: and the chief of gods of maintaining the accounts of each and every person was under his supervision.

column2: ਪੈਰਾਯਹ ਫ਼ਰੋਗ਼ੇ ਆਂ, ਹਰ-ਦੋ ਮਸ਼ਅ਼ਲੇ ਹ਼ੱਕ਼
column5: The glow of the garb of the flame of truth,

column2: ਹ਼ੱਕ਼ ਵ ਇਬ-ਤਿ-ਸਾਮੇ, ਹਰ ਗ਼ੁੰਚ-ਹਏ ਬਸਤਹ ਵਰਕ਼॥
column5: and the blossoming of the closed buds is their joy and happiness.

column2: ਨੁਖ਼ਸਤੀਂ ਅਲਿਫ਼ੇ, ਨਾਮੋ ਕ਼ੁਦਸੀ-ਅਸ਼; ਆਰਾਮ ਬਖ਼ਸ਼ੇ, ਹਰ ਆਵਾਰਹ॥
column5: The first letter of his holy name, 'Alif', bestows elation and serenity to every strayed person.

column2: ਵ ਮੀਮੇ ਫ਼ੱਰੁਖ਼ ਤਨ-ਜ਼ੀਮਸ਼; ਮੁਸਤ ਮਅ਼ਾਨੇ, ਹਰ ਬੇਚਾਰਹ॥
column5: The sacred 'Meem, blesses the ear of every grieved and afflicted person with the savor of poetry.

column2: ਰਾਏ ਮੈਮਨਤ, ਆਰਾਇਸ਼ ਰਿਆਜ਼ੇ-ਜਾਵੀਦ
column5: The fortunate 'Ray' of his name is the glory and grace of his divine face

column2: ਵ ਦਾਲੇ, ਦਅ਼ਾਦਤ ਇਸ਼ਤਮਾਲਸ਼; ਦਸਤਗੀਰੇ ਹਰ ਨਾ ਉਮੀਦ॥
column5: and the well-intentioned 'Daal' is the support of every helpless.

column2: ਅਲਿਫ਼ੇ ਸਾਨੀਅਸ਼; ਅਮਾਨੇ ਹਰ, ਮੁਜਰਿਮ ਵ ਸਿਆਹਕਾਰ
column5: The second 'Alif' of his name provides protection and refuge to every sinner

column2: ਵ ਸੀਨੇ ਆਖ਼ਿਰੀਨਸ਼; ਸਾਯਹਏ ਕਿਰਦਗਾਰ॥੬੩॥
column5: and the last 'Seen' is the image of the Almighty Waaheguru. (63)

column2: ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ ਜੀਉ ਸਤ
column5: Waaheguru is the Truth,

column2: ਗੁਰੂ ਅਮਰਦਾਸ ਆਂ ਗਰਾਮੀ ਨਜ਼ਾਦ
column5: Gueu Amar Das was from a great family lineage,

column2: ਜ਼ਿ ਅਫ਼ਜ਼ਾਲਿ ਹੱਕ ਹਸਤੀਅਸ਼ ਰਾ ਮੁਆਦ॥੬੪॥

column5: Joth Bigaas

ਗੁਰੂ ਨਾਨਕ ਆਮਦ ਨਰਾਇਨ ਸਰੂਪ
